- bay leaves
- dry sherry wine

## Directions

1. After cleaning well, split the head in half and sprinkle with salt
2. Chop a little parsley, very finely minced garlic, and cut ham into long thin strips
3. Mix with pepper powder, a little salt, and a tablespoon of melted butter
4. Stuff the head with this mixture, rubbing the outside with butter and salt
5. Place in a vessel and cover with water
6. Add a bundle of parsley, some leeks, some orange leaves, two bay leaves, salt, and half a bottle of dry sherry
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cabeza de Ternera a la Mexicana (Calf's Head Mexican Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head
- salt
- pepper powder
- oregano powder
- vinegar
- garlic head, peeled
- dried chiles (four to six), cooked
- potato salad
- lettuce

## Directions

1. After peeling and cleaning well, split the head in half to remove the brains
2. Rub with salt, pepper powder, oregano powder, and vinegar
3. Grind a whole head of garlic with four or six cooked dried chiles
4. Dissolve this with a little more vinegar and rub inside and outside the head
5. Leave overnight in this brine
6. Early the next morning put it in the oven, carefully basting it with its juices every moment
7. After three hours in the oven, remove and serve with a salad of potatoes and lettuce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cabeza de Ternera al Horno (Calf's Head Roasted)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head
- salt
- garlic
- oregano
- chile pasa (dried chile)
- Castilian peppers
- cumin
- roasted tomatoes
- vinegar

## Directions

1. After peeling the head, split it in half and remove the brains
2. After washing well, rub with salt
3. Grind garlic, oregano, dried chile, some Castilian peppers, a little cumin, and some roasted tomatoes
4. Dissolve this with vinegar and a little salt
5. Rub this mixture inside and outside the head
6. Place in a cazuela (earthenware dish) and put in the oven for three hours, continuously turning it and basting with its juices
7. Can be served as a table course with vinaigrette sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cabeza en Adobo (Sheep's Head in Adobo Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep head
- tomato
- miltomate
- garlic
- oregano
- cumin
- chile
- achiote
- French roll

## Directions

1. Cook the head
2. Grind cooked tomato and miltomate with garlic, oregano, cumin, chile, a small piece of achiote, and a soaked French roll
3. Thin the mixture with the cooking broth
4. Fry the sauce
5. Add the head cut into pieces with the tongue cut into pieces
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cachotios de Carne (Stuffed Meat Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- lard, melted
- salt
- ham
- parsley
- garlic
- almond, fried in oil
- vinegar or lemon juice, a little
- pepper powder
- clove
- cinnamon
- raw egg
- water
- potato salad or tomato sauce for serving

## Directions

1. Take a piece of meat and slice it into thin rounds
2. Mash the slices very well and coat with melted lard and salt
3. Chop ham, parsley, garlic, and fried almond
4. Add a little vinegar or lemon juice, pepper powder, clove, and cinnamon
5. Mix this filling with a raw egg
6. Fill the meat slices with this mixture, roll them, and sew them closed
7. Fry in lard or oil until they take color
8. Cover with water and cook until done
9. Serve with potato salad or tomato sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cake de Gracia (Grace Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half cups of brown sugar
- two large tablespoons of butter
- two cups of sifted flour
- four cooked potatoes, well mashed
- three tablespoons of cognac
- pieces of prune and walnuts
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Mix everything well
2. Pour into a well-greased dish
3. Put in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cakes Americanos (American Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound flour
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 4 tablespoons lard
- 1 teaspoon Royal baking powder
- 2 eggs
- chopped almond pieces

## Directions

1. Put 0.5 pound of flour, 4 tablespoons of sugar, 4 tablespoons of lard, 1 teaspoon of Royal and 2 eggs
2. Mix everything well and by spoonfuls place on a baking sheet with pieces of chopped almonds on top
3. Bake in a hot oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Camarones a la Chilena (Shrimp Chilean Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp, well peeled
- Salt
- Flour
- Pepper powder
- Oil
- Garlic
- Peeled tomato
- Chopped parsley
- Vinegar
- Chopped pickles
- One or two tablespoons of mustard

## Directions

1. After peeling the shrimp well, cook them with salt.
2. Let them boil for a quarter of an hour, then remove.
3. Coat them in flour and pepper powder.
4. Fry them in oil, and when they turn golden, remove them.
5. In the same oil, fry garlic, peeled tomato, and chopped parsley.
6. Add a little vinegar, chopped pickles, and one or two tablespoons of mustard.
7. Place the shrimp on a platter and cover with this sauce.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Camarones con Papas (Shrimp with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp
- Potatoes, peeled and cut
- Shrimp shells
- Castilian peppercorns
- Two roasted tomatoes
- One dried chile
- A small piece of annatto
- A little finely ground masa
- Water
- One tablespoon of hot lard

## Directions

1. Soak the shrimp with hot water and peel them.
2. Cook them with peeled potatoes that have been cut.
3. Grind the shrimp shells with some Castilian peppercorns, two roasted tomatoes, one dried chile, a small piece of annatto, and a little finely ground masa.
4. Mix this with a little water.
5. Fry in one tablespoon of hot lard.
6. Add the shrimp and potatoes to this mixture, ensuring it is neither too thin nor too thick.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Camarones Frescos (Fresh Shrimp)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh shrimp, cleaned
- two glasses white wine
- salt
- parsley, minced
- onion, minced
- garlic, minced
- two cloves
- half glass oil
- two bay leaves
- pepper powder
- two or three slices peeled and seeded lemon

## Directions

1. Place cleaned shrimp in a dish with white wine, salt, minced parsley, onion and garlic
2. Add cloves, oil, bay leaves, pepper powder and lemon slices
3. Let boil and when reduced, remove the bay leaves and lemon
4. Serve juicy
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Camarones Guisados (Stewed Shrimp)
body:
## Ingredients

- Shrimp, well peeled and dried
- Lard
- Three roasted tomatoes
- One small glass of white wine
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Fried bread slices, thin

## Directions

1. Soak the well-peeled and dried shrimp with hot water.
2. Fry them in lard.
3. Grind three roasted tomatoes and fry them with the shrimp.
4. Add one small glass of white wine.
5. When well thickened, season with salt and a pinch of pepper.
6. Serve over thin slices of fried bread.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Camote (Sweet Potato Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potato (preferably yellow variety)
- one and a half pounds of sugar
- a small teaspoon of lime lye
- water

## Directions

1. Peel the sweet potato, preferably if it is of the yellow variety
2. Place it in water with a small teaspoon of lime lye for a quarter of an hour
3. Have one and a half pounds of sugar clarified
4. Place the sweet potato in the clarified sugar to cook through
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Camote (Sweet Potato Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- camote
- water with lime lye
- one and a half pounds of sugar clarified

## Directions

1. Peel the camote, better if yellow colored
2. Soak for a quarter hour in water with one teaspoon of lime lye
3. Have already clarified one and a half pounds of sugar
4. Put the camote in the syrup to heat through
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Canelones (Cannelloni)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound bread flour
- five eggs
- two ounces butter
- water
- salt
- onion stalks
- white sauce
- chicken
- ham
- brains
- pâté
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Place one pound of flour on a kneading board and add five eggs and two ounces of butter.
2. Knead very well, let rest for some hours, or better if moistened the day before.
3. Roll out the dough and cut cannelloni four inches long by two inches wide.
4. Prepare a pan with boiling water, salt and onion stalks.
5. Boil the cannelloni for ten minutes, then remove them.
6. Prepare a thick white sauce and mix with minced chicken, ham, brains and pâté.
7. Fill the cannelloni and roll them into cannelloni shape.
8. Place them in a buttered pan, cover with the remaining sauce and sprinkle Parmesan cheese on top.
9. If desired, place in the oven for a while.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cangrejos en Adobo (Crabs in Adobe Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- crabs
- 1 garlic clove
- Castilian peppers
- oregano
- cumin seeds
- 2 roasted tomatoes
- 4 raw tomatoes
- guaque chile, cooked or dried chile with a small piece of achiote
- French bread, soaked
- lard
- crab cooking broth

## Directions

1. Clean the crabs and cook them as the previous recipe
2. Grind the small claws raw with one garlic clove, some Castilian peppers, a little oregano, some cumin seeds, two roasted tomatoes, four raw tomatoes, and one cooked guaque chile; if not available, grind dried chile with a small piece of achiote, and a soaked French bread
3. Dissolve all this with a little of the broth in which the crabs were cooked
4. Fry in a little hot lard and in this put the crabs to give a few boils and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cangrejos en Recado (Crabs in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- crabs
- salt
- 4 or 5 Castilian peppers
- 2 roasted tomatoes
- a small piece of achiote
- dried chile (optional)
- French bread, soaked and ground or a small piece of very finely ground corn dough
- a sprig of epazote or cumin seeds

## Directions

1. Clean the crabs very well and remove all the small claws, leaving them to cook with salt, and keep the large claws on the crab
2. Grind the small claws with four or five Castilian peppers, two roasted tomatoes, a small piece of achiote and if desired a dried chile
3. Thicken with soaked and ground French bread or with a small piece of very finely ground corn dough
4. Dissolve the sauce in the broth in which the claws were cooked, add a sprig of epazote and if epazote is not ground, grind some cumin seeds instead
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cangrejos Guisados (Stewed Crabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Crabs
- Salt
- Four raw tomatoes
- One garlic clove
- Oregano
- Cumin
- Castilian peppers
- Achiote
- Cooked guaque chile
- French bread soaked
- Lard

## Directions

1. After cleaning the crabs well, cook them with only the claws, with salt
2. Grind all the small legs with four raw tomatoes, one garlic clove, a little oregano, a little cumin, some Castilian peppers, a piece of achiote, a cooked guaque chile and a piece of soaked French bread
3. Mix with the same broth, fry in one teaspoon of lard and place the crabs in this to serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cangrejos Rellenos (Stuffed Crabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Crabs
- Salt
- Butter
- Peeled and chopped tomato
- Onion
- Peas
- Potatoes
- String beans
- Carrot
- Pepper powder
- White bread powder
- Vinegar
- Egg
- Lard

## Directions

1. After cooking the crabs well with salt, remove all the small legs, lift the shell and remove everything inside and everything in the claws
2. Fry all this in one tablespoon of butter with peeled and chopped tomato and onion, mix peas, potatoes, string beans, carrot, cooked and chopped
3. Add pepper powder, one teaspoon of white bread powder, one teaspoon of vinegar and salt
4. Fill the crabs with this, place the little cover on, wrap in beaten egg, fry in hot lard and serve with fried recado
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Capon en Chicha (Capon in Chicha)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 capon, cleaned
- 1 onion
- 1 tomato
- 1 sprig of thyme
- Castilian pepper
- A few cloves
- Salt
- Chicha (not too strong)

## Directions

1. After the capon is well cleaned, place it in a dish with onion, tomato, thyme, Castilian pepper, a few cloves and salt
2. If desired, keep everything whole, or alternatively grind everything to powder
3. Cover with a little chicha that is not too strong
4. Leave until it falls apart
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Capón en Chicha (Capon in Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- onion
- tomato
- thyme
- Castilian pepper
- cloves

## Directions

1. Clean the capon well
2. Place in a dish with onion, tomato, thyme, Castilian pepper, and some cloves
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Capón Relleno (Stuffed Capon)
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- livers
- bread
- cream
- parsley
- onions
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- lard
- three egg yolks
- butter
- bread powder

## Directions

1. Clean the capon well and debone it
2. Chop the livers and with all the meat pieces that remain, put a piece of bread in a dish with half a cup of cream with finely chopped parsley, onions and mushrooms, salt and pepper
3. Pass all this through lard
4. Dissolve three raw egg yolks and use this to stuff the capon
5. Spread well with butter and well covered with bread powder, place in the oven to cook
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Caramelos de Café con Leche (Milk Toffee Candies)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of milk
- one pound of dry sugar
- one small glass of white honey

## Directions

1. In half a liter of milk put one pound of dry sugar
2. When it is already reaching the thread stage, add one small glass of white honey
3. Let it boil until it reaches the caramel stage
4. Grease a wooden board with a little lard, pour the caramel into it and when cold cut it into the size and shape desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carlota
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 1 pound sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon potato starch or cornstarch
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 packet gelatin dissolved in cold water or 6 sheets isinglass dissolved in hot water

## Directions

1. Put a saucepan on the fire with one liter of milk and one pound of sugar and let it boil until it thickens
2. Remove from heat and when it is fried, add eight beaten egg yolks and one teaspoon of potato starch or cornstarch dissolved in one cup of milk and one teaspoon of vanilla
3. Put back on the fire with a small packet of gelatin dissolved in cold water or six sheets of isinglass dissolved in hot water
4. Strain and put in a mold to set
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carlota (Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of milk
- one pound of sugar
- eight egg yolks
- one teaspoon of potato starch or cornstarch
- one cup of milk
- one teaspoon of vanilla
- one packet of gelatin dissolved in cold water or six sheets of isinglass dissolved in hot water

## Directions

1. Put a saucepan with one liter of milk and one pound of sugar on the fire and let it boil until it thickens
2. Lower heat and when it is fried, add eight egg yolks and one teaspoon of potato starch or cornstarch dissolved in one cup of milk and one teaspoon of vanilla
3. Put back on the fire with a small packet of gelatin dissolved in cold water or six sheets of isinglass dissolved in hot water
4. Strain and put in a mold to set
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Catalana (Catalan-Style Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- oregano
- pepper, ground
- garlic
- salt
- pepper powder
- vinegar
- tomato, chopped
- onion, chopped
- parsley
- bay leaves, two
- lard, one tablespoon
- red or white wine, one glass

## Directions

1. Wash the meat well
2. Coat with oregano, ground pepper, garlic, salt, pepper powder, and vinegar
3. Let infuse for two hours
4. In a pot, place chopped tomato and onion
5. Place the meat on top
6. Add more chopped tomato with parsley, two bay leaves, one tablespoon lard, and one glass red or white wine
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Cordobesa (Cordoba-Style Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- oil, a little
- garlic, crushed
- meat
- onions, in slices
- tomatoes, chopped
- pepper, ground
- cinnamon, a small strip
- saffron, five threads
- vinegar, a little
- bay leaves, two
- potatoes for serving (optional)

## Directions

1. Fry a little oil and fry crushed garlic until cooked; remove it
2. In the same pan, fry a piece of meat until it takes on a yellow color
3. Add sliced onions, chopped tomatoes, ground pepper, a small strip of cinnamon, and five threads of saffron
4. Dissolve all this in a little vinegar and add two bay leaves
5. Once fried, serve with potatoes if desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Española (Spanish-Style Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef or pork
- salt
- pepper
- onion, chopped
- parsley, chopped
- celery, chopped
- three hard-boiled eggs
- butter
- broth

## Directions

1. Pound a piece of beef or pork meat
2. Rub with salt and pepper
3. Chop onion, parsley, celery and three hard-boiled eggs
4. Season with salt, pepper powder and a little butter
5. Place this mixture on the meat, roll it well and tie it
6. Brown in butter
7. Add broth and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Francesa (French-Style Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin, brisket, or chuck meat
- Two turnips
- Two carrots
- Chopped parsley
- Pepper powder
- Clove
- Cinnamon
- One glass of Sherry wine
- Water
- Butter or lard for frying

## Directions

1. Fry the meat in butter
2. Add two turnips, two carrots, chopped parsley, pepper powder, clove, and cinnamon
3. Pour in one glass of Sherry wine and enough water to cover the meat
4. Let it reduce
5. Serve with plain potatoes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Francesa (French-Style Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter, oil, or lard
- a piece of tender meat
- onion sliced very thin
- two bay leaves
- a sprig of thyme
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Put a saucepan on the fire with one tablespoon of butter, oil, or lard
2. In this fry a piece of tender meat and add a quantity of very thinly sliced onion
3. Add two bay leaves and a sprig of thyme with salt to taste
4. When well fried, cover with water
5. Seal the saucepan hermetically and place on very low heat
6. Shake the saucepan without opening it
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Francesa (Meat French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- lomo, posta negra o badilla (loin, black brisket or shoulder cut)
- manteca (lard)
- nabos (turnips)
- zanahorias (carrots)
- perejil (parsley)
- polvo de pimienta (ground pepper)
- clavo (clove)
- canela (cinnamon)
- vino de Jerez (Jerez wine)
- agua (water)

## Directions

1. Fry loin, black brisket or shoulder cut in lard
2. Add two turnips, two carrots, chopped parsley, ground pepper, clove, cinnamon, a glass of Jerez wine and water
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Granadina (Granada-Style Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat, in pieces
- lard
- tomatoes, raw, chopped
- tomatoes, roasted, chopped
- pepper
- clove
- cinnamon
- garlic, crushed
- pimentón (if available)
- water, sufficient

## Directions

1. Fry meat pieces in lard
2. Add chopped raw tomatoes and roasted tomatoes, pepper, clove, cinnamon, and crushed garlic (add pimentón if available)
3. Add sufficient water to cook
4. Simmer until cooked
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Jardinera (Meat with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat
- Ham
- Butter or margarine
- Water
- Salt
- Oregano
- Thyme
- Laurel
- Carrots
- Turnips
- Peas
- Green beans
- Butter
- Flour
- Milk
- Pepper powder
- Nutmeg powder

## Directions

1. Lard with ham and put to fry in butter or margarine
2. When it has taken on some color, add water, salt, oregano, thyme, and laurel, all ground
3. While the meat cooks, put carrots, turnips, peas, and tender green beans to cook
4. Once cooked, let them drain
5. Fry a little butter and in it two tablespoons of flour with a little milk so the sauce is quite thin
6. Add pepper powder and nutmeg powder
7. When it boils, mix in the vegetables
8. Cut the meat into very thin slices and place on the platter, pouring this sauce over it
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Marinera (Seafarer-Style Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Oregano
- Butter
- Large onions
- Red wine
- Bay leaf
- Thyme

## Directions

1. Cook the meat with salt, pepper, parsley, and oregano
2. When cooked, cut into slices and arrange in a well-spread skillet
3. Prepare a sauce by browning large onions in butter
4. When the onions have taken on color, add a little red wine, salt, pepper, a bay leaf, and a sprig of thyme
5. Bathe the meat with this sauce and place over very low heat
6. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne a la Reina (Meat Queen Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion, chopped
- tomato, chopped
- parsley, chopped
- meat, pierced with fork and coated with lard or butter
- bay leaves, two
- pepper
- clove
- cinnamon
- white wine or vinegar, one cup

## Directions

1. Chop onion, tomato, and parsley
2. In a pot, place a layer of this mixture
3. On top place the meat pierced with a fork and coated with lard or butter
4. Add two bay leaves, pepper, clove, cinnamon, and one cup white wine or vinegar
5. Cook over low heat
6. Remove all spices before serving
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Asada (Roasted Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- carne suave o lomo chiquito (tender meat or small loin)
- limón (lemon)
- sal (salt)
- mantequilla (butter)
- papas (potatoes)
- tomate asado (roasted tomato)
- chile tostado (toasted chile)
- manteca (lard)
- cebolla (onion)
- hierbabuena (mint)

## Directions

1. Cut meat into slices
2. Squeeze lemon drops on it, add salt, coat with butter and let sit
3. After ten minutes, place on the grill
4. For tenderness, turn three times; otherwise let it roast longer
5. Serve with boiled or fried potatoes, sauce or chirmolito made as follows: roast tomato and toast chile in lard or on hot coals, wash the chile and mash it well after removing seeds with a little salt, when well mashed add the roasted peeled tomato with a little hot water, ensuring it is not too thin, chop onion and mint and serve
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne con Alcaparras o Aceitunas (Meat with Capers or Olives)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin, brisket, or chuck
- Finely minced onion
- Garlic
- Tomato
- Parsley
- Butter or margarine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Four ounces of capers or olives
- A piece of fried bread
- Broth
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Slice the loin, brisket, or chuck as for steak
2. Make a minced mixture of very finely chopped onion, garlic, tomato, and plenty of parsley; the tomato should be sufficient and peeled
3. Coat a pan with butter or margarine
4. Layer in the pan: a layer of the minced mixture, then a layer of meat with salt and pepper, repeating until full
5. Cover and let sit overnight
6. Place over low heat, stirring very carefully
7. Grind four ounces of capers or olives and a piece of fried bread and strain through broth over the meat
8. Leave over low heat and serve very hot with potatoes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne con Almendra (Meat with Almond)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces peeled almonds
- 1 sprig thyme
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 stick cinnamon
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Loin or a piece of very tender brisket

## Directions

1. Clean the loin or brisket well
2. Grind together the peeled almonds, thyme sprig, bay leaf, and cinnamon stick
3. Dissolve the ground mixture in vinegar and salt
4. Rub the loin very well with this mixture and let it sit overnight
5. The following day, place it in the oven
6. Serve with salad of your choice
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne con Champiñones (Meat with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of meat
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- raw tomato
- bay leaf
- a sprig of thyme
- onion stems
- a small jar of mushrooms with its juice
- 1 cup red wine
- 2 tablespoons flour
- a piece of burnt sugar

## Directions

1. The day before, place a piece of meat in brine with salt, pepper, vinegar, raw tomato, bay leaf, a sprig of thyme, and onion stems
2. The next day, place over heat; when half cooked, add a small jar of mushrooms with its juice, 1 cup of red wine mixed with 2 tablespoons of flour and a piece of burnt sugar
3. Cook in an oven if available, or over two fires if not
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne con Mantequilla (Meat with Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- lomo (loin)
- mantequilla (butter)
- cebolla (onion)
- polvo de orégano (ground oregano)
- jugo de naranja dulce (sweet orange juice)
- tomillo (thyme)
- hoja de laurel (bay leaf)
- agua (water)
- aceite de oliva (olive oil)
- pan frito (fried bread)
- manteca (lard)
- aceitunas (olives)
- alcaparras (capers)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- lechuga (lettuce)

## Directions

1. Make some small cuts in the loin with a pointed knife and fry in butter
2. When colored, add sliced onion, ground oregano, a little sweet orange juice, thyme, two bay leaves, water to cover the meat and two tablespoons of olive oil
3. Grind a small piece of fried bread in lard with six olives and six capers, dissolve this in a cup of vinegar and add to the meat, letting it reduce
4. Serve with lettuce or as desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne con Revolcado Seco (Dry Meat with Coating)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat
- Salt
- Miltomate, cooked
- Cumin
- Garlic
- Oregano
- Achiote
- Chile (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the meat, cut into slices, pound them, and fry with salt
2. Have ready ground cooked miltomate with a bit of cumin, a small clove of garlic, a bit of oregano, and a small piece of achiote
3. Add ground chile if a spicy version is desired
4. Add this mixture to the meat and serve after turning it several times until it is dry
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne con Tomate Cocido (Meat with Cooked Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat for slicing
- butter
- peeled tomatoes
- parsley
- garlic cloves, roasted
- pepper powder

## Directions

1. Heat butter
2. Add chopped peeled tomatoes to the meat with a little parsley and a few roasted garlic cloves
3. When it has boiled, remove the parsley and garlic
4. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne con Tomate Crudo (Meat with Raw Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat sliced like for steak
- butter or oil
- peeled tomato, cut into pieces
- garlic, thinly minced
- parsley
- pepper powder
- a little water
- salt

## Directions

1. Heat butter or oil
2. Add the meat with the peeled tomato in pieces, thinly minced garlic, parsley, pepper powder, a little water and salt
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne con Tomate Crudo (Meat with Raw Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- lard or oil
- tomato, peeled and in pieces
- garlic, sliced thin
- parsley
- pepper powder
- water, a little
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut meat slices as for steak
2. Heat lard or oil and add the meat with peeled tomato cut in pieces, thinly sliced garlic, parsley, pepper powder, a little water, and salt
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne de Carnero Guisada (Stewed Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- water
- salt
- lard
- flour
- broth or water
- pepper
- clove
- parsley
- garlic
- small onions
- egg yolks
- lemon

## Directions

1. Wash the meat well and cut into pieces
2. Place in a pot with a little water and salt; when it comes to a boil, remove and wash in cold water and drain well
3. Heat a saucepan with lard on the fire
4. Add the meat with salt; when it takes on color, add one or two tablespoons of flour according to the amount of meat and stir so it does not burn
5. Add a little broth or water and season with pepper, clove, parsley, garlic and small crushed onions
6. When cooked, remove all the herbs
7. Dissolve three egg yolks in the remaining sauce
8. When serving, add a few drops of lemon
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne de Ternera Guisada (Stewed Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- carne de ternera (veal)
- tomate pelado (peeled tomato)
- perejil (parsley)
- chile verde (green chile)
- polvo de pimienta (ground pepper)
- manteca o mantequilla (lard or butter)
- agua (water)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- sal (salt)

## Directions

1. Cook the meat and slice it
2. Prepare a mixture of peeled tomato, parsley, green chile and ground pepper
3. Coat a pan with lard or butter if available
4. Place a layer of meat, then a layer of mixture, continuing alternately, ending with meat
5. Add half a cup of water and half a cup of vinegar with salt
6. Place in the oven or over two fires
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Emperejilada (Parsley-Seasoned Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- lard
- parsley, ground
- Castilian pepper, two small
- chile, a small piece
- water
- vinegar, a little
- potatoes, peeled

## Directions

1. Pierce a piece of meat with a fork and place in hot lard
2. Add a little ground parsley with two small Castilian peppers and a small piece of chile
3. Cover the meat with water and a little vinegar
4. When half-cooked, add peeled potatoes
5. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne en Adobo Jugoso (Juicy Marinated Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomato
- miltomate (ground cherry)
- onion head, roasted
- cumin, a little
- oregano
- garlic clove
- achiote (annatto), a small piece
- chile, well toasted (if desired for spice)
- meat, already fried

## Directions

1. Grind tomato and miltomate, toasted or cooked with a roasted onion head, a little cumin, oregano, a small garlic clove, and a small piece of achiote
2. If spice is desired, grind a well-toasted chile
3. Place this mixture on the meat that is already fried
4. Let it simmer for a few boils so that it does not dry out
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne en Adobo Jugoso (Juicy Meat in Adobe Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomato
- Miltomate, roasted or cooked
- Onion head, roasted
- Cumin
- Oregano
- Garlic clove
- Achiote
- Chile, well toasted (optional for spicy version)

## Directions

1. Grind tomato and miltomate (roasted or cooked) with a roasted onion head, a bit of cumin, oregano, a small garlic clove, and a small piece of achiote
2. Add ground well-toasted chile if a spicy version is desired
3. Add this mixture to meat that is already fried
4. Let it come to a boil a few times so it does not dry out
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne en Pepián de Culantro (Meat in Cilantro Pepper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef or pork
- Salt
- Cabbage
- Güisquil
- Green beans
- Pacayas
- Miltomate
- Rice, toasted
- Chile zambo or other chile
- Onion head, roasted
- Garlic clove
- Chiapa pepper
- Castilian pepper
- Plantain skin, burned (for color, optional)
- Cilantro
- Water

## Directions

1. If using beef, cook rib and flank cut into pieces with salt
2. When the meat is halfway cooked, add cabbage, güisquil, green beans, and pacayas, all cut into small pieces
3. Grind miltomate and toasted rice, chile zambo or other chile, a roasted onion head, a clove of garlic, and a bit of Chiapa and Castilian pepper
4. If desired, give it color with a small piece of burned plantain skin
5. Dissolve this in cold water and grind in a bit of fresh cilantro
6. Let it boil for half an hour so the rice cooks well and ensure it is not too thick
7. Can be served with white rice soup
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne en Pepián de Culantro con Pepitoria (Meat in Cilantro Pepian with Seeds)
body:
## Ingredients

- pepitoria (pumpkin seeds)
- sesame
- guaque chile
- miltomate
- two or three french peppers
- Castilian pepper
- cinnamon, a small piece
- cilantro, a little green
- meat broth (beef or pork)
- lard

## Directions

1. Toast pepitoria, sesame, a guaque chile, miltomate, and two or three french peppers
2. Once well toasted, grind with Castilian pepper, a small piece of cinnamon, and a little green cilantro
3. Dissolve this mixture with the meat broth
4. Fry in lard and add the meat in pieces
5. Let it simmer for a while and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne en Tomate (Meat in Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat sliced
- oil
- two tomatoes, chopped
- one onion
- minced garlic
- white wine, sherry or red wine
- pepper powder
- cinnamon
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Slice the meat and coat with oil and place in a dish
2. Add two chopped tomatoes, one onion, minced garlic, white wine, sherry or red wine, pepper powder, cinnamon, salt and water to cover the meat
3. When it has boiled, remove everything except the tomato
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne en Tomate (Meat in Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- oil
- tomatoes, chopped, two
- onion
- garlic, chopped
- white wine, sherry, or red wine

## Directions

1. Cut meat into slices and coat with oil
2. Place in a dish
3. Add two chopped tomatoes, one onion, chopped garlic, and white wine, sherry, or red wine
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne en Vino (Meat in Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- carne (meat)
- manteca o mantequilla (lard or butter)
- ajo (garlic)
- caldo (broth)
- agua (water)
- sal (salt)
- vino jerez (sherry wine)
- hoja de laurel (bay leaf)
- pimientas de Castilla (Castilian peppers)
- clavos de especia (cloves)

## Directions

1. Fry a piece of meat in lard or butter with three or four mashed garlic cloves
2. When golden, add half broth and half water until the meat is covered, adding salt
3. When half this liquid has been consumed, add a glass of sherry wine, two bay leaves, two Castilian peppers and two cloves
4. Let it reduce and when serving, remove the garlic and spices
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Encebollada (Onion-Braised Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat, in pieces
- onions, peeled, three
- tomatoes, three
- bay leaves, two
- pepper
- cinnamon, a small piece
- vinegar, half cup
- oil, half cup

## Directions

1. Place meat pieces in a pot
2. Add three peeled onions, three tomatoes, two bay leaves, pepper, a small piece of cinnamon, half cup vinegar, and half cup oil
3. Let it cook and serve with whatever is desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Fría (Cold Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin
- Garlic
- Pepper
- A small piece of ginger
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Chorizo
- Ham
- Chile
- Pickles
- Oregano powder
- Thyme
- Clove
- Pepper
- Lemon slices
- Vegetables
- Olives
- Capers

## Directions

1. Tenderize the loin very well
2. Grind garlic, pepper, and a small piece of ginger
3. Dissolve this in vinegar with salt
4. Rub the loin with this mixture
5. Mince chorizo, ham, chile, and pickles
6. Fill the loin with this mixture
7. Roll it up well and sew with twine
8. Place in a pot with vinegar, garlic, crushed ginger, oregano powder, thyme, clove, pepper, and some lemon slices
9. Ensure the meat is well covered with vinegar
10. Cover well and after three days put to cook
11. When cold, serve with vegetables, olives, and capers
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Fría a la Pamela (Cold Meat Pamela Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of meat
- salt
- pepper powder
- one egg per pound of meat
- pieces of cooked ham
- hard boiled egg
- cheese
- bay leaves
- thyme

## Directions

1. Take a piece of meat; if desired with the color of sausage, leave it one day with salt; if not, grind it the same day in a machine until very fine
2. Add salt and pepper powder
3. For one pound of meat add one beaten egg and mix very well
4. Spread it out and place long pieces of cooked ham, hard boiled egg and cheese cut lengthwise
5. Wrap it, giving it the shape desired, either round or long
6. Place in very fine cloth, tie it and cook in water for three hours with two bay leaves, thyme and salt
7. The next day slice it and serve with salad or sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Guisada (Stewed Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- lard
- tomatillo
- one or two tomatoes
- sprig of thyme
- water
- salt
- potatoes (optional)

## Directions

1. Fry the meat in a little lard
2. Grind a bit of tomatillo, one or two tomatoes, and a sprig of thyme
3. Add this ground mixture to the meat with sufficient water to cook it
4. Add salt to taste
5. If desired, add peeled whole potatoes
6. Garnish with lettuce and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Guisada (Stewed Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- lomo, posta negra o badilla (loin, black brisket or shoulder cut)
- agua (water)
- sal (salt)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- miltomate crudo (raw tomatillo)
- tomates (tomatoes)
- orégano (oregano)
- comino (cumin)
- pimientas de Castilla (Castilian peppers)
- ajo (garlic)
- chile de pasa (dried chile)
- achiote (annatto)
- papas (potatoes)
- lechuga verde (green lettuce)

## Directions

1. Place a piece of loin, black brisket or shoulder cut with water, salt and a little vinegar
2. Grind some raw tomatillo, two tomatoes, a little oregano, cumin, ten Castilian peppers, one garlic clove and one dried chile
3. If using dried chile, grind a small piece of annatto with it
4. Dissolve this in one liter of water and add to the meat
5. When most of the water has been consumed, add some potatoes
6. Cook on very gentle heat
7. Serve with green lettuce leaves
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Guisada (Stewed Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- good piece of meat
- onion heads, peeled and split
- tomatoes
- garlic
- Castilian pepper
- clove

## Directions

1. Fry a good piece of meat
2. Make several small cuts in it
3. Add peeled onion heads split in half, tomatoes, garlic, Castilian pepper, and clove
4. Remove the foam from the pot
5. Let it cook very well
6. Remove the spices before serving
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Guisada (Stewed Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- oil
- parsley
- hazelnuts, peeled, eight
- pepper
- tomato, peeled and chopped
- onion
- garlic, minced

## Directions

1. Fry a piece of meat in oil
2. When it takes color, add peeled and ground hazelnuts and pepper
3. Before adding this, add peeled and chopped tomato with onion and finely minced garlic
4. Cover with water and let it reduce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Mechada (Larded Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin
- Pieces of ham
- Garlic
- Pepper powder
- Pieces of red peppers
- Water
- Vinegar
- Pepper powder
- Clove
- One sprig of thyme
- Two bay leaves
- Lettuce
- Olives
- Capers

## Directions

1. Beat a loin very well
2. Lard it with small pieces of ham, garlic, pepper powder, and pieces of red peppers
3. Put to cook with water and vinegar
4. Add pepper powder, clove, a sprig of thyme, and two bay leaves
5. When well cooked, cover and leave in the air overnight
6. The next day clean it, cut into slices, and serve with lettuce, olives, and capers
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Mechada (Larded Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- jamón (ham)
- chorizo (sausage)
- tomate pelado (peeled tomato)
- ajo (garlic)
- cebolla (onion)
- perejil (parsley)
- polvo de pimienta (ground pepper)
- sal (salt)
- manteca o mantequilla (lard or butter)
- agua (water)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- vino (wine)
- hoja de laurel (bay leaf)
- ramita de tomillo (sprig of thyme)
- clavo (clove)
- canela (cinnamon)
- jengibre (ginger)
- tomates (tomatoes)

## Directions

1. Chop ham, sausage, peeled tomato, garlic, onion and parsley
2. Mix with ground pepper and salt
3. Use this mixture to lard the meat by making cuts with a pointed knife
4. Brown in lard or butter
5. When colored, add one liter of water, salt, one glass of vinegar and another of wine if available
6. Add a bay leaf, a sprig of thyme, pepper, clove, a small piece of cinnamon, a small piece of ginger, some diced tomatoes and two or three garlic cloves
7. Let it conserve; serve juicy if desired or save to serve with sauce or salad
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Migada (Shredded Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- oil
- onion
- garlic
- parsley
- vinegar, a little
- salt
- pepper powder
- clove
- cinnamon
- eggs, two
- bread powder

## Directions

1. Make meat slices and coat with oil
2. Place in a dish with onion, garlic, parsley, a little vinegar, salt, pepper powder, clove, and cinnamon
3. Let infuse for four hours
4. Beat two eggs (whites and yolks) and add salt
5. Coat the meat in the beaten egg
6. Wrap in bread powder and fry in hot lard
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Negra (Black Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- flour
- salt
- Royal baking powder
- lard
- goose liver or fried spinach or salmon

## Directions

1. Beat eggs according to the number of people, add one tablespoon of flour, salt to taste, and half a teaspoon of Royal baking powder.
2. Fry in hot lard and fill with goose liver or spinach fried in butter or salmon.
3. Serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne o Rabo de Res en Estofado (Beef or Oxtail Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pieces of meat or oxtail
- Water
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Lard
- Pepper powder
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Tomato
- Garlic (whole heads)
- Ham
- Chorizo
- Bread (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut pieces of meat or oxtail
2. In a pot with water, add salt, vinegar, lard, pepper powder, cloves, and cinnamon (if preferred, add whole spices and remove them before serving)
3. Chop tomato, garlic (whole heads), ham, and chorizo and add to the pot
4. Cook over low heat
5. If desired, let it reduce; if not, grind bread and serve juicy
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carne Quezalteca (Quetzaltec Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- carne (meat)
- jamón (ham)
- manteca o mantequilla (lard or butter)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- agua (water)
- tomate (tomato)
- cebolla (onion)
- polvo de pimienta (ground pepper)

## Directions

1. Open the meat lengthwise
2. Place a piece of ham inside
3. Brown in lard or butter
4. When golden, add half a cup of vinegar and the rest water until the meat is covered
5. Cook some tomato with two or three onion heads
6. Once cooked, mash with a spoon
7. Add a little ground pepper
8. Pour onto the meat to keep it well and serve with its own sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carnero a la Polaca (Mutton Polish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- salt
- garlic
- parsley
- bay leaves
- onion
- vinegar
- pepper powder
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Fry the meat cut into pieces with salt, garlic, parsley, two bay leaves, two onions divided, two cups of vinegar and pepper powder
2. When ready to serve, add one tablespoon of flour fried in butter
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carnero a lo Corso (Mutton Corsican Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- lard
- salt
- ham
- parsley
- garlic
- lemon juice
- white wine
- oil
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Cut pieces of meat and place in a pan with lard and salt
2. When half browned, add some chopped ham, some crushed parsley and crushed garlic
3. Add the juice of two lemons, one cup of white wine and two tablespoons of oil
4. Cover with broth or water
5. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Carquiñolis (Almond Biscotti)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of flour
- 1 pound of dry sugar
- half a glass of water
- half an ounce of ammonium bicarbonate dissolved in water
- pieces of almonds

## Directions

1. Place two pounds of flour on a very clean table
2. Make a well
3. Add one pound of dry sugar
4. Add half a glass of water
5. Add half an ounce of ammonium bicarbonate dissolved in the water
6. Mix everything very well
7. Pour into pans coated with butter, inserting small pieces of almonds all around
8. When cooked, remove and cut into long pieces
9. Place on tins and return to the oven until golden
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cebollas Chapinas (Chapines Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- large onions
- salt
- boiling water
- vinegar, one cup
- oil, half cup
- cooked guaque chiles, eight, ground
- pepper powder

## Directions

1. Clean large onions and cut the top
2. Pour boiling water with one tablespoon of salt
3. The next day, put on the fire with one cup of vinegar and half cup of oil, salt to taste, and eight cooked and well-ground guaque chiles
4. Add a pinch of pepper powder if desired
5. After half an hour, remove and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cebollitas Económicas (Economic Little Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- one glass vinegar
- half glass water
- salt
- a piece of rapadura (unrefined sugar)
- six large tomatoes
- two dried chiles or guaques

## Directions

1. Prepare the onions as for pickling, well cleaned.
2. Place them in a pot with one glass of vinegar and half glass of water, salt, and a piece of rapadura.
3. Grind for fifty onions: six large tomatoes and two dried chiles or guaques.
4. Serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cecina en Recado (Beef in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- onion stalks
- handful of tomatillos
- two cooked chiles
- handful of soaked rice
- Castilian peppercorns
- cumin grains
- small piece of achiote
- potato pieces
- güisquil

## Directions

1. Put the meat to cook in sufficient water with onion stalks cut into pieces
2. When the meat is cooked, add a sauce made as follows: grind a handful of tomatillos with two cooked chiles, a handful of soaked rice, some Castilian peppercorns, some cumin grains and a small piece of achiote
3. Let it boil until the rice is calculated to be cooked
4. When desired, add small pieces of potato and güisquil
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cecina en Recado (Dried Beef in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- water
- onion stalks
- handful of miltomate
- 2 cooked chiles
- handful of rice, soaked
- Castilian peppers
- cumin seeds
- small piece of achiote
- potato pieces (optional)
- güisquil pieces (optional)

## Directions

1. Put the meat to cook in sufficient water with onion stalks cut in pieces
2. When the meat is already cooked, add a sauce made as follows: grind a handful of miltomate with two cooked chiles, a handful of soaked rice, some Castilian peppers, some cumin seeds and a small piece of achiote
3. Let it boil until the rice is calculated to be cooked
4. When desired, add small pieces of potato and güisquil
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chalupas (Mexican Corn Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of cooked pork meat, chopped
- One tablespoon of lard
- Peeled tomato
- Onion
- One teaspoon of vinegar
- One teaspoon of sherry
- Raisins
- Peeled and chopped almonds
- Masa dough, well ground with salt
- One quarter ounce of pepitoria seeds
- One quarter ounce of sesame seeds
- One cup of roasted tomatillo
- Two dried pasilla chiles, seeded and fried in lard
- Six cloves of spice
- Six Castilian peppercorns
- A small piece of cinnamon
- One French bread, fried in lard
- Water
- Grated hard cheese
- Finely chopped lettuce
- Chopped onion
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop one pound of cooked pork meat and fry in one tablespoon of lard with peeled tomato and onion.
2. Add one teaspoon of vinegar, one teaspoon of sherry, raisins, and peeled chopped almonds.
3. Prepare masa dough ground well with salt and form tortillas to desired size.
4. Fill with the meat mixture.
5. Fold in half, ensuring edges are well sealed so they don't open.
6. Fry in hot lard until golden, then remove.
7. Grind one quarter ounce of pepitoria seeds, one quarter ounce of sesame seeds, one cup of roasted tomatillo, two fried dried pasilla chiles, six cloves of spice, six Castilian peppercorns, a small piece of cinnamon, and one French bread fried in lard.
8. Mix this with a little water.
9. Fry and coat the chalupas in this sauce.
10. Arrange on a platter and finish coating with the remaining sauce.
11. Sprinkle with grated hard cheese, finely chopped lettuce, and chopped onion dressed with lemon juice.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chanfaina Castellana (Castilian Chanfaina)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked liver
- cooked jowls
- oil
- onion
- garlic
- minced parsley
- pepper powder
- cloves
- one tablespoon flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the liver and cut the jowls into pieces after cooking them beforehand
2. Put everything in a pan with a little oil, onion, garlic, minced parsley, pepper powder and cloves
3. When well fried, add one tablespoon of flour well browned in butter
4. Let it cook and serve in a thin sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chaudfroid de Salmón (Cold Salmon in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 1/2 tablespoon lemon juice
- salt
- pepper powder
- a few drops of onion juice
- 2 tablespoons gelatin
- 1 tablespoon water
- cucumber strips
- ice water
- salmon

## Directions

1. Mix two tablespoons of butter, three of flour, one cup of heavy cream and one beaten egg yolk very well
2. Add one and one-half tablespoon of lemon juice, a little salt, pepper powder and a few drops of onion juice
3. Dissolve two tablespoons of gelatin in one tablespoon of water and add to the sauce when it is already cold
4. Let it set; use this to cover the salmon and decorate with cucumber strips, which have been placed in ice water beforehand
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chicharrones (Pork Rinds)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork skin and fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove all lard from the clean pork skin and set aside
2. Cut the lard into pieces of desired size and season with salt
3. Cut the skin lengthwise, pound it, and place in the sun for one full day
4. The next day, fry the lard and when it is half-fried, add pieces of skin of desired size
5. When the fat pieces are fried, remove them and let the skin fry a bit more without burning
6. The following day, put a pan with a little lard on the fire and add the skin pieces one by one, basting them with hot lard to make them puff up
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chilacayote (Squash in Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- chilacayote
- one and a half pounds of sugar clarified per pound of fruit
- white honey
- about twelve cloves

## Directions

1. Beat the chilacayote very well with a mallet
2. Peel it, remove all the fibrous parts, and chop in whatever form is desired
3. Blanch in very clean water
4. When blanched, remove and place in cold water
5. For one pound of fruit clarify one and a half pounds of sugar
6. Put the chilacayote in the sugar and when it begins to reach medium heat add one cup of white honey and about twelve cloves
7. Remove the cloves before serving
8. If desired to make it covered, do not add white honey or cloves; remove and when dry, coat like the figs
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chilaquiles de Queso (Cheese Chilaquiles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tortillas
- Fresh cheese
- Eggs, two for ten tortillas
- Salt
- Half a teaspoon of flour
- Two peeled tomatoes
- Two onions
- One or two green chiles

## Directions

1. Take the tortillas, add fresh cheese and fold them in two, wrap them in beaten egg; for ten tortillas, use two eggs.
2. Beat the egg whites first and when they are very stiff add the yolks with a few grains of salt and half a teaspoon of flour.
3. When well fried, put them in the oven to drain the grease.
4. Cook two peeled tomatoes, two onions cut in half, one or two green chiles; this is broken down and the enchiladas are bathed with it, serving them hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chilate Oriental (Eastern Chilate)
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty cacao beans
- twenty pataxtes
- one sapuyulo
- one quarter ounce of anise
- one quarter ounce of Chiapa pepper
- about ten chili strings
- a small piece of ginger
- half a pound of chan
- about ten cold tamales
- water
- four cinnamon chocolates
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Toast twenty cacao beans, twenty pataxtes, one sapuyulo, one quarter ounce of anise, one quarter ounce of Chiapa pepper, about ten chili strings, one small piece of ginger, half a pound of chan, all well toasted without burning
2. Grind about ten cold tamales
3. Dissolve everything with a little cold water
4. Add four beaten cinnamon chocolates and sugar to taste
5. Serve cold as a refreshment
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chiles Rellenos (Stuffed Chiles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Green guaque chiles
- Twelve ounces of partially cooked pork meat (for ten chiles)
- One tablespoon of lard
- Tomato
- Onion
- One garlic clove
- One teaspoon of vinegar
- Salt
- Bread powder ground with pepper, cinnamon, and clove
- Chopped almonds and raisins
- A touch of sweetness (optional)
- Zucchini, carrots, green beans, all chopped finely and cooked with salt (optional, instead of almonds and raisins)
- Capers (if using vegetables)
- Flour
- Two eggs
- Hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Place the green guaque chiles over coals that do not burn them
2. Put them in a pot and cover with boiling water
3. Cover and let them sit for ten minutes
4. Peel them easily
5. Open them in the middle and carefully remove the seeds with the tip of a knife
6. Leave them in water, changing it every two hours
7. If they are very spicy, you can add one tablespoon of sweetened vinegar
8. After one or two days in water (depending on preference), remove them and let them drain
9. Dry them with cloth
10. Chop partially cooked pork meat (twelve ounces for ten chiles)
11. Fry in one tablespoon of lard with tomato, onion, and one peeled and chopped garlic clove
12. Add one teaspoon of vinegar, salt, bread powder ground with pepper, cinnamon, and clove, chopped almonds and raisins, and if desired, a touch of sweetness
13. Alternatively, add finely chopped and cooked zucchini, carrots, and green beans with salt, in which case omit raisins and almonds but add capers
14. Fill the chiles with this mixture
15. Dust with flour
16. Dip in beaten egg
17. Fry in hot lard
18. Beat two eggs (first the whites, then the yolks) for ten chiles
19. Serve with a sauce of peeled and chopped tomato, onion, and one fried garlic clove, with one tablespoon of bread powder or one teaspoon of flour dissolved in water, with a touch of achiote color, and one cup of water with salt
20. Alternatively, serve with a sauce of cooked tomato ground with about ten or twelve capers, fried in a little lard, with water to make a thin sauce, seasoned with salt, and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chiles Rellenos con Pollo (Stuffed Chiles with Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Green guaque chiles
- Chicken
- Salt
- One tablespoon of lard or butter
- One peeled and chopped tomato
- One garlic clove
- One onion head, chopped
- One teaspoon of vinegar
- One teaspoon of white bread powder
- Pepper powder

## Directions

1. Prepare the chiles as in the previous recipe
2. Cook the chicken with salt
3. When cooked, chop all the meat into small pieces
4. Put one tablespoon of lard or butter in a pan
5. Add one peeled and chopped tomato, one garlic clove, and one chopped onion head
6. Add the chicken with one teaspoon of vinegar, one teaspoon of white bread powder, salt, and a little pepper powder
7. Fill the chiles with this mixture
8. Wrap them in beaten egg
9. Fry in hot lard and serve like the previous recipe
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chiles Rellenos con Verdura (Stuffed Chiles with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- Green guaque chiles
- Zucchini
- Carrot
- Tender green beans
- Potatoes
- Tender peas
- Salt
- One teaspoon of lard
- Peeled tomato
- Garlic
- Onion
- A little vinegar
- One tablespoon of bread powder ground with Castilian pepper
- A small piece of cinnamon
- Two cloves
- Beaten egg
- Hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Prepare the chiles as in the first chile recipe
2. Chop zucchini, carrot, tender green beans, potatoes, and tender peas
3. Cook everything with salt
4. Put a pan on the fire with one teaspoon of lard
5. Add peeled, chopped tomato, garlic, and chopped onion
6. When fried, add all the vegetables with a little vinegar, salt, one tablespoon of bread powder ground with Castilian pepper, a small piece of cinnamon, and about two cloves
7. Fill the chiles with this mixture
8. Wrap them in beaten egg
9. Fry in lard and serve like the others
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chimbos de Almendras (Almond Custards)
body:
## Ingredients

- three egg whites
- fifteen egg yolks
- half pound of ground almonds
- two egg whites
- two and a half pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Beat three egg whites and fifteen egg yolks well until they reach the consistency of rompope.
2. Add half pound of ground almonds mixed with two egg whites.
3. Grease a mold lightly with butter and place the mixture either in the oven or in a water bath.
4. The next day, remove the crust from the bottom, break into pieces.
5. Clarify two and a half pounds of sugar and place on the fire to harden.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chiqueadores (Egg Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- egg yolks
- 4 ounces of sugar
- 5 ounces of sifted flour
- butter for coating
- cinnamon powder (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat six egg whites as for nougat
2. Beat the yolks separately with four ounces of sugar
3. Combine both, mix well
4. Add five ounces of sifted flour, mix well
5. Place papers well-coated with butter on tins
6. Drop small spoonfuls of the batter in desired shapes, long or round, with great lightness so it doesn't settle
7. If desired, dust with cinnamon powder
8. Place in a not-very-hot oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chiqueadores (Sponge Fingers)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- two ounces of sugar
- four ounces of flour

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar for a quarter hour
2. Beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form
3. Mix everything together
4. Add the flour, well sifted, being careful to only mix it
5. Immediately make the chiqueadores
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chiricaya (Custard Flan)
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- white honey
- one glass of cream
- five egg yolks
- sugar to taste
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Burn a little sugar and mix it with white honey, use this to coat the mold
2. Arrange one glass of cream with five egg yolks, sugar to taste and lemon zest
3. Mix well and cook in a water bath
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chiricaya (Egg Custard Dessert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 glasses cooked milk
- 12 egg yolks, strained
- 4 ounces sugar
- cinnamon strips

## Directions

1. Strain twelve egg yolks into three glasses of cooked milk with sugar and cinnamon strips
2. Pour into a large china cup or mold
3. Place in the oven or in a water bath, which consists of placing clean water in a vessel and introducing the mold while being careful no water enters it, covering with metal and placing coals on top
4. As soon as it browns, remove the heat and let it simmer for two hours over even fire
5. Insert a knife and if it comes out clean, it can be served either cold or hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chiricaya con Almendra (Chiricaya with Almond)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 glasses milk
- 3 ounces sugar
- 3 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 8 egg yolks
- a little cinnamon powder
- butter

## Directions

1. Put in 3 glasses of milk 3 ounces of sugar and 3 ounces of peeled and ground almonds
2. Boil until it thickens
3. Lower the heat and when cold, add 8 egg yolks with a little cinnamon powder
4. Grease a mold with a little butter
5. Pour everything into it and put in the oven in a water bath
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chocolate de Vainilla (Vanilla Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- cacao
- sugar
- one ounce of vanilla essence

## Directions

1. Toast and weigh the cacao and sugar
2. Grind and when very fine, mix in one ounce of vanilla essence
3. Prepare as with cinnamon chocolate
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chocolate Mestizo (Mixed Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of cacao
- one pound of rice (well washed)
- five and a half pounds of sugar
- one quarter ounce of Chiapa pepper
- one ounce of anise
- eight chili strings
- one small piece of ginger
- one very small piece of chuchupate

## Directions

1. Toast three pounds of cacao and one pound of very well washed rice
2. Add five and a half pounds of sugar, one quarter ounce of Chiapa pepper, one ounce of anise, eight chili strings, one small piece of ginger and one very small piece of chuchupate, all well toasted without burning
3. Grind the rice very well first with all these ingredients, the cacao and sugar together
4. When well ground mix in the rice and give it two or three more passes until everything is very well mixed
5. Then finish the chocolate as the previous recipe
6. When consumed it must be boiled very well so that the rice cooks
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chocolates de Canela (Cinnamon Chocolates)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of cacao
- five pounds of dry sugar
- three ounces of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Toast three pounds of cacao and peel it, then weigh it; it will yield two and a half pounds
2. Add five pounds of dry sugar
3. Grind three ounces of cinnamon first and when well ground wrap it in paper
4. Then grind the sugar and cacao well mixed together
5. When very well ground mix in the cinnamon
6. Give it two more passes
7. Then remove the chocolate and weigh by pound
8. Form it by hand and make eight chocolates from each half pound
9. Cover them and the next day store them
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe a la Española (Spanish-Style Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chompipe (turkey), cleaned
- half butter and half lard
- 3 carrots, sliced lengthwise
- 4 bay leaves
- 6 to 8 onion heads, peeled and quartered
- a little Castilian pepper powder
- 1 cup of saffron water
- 1 tablespoon of flour, fried in the same butter where the turkey was fried
- 1 to 2 cups of water

## Directions

1. Clean the turkey well and cut into pieces of desired size.
2. Heat a pan with half butter and half lard.
3. When the turkey pieces begin to turn yellow, remove them and place in a pan.
4. Once all is well fried, add the carrots sliced lengthwise, bay leaves, peeled and quartered onion heads, Castilian pepper powder, saffron water, and flour fried in the same butter.
5. Add 1 to 2 cups of water until the meat is covered.
6. If the carrots are cooked before the turkey, remove them as well as the onions.
7. Serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe a la Francesa (Turkey French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one deboned turkey
- five pounds of loin
- one or two beef tongues
- ham

## Directions

1. Debone the turkey
2. Cut five pounds of loin into long, very thin strips, removing the skins
3. Have one or two beef tongues (either salted or fresh), according to the size of the turkey
4. Cut the tongues into very thin strips
5. Stuff the turkey by layering: one layer of loin strips, one of devil ham, one of tongue, and so on until finished
6. Sew closed, wrap in cloth, cook and press as described before
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe con Paté (Turkey with Paté)
body:
## Ingredients

- five pounds of meat
- half an ounce of salt
- a quarter ounce of Castilian pepper
- four eggs
- one tablespoon of flour
- one jar of paté
- one deboned turkey

## Directions

1. Grind five pounds of meat and add half an ounce of salt, a quarter ounce of Castilian pepper, four eggs and one tablespoon of flour
2. Have ready a jar of paté and a deboned turkey
3. Layer the meat and paté alternately, one layer of meat and one of paté, continuing until finished
4. Sew closed with thick thread, wrap in cloth, cook and press as with the previous recipes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe con Picado de Butifarra (Turkey with Sausage Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat (5 libras)
- Salt
- Cinnamon
- Clove
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Turkey

## Directions

1. Grind five libras of meat and add half an ounce of salt, a quarter ounce of cinnamon, the same amount of clove, the same amount of pepper, a glass of vinegar and let sit for two days, stirring to ensure even color
2. After two days, debone the turkey, cook and press the same as the previous recipe
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe con Relleno Negro (Turkey with Black Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin meat (4 libras)
- Ham (2 libras)
- Clove
- Castilian pepper
- Bay leaves
- Cinnamon
- Thyme
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Turkey

## Directions

1. Buy four libras of loin and two of ham and cut both meats into very thin pieces
2. Toast a quarter ounce of clove, the same amount of Castilian pepper, two bay leaves, a quarter ounce of cinnamon and a sprig of thyme
3. Once well toasted and ground with half an ounce of salt, dissolve in a cup of vinegar and add to the meat
4. Stir during the two days it sits in brine
5. On the third day, debone the turkey and fill it by alternating a layer of meat and a layer of ham until full
6. Sew with thick thread, wrap in cloth, cook and press as the previous recipes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe en Adobo (Turkey in Adobo Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chompipe (turkey), in pieces
- salt
- miltomate and tomato (or tomatillo snake variety)
- 1 guaque chile, cooked
- garlic
- oregano
- cumin
- 1 small piece of achiote
- 1 French (pepper?), chopped

## Directions

1. Cook the turkey in pieces with salt.
2. Grind miltomate, tomato, cooked guaque chile, garlic, oregano, cumin, achiote, and French together.
3. Dissolve this mixture in the same broth.
4. Fry and add the turkey pieces.
5. Serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe en Amarillo (Turkey in Yellow Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chompipe (turkey), in pieces
- salt
- carrots
- green beans
- chayote squash
- potatoes
- peas
- green fava beans
- bicarbonate of soda
- 1 tablespoon of lard
- 2 to 3 tomatoes, peeled and chopped
- 1 garlic clove
- 2 to 3 onions
- bread crumbs
- Castilian pepper
- 1 small piece of achiote

## Directions

1. Cook the turkey in pieces with salt.
2. Chop carrots, green beans, chayote, and potatoes.
3. Cook these vegetables separately, with salt. Cook carrots and potatoes separately, while green beans, peas, and fava beans are cooked together with a little bicarbonate of soda to bring out a good color.
4. Once all is cooked, place a pan over heat with lard, peeled and chopped tomatoes, garlic clove, and onions.
5. Once well fried, add a little of the same broth and the turkey, which should be well cooked.
6. Add bread crumbs mixed with Castilian pepper and a small piece of achiote.
7. Before serving, add the vegetables so they do not discolor.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe en Estofado (Turkey Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chompipe (turkey), cleaned
- 4 large tomatoes, peeled and halved
- 4 to 6 onions
- 1 head of garlic, peeled and mashed
- 1/4 ounce of clove
- 1/4 ounce of cinnamon
- 1/4 ounce of Castilian pepper
- 4 bay leaves
- 4 orange leaves
- 1 tablespoon of salt
- 1 glass of vinegar
- 1 piece of ground pineapple
- water to cover the meat

## Directions

1. Clean the turkey well and cut into quarters or pieces of desired size.
2. Place in a pan and add the peeled and halved tomatoes, onions, peeled and mashed garlic head, clove, cinnamon, Castilian pepper, bay leaves, orange leaves, salt, vinegar, and ground pineapple.
3. Cover with water until the meat is covered.
4. Place over gentle and even heat.
5. If the turkey is very large, it must simmer for three hours; if not, only two hours.
6. If you want it juicy, add bread powder; otherwise, let it dry and serve with salad or sauce.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe en Estofado (Turkey Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cleaned turkey
- four large tomatoes
- four to six onions
- one garlic head
- a quarter ounce of clove
- a quarter ounce of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Clean the turkey well and cut it into quarters or pieces of the desired size
2. Place in a pan and add four large peeled and halved tomatoes, four to six onions, one peeled and crushed garlic head, a quarter ounce of clove and a quarter ounce of cinnamon
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe en Mole Mexicano (Turkey in Mexican Mole)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chompipe (turkey), in pieces of desired size
- salt
- 6 to 8 pasilla chiles, seeded
- 6 ounces of peeled and fried almonds
- 4 ounces of toasted sesame seeds
- 1 small piece of nutmeg
- 4 chocolate tablets with cinnamon
- lard for frying
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the turkey in pieces with salt.
2. Fry the pasilla chiles in lard and grind them with peeled and fried almonds, toasted sesame seeds, nutmeg, and chocolate tablets with cinnamon.
3. Dissolve this mixture with the same broth and fry.
4. If desired sweet, add sugar to taste.
5. Serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe en Mole Tapio (Turkey in Tapio Mole)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pasilla chiles
- 2 French peppers, halved
- miltomate
- 1 ounce of sesame seeds
- 1 ounce of pumpkin seeds
- lard
- 1 small piece of edible clove
- 1 small piece of cinnamon
- Castilian peppers
- bread
- water
- raw turkey pieces

## Directions

1. Toast the pasilla chiles in lard, along with French peppers, miltomate, sesame seeds, and pumpkin seeds.
2. Grind all this with the chiles, edible clove, cinnamon, and Castilian peppers.
3. Last, grind the bread so it does not stick to the stone.
4. Dissolve with a little water and fry with 2 to 3 tablespoons of lard.
5. Add raw turkey pieces and sufficient water to allow them to simmer.
6. Cook over even heat for three hours.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe Jocom (Turkey Jocom)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chompipe (turkey), in pieces
- salt
- miltomate, raw
- handful of green chiles
- 1 garlic clove
- Castilian peppers

## Directions

1. Cook the turkey the same way as the previous recipe.
2. Grind sufficient raw miltomate according to the amount of meat, with a handful of green chiles, garlic clove, and Castilian peppers.
3. Fry this mixture, dissolving it with the same broth.
4. Add the turkey pieces.
5. Serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe Mechado (Turkey Studded with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey
- ham
- pepper powder
- garlic
- salt
- onion
- butter
- breadcrumb powder
- two tablespoons of Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Kill the turkey as the others do, well washed
2. Stud it on all sides with ham, pepper powder, garlic, salt and onion, all chopped finely
3. Rub well on all sides with butter and salt
4. Place in a pan with breadcrumb powder and two tablespoons of Parmesan cheese on top
5. Put in the oven for three hours as the previous recipes, basting with its juices to prevent it from drying out
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe Relleno (Stuffed Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Pork meat (5 libras for large turkey)
- Salt
- Castilian pepper
- Cinnamon powder
- Raw eggs
- Bread flour
- Ham (half libra)
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Leeks
- Parsley
- Beef feet

## Directions

1. Hang the turkey and cut its tongue or throat
2. Once dead, place in a pan and pour a jug of boiling water, turning quickly so it does not boil over
3. Pluck and clean well, then burn some straw or twigs to remove all small feathers
4. Carefully cut the wings and legs without breaking them, cut the head and split the skin along the backbone
5. Carefully remove the meat leaving only the bones
6. Once well deboned, remove most of the fat and the top part that has no meat, cover with breast pieces trimmed from thick areas
7. Grind five libras of pork meat, passing it twice through the grinder until very fine
8. Once ground, add half an ounce of salt, a quarter ounce of Castilian pepper, half an ounce of cinnamon powder (if desired), four or five raw eggs and a spoonful of bread flour
9. Mix well and stuff the turkey
10. Cut half a libra of ham into long strips and insert inside the meat
11. Sew closed with thick thread the openings of the legs and wings
12. Wrap in a long cloth, sewing it very well and very tightly to press it
13. Place in a pan with water, salt, bay leaves, a sprig of thyme, a bunch of leeks, a bunch of parsley and two well-cleaned beef feet
14. Let boil four hours over very even heat
15. After this time place in a press while still very hot
16. The next day serve as desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe Relleno a la Mexicana (Mexican-Style Stuffed Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cleaned turkey
- ten apples
- half pound of butter
- half pound of peeled almonds
- half pound of prunes
- half pound of raisins
- one glass of sherry wine
- a little ground bread
- Castilian pepper
- a small piece of nutmeg
- sugar (optional)
- two bay leaves
- two onion heads

## Directions

1. Clean the turkey well and debone it, leaving only the wings and legs
2. Peel ten apples and remove the seeds very well, cut them into small pieces and give them a light boil
3. Melt half a pound of butter and in this place the apples with half a pound of peeled and halved almonds, half a pound of prunes and half a pound of raisins, one glass of sherry wine, a little ground bread with Castilian pepper and a small piece of nutmeg, and if desired a little sugar
4. When well mixed, stuff the turkey, sew it closed with thick thread
5. Place in a pan, rub with butter and salt, place two bay leaves and two onion heads on it
6. Place in a gentle oven for three hours, basting with its juices so that it comes out juicy
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe Relleno al Horno (Oven-Roasted Stuffed Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey with wings and legs
- salt
- butter
- four pounds of pork meat
- one tablespoon of lard
- four peeled and chopped tomatoes
- four onions
- two garlic cloves
- one small glass of vinegar
- one glass of sherry
- a little ground breadcrumb powder
- eight cloves
- eight Castilian peppers
- one small piece of cinnamon
- one tablespoon of sugar (optional)
- two carrots
- one güisquil or half if large
- ten to fifteen tender green beans
- four large potatoes
