- one cup of tender peas
- one ounce of raisins
- ten to twelve peeled and halved almonds
- capers
- olives
- four bay leaves
- cloves
- Castilian peppers
- lettuce

## Directions

1. Debone the turkey leaving the wings and legs
2. Rub with salt and butter and let sit for one hour
3. Parboil four pounds of pork meat with salt, remove and chop finely
4. Place a pan over the fire with one tablespoon of lard, four peeled and chopped tomatoes, four onions and two garlic cloves
5. Add the meat with a small glass of vinegar, one glass of sherry, a little ground breadcrumb powder, eight cloves, eight Castilian peppers and one small piece of cinnamon
6. Add one tablespoon of sugar if you wish it sweet
7. Remove when half cooked
8. Have ready two carrots, chopped and cooked, half a güisquil (or whole if small), ten to fifteen tender green beans, four large potatoes and one cup of tender peas
9. Mix everything with the meat, adding one ounce of raisins, ten to twelve peeled and halved almonds, capers and olives
10. When well mixed, sew the turkey closed with thick thread
11. Rub the turkey with salt and butter, place four bay leaves, some cloves and Castilian peppers on it
12. Place in the oven for three hours, basting with its juices every fifteen minutes
13. Serve hot, garnishing with green lettuce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chompipe Trufado (Truffle Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- five pounds of meat
- half an ounce of salt
- a quarter ounce of pepper
- a quarter ounce of cinnamon powder
- four eggs
- one teaspoon of flour
- one deboned turkey
- one or two bottles of fruits

## Directions

1. Grind five pounds of meat and add half an ounce of salt, a quarter ounce of pepper, a quarter ounce of cinnamon powder, four eggs and one teaspoon of flour
2. Have ready a deboned turkey
3. Stuff the turkey with the meat mixture, placing one or two bottles of fruits inside
4. Sew closed with thick thread, wrap in cloth, cook and press as previously described
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chorizos Colorados (Red Chorizos)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds meat
- 1/2 ounce salt
- 1/4 ounce pepper
- 1/4 ounce cumin
- 1/4 ounce oregano
- 4 garlic cloves
- 3 pieces achiote
- 4 cooked guajillo chiles without seeds
- 1 cup cooked miltomate
- 1/2 bottle vinegar
- intestines

## Directions

1. Grind 6 pounds of meat
2. Add 1/2 ounce salt, 1/4 ounce pepper, 1/4 ounce cumin, 1/4 ounce oregano, 4 garlic cloves, 3 pieces achiote, 4 cooked guajillo chiles without seeds
3. Dissolve everything in 1/2 bottle vinegar and add to the meat, mixing very well
4. Let sit in this infusion for three days
5. Fill intestines with the mixture and tie them as with the previous chorizo recipe
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chorizos Negros (Black Chorizos)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds meat
- 1/2 ounce salt
- 1/8 ounce Castilian pepper
- 1/8 ounce cinnamon
- 1/4 ounce clove
- 1/2 bottle vinegar
- 2 ounces ham
- intestines

## Directions

1. Grind 6 pounds of meat
2. Add 1/2 ounce salt, 1/8 ounce Castilian pepper, 1/8 ounce cinnamon, and 1/4 ounce clove
3. Dissolve everything in 1/2 bottle vinegar and mix well with the meat
4. Leave in brine for four days, stirring daily
5. Mince 2 ounces ham and mix very well
6. Fill well-washed intestines with the mixture and tie them about a hand's width long
7. Place in the sun for one day
8. Store in a fly trap to prevent spoilage
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Choux à la Crème (Cream Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- One glass of hot water
- Salt to taste
- Two tablespoons of sugar
- Two ounces of butter
- One fresh lemon peel
- Four ounces of sifted bread flour
- Four eggs
- Powdered sugar for topping
- Cream filling (Crema de Leche)

## Directions

1. Put one glass of hot water with salt, two tablespoons of sugar, two ounces of butter, and one fresh lemon peel in a pan
2. At the first boil, remove from heat, remove the lemon peel
3. Gradually add four ounces of sifted bread flour, stirring very well to avoid lumps
4. Place over very low heat, stirring constantly until a compact ball forms
5. Remove from heat and stir until cool
6. Mix in four eggs one at a time, ensuring each is well combined before adding the next
7. Place on a baking sheet in desired sizes, sprinkle with powdered sugar
8. Bake until cooked
9. Split in half and fill with cream filling (Crema de Leche)
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chuchitos (Corn Tamale Patties)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds corn dough (ground fine)
- salt
- one cup saffron water (or made without saffron, plain white)
- miltomate (ground)
- tomato
- dried guaque chile (cooked)
- lorocos (optional)
- half pound melted lard
- two dried chiles (cooked)
- a small piece of cinnamon
- three roasted tomatoes
- a small piece of achiote
- broth from cooked pork
- corn husks or dobladores (wrappers)
- pork meat with sauce (for filling)
- achiote, chile, and a little cumin (alternative filling seasonings)

## Directions

1. Grind two pounds of corn dough fine
2. Add salt and one cup saffron water, or make it plain white without saffron
3. To the white version, grind plenty of miltomate, tomato, and cooked dried guaque chile; lorocos may be added if desired; add half pound of melted lard
4. Knead for half an hour; if it sticks to the hand, add four more ounces of lard
5. Grind two cooked chiles with a small piece of cinnamon, three roasted tomatoes, and a small piece of achiote
6. Mix this with a little of the broth in which the pork meat was partially cooked
7. Form tortillas of the desired size from the white dough
8. Place a piece of meat with its sauce on each tortilla
9. Close the dough and wrap in corn husks or dobladores
10. Cook the same way as the others; if desired, grind only achiote, chile, and a little cumin for the sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chuletas a la Italiana (Chops Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- backbone with meat
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Ensure the backbone remains well fleshed
2. Cut into round pieces
3. Flatten very well with a machete
4. Season with salt and lemon juice
5. Leave for a quarter hour
6. Then place on a grill, turning frequently with great care so they do not dry out
7. Serve juicy
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chuletas a la Milanesa (Milanese-Style Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin with rib attached
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Two eggs
- White bread powder
- Lard
- Lettuce
- Mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut chops leaving one third bone attached
2. Pound with a mallet to flatten but not make thin
3. Rub with salt and pepper powder
4. One hour before cooking, beat two eggs with salt for twelve chops
5. Coat chops in the beaten egg
6. Roll in white bread powder
7. Fry in hot lard over low heat
8. Serve on tender lettuce leaves with mashed potatoes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chuletas de Carnero (Mutton Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin with rib attached
- salt
- pepper powder
- egg
- bread powder
- lard
- lettuce
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Keep the loin attached to the rib
2. Cut the bones about a hand's length long
3. Flatten with a mallet
4. Season with salt and pepper powder
5. Coat in beaten egg and then in bread powder
6. Fry in hot lard
7. Serve on very tender lettuce leaves with spicy sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chuletas de Pollo (Chicken Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- very tender chickens
- lemon
- salt
- pepper
- egg, beaten
- bread crumbs
- lard or butter, hot
- lettuce
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Clean very tender chickens and cut into pieces
2. Place in a brine of lemon, salt, and pepper
3. Pass the pieces through beaten egg and then through bread crumbs
4. Fry in hot lard or butter
5. Garnish with lettuce and chopped parsley
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Chuletas Fingidas (Mock Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork ribs
- Salt
- Pork loin
- Tomato
- Onion
- Raisins
- Almonds
- Sweet sherry
- Bread powder
- Clove
- Pepper
- Cinnamon
- Flour
- Eggs
- Sweet orange juice
- Butter
- Colored tissue paper
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the ribs with salt
2. Cut bones to a span in length and scrape away all meat until the bone is very clean
3. Fry this meat with one pound of cooked pork loin, chopped tomato and onion
4. Add raisins and almonds sliced lengthwise, salt, one cup of sweet sherry, a little bread powder mixed with clove, pepper and cinnamon
5. Prepare a dough with one pound of flour, four eggs, juice of three sweet oranges, half a teaspoon of salt, and one tablespoon of butter
6. Knead for half an hour until it crackles and is very fine
7. Roll out with a rolling pin, neither thin nor thick
8. Cut circles with the rim of a glass
9. Place a bone on one circle with a large spoonful of the meat mixture
10. Cover with another circle, sealing the edges very carefully so the filling does not escape
11. Have a pan with sufficient hot butter ready
12. Fry the cutlets until golden
13. Dust with fine sugar powder
14. Have colored tissue paper cut very fine ready
15. Use this to wrap the bone protruding from the dough
16. Arrange on a platter
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Churros (Churros)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- Half teaspoon of salt
- Milk

## Directions

1. Take one pound of flour, add half teaspoon of salt and mix with a little milk as hot as possible
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Churros (Spanish Fried Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- half a teaspoonful of salt
- milk, as hot as possible
- lard for frying
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Take one pound of flour, add half a teaspoonful of salt and moisten with a little of the hottest milk possible, stirring so it does not form lumps, leaving it like dough for making fritters
2. Place in a long tin can that has a small hole opened in the bottom, add the dough and press with a wooden pin so the dough comes out and falls into very hot lard
3. Cut and while still hot, sprinkle powdered sugar on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cocada (Coconut Candy)
body:
## Ingredients

- two liters of milk
- two pounds of sugar
- two coconuts, grated with their water
- eight egg yolks
- raisins
- almonds

## Directions

1. Put milk and sugar on the fire
2. When it reaches medium-soft ball stage, mix in the two grated coconuts with their water
3. Put on the fire until it is very thick
4. When it is fried, mix in eight well-beaten egg yolks
5. Put on a platter, decorate with raisins and almonds
6. Place in the oven to brown
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cocido (Spanish Boiled Meat and Vegetable Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- four liters of water
- two pounds of very good beef brisket
- two pounds of very good bone
- one tablespoon of salt
- four to six leeks
- one head of garlic
- half an ounce of well-washed rice
- two ounces of chickpeas (soaked the night before)
- ten crayfish, well washed with hot water (optional)
- one sheep's head (optional)
- one chicken or half of a fat hen (optional)
- one chayote squash
- one piece of cabbage
- two tender corn ears
- two carrots
- two white turnip roots
- one piece of tender güicoy squash
- some small potatoes (if available), about four large potatoes
- tender green beans
- ichíntalo
- plantain slices (steamed)
- pieces of black ayote squash
- fried seasoning paste of tomato, onion, and minced garlic
- one tablespoon of flour fried in lard or butter
- minced parsley

## Directions

1. Put four liters of water in a pot and add two pounds of very good beef brisket and two pounds of very good bone
2. Add one tablespoon of salt, four to six leeks, one head of garlic, half an ounce of well-washed rice, and two ounces of chickpeas that have been soaked the night before
3. When it begins to boil, remove all the foam
4. From a medium height, let one liter of cold water fall into it
5. Let it boil for two and a half hours
6. If desired, add ten crayfish well washed with hot water, lifting their little caps and cutting the tips off their pincers, one sheep's head, one chicken or half of a fat hen
7. After all of this has boiled for two and a half hours, add one chayote squash, one piece of cabbage, two tender corn ears, two carrots, two white turnip roots, and one piece of tender güicoy squash
8. Add tender green beans, ichíntalo, some small potatoes if available (about four large potatoes)
9. When everything is cooked, serve the cooked or stewed meat on one platter
10. On another platter serve all the vegetables with slices of plantain steamed and pieces of black ayote squash, not forgetting to add tender green beans and ichíntalo to the cocido
11. The broth is used for all kinds of hearty soups by simply adding fried seasoning paste of tomato, onion, and minced garlic, one tablespoon of flour fried in lard or butter, and minced parsley
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cocido Español (Spanish Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one good hen broth
- one pound rib
- two beef heels
- four ounces ham
- two or three red chorizos
- half pound chickpeas (soaked since the day before)
- four white turnips
- three or four carrots
- chopped cabbage
- handful of washed rice
- sufficient tomatoes
- four or five onion heads
- lard

## Directions

1. Prepare a good hen broth and add one pound rib, two beef heels, four ounces ham, and two or three red chorizos
2. Boil until everything is cooked
3. Strain the broth and finely chop all the meat
4. Return the meat to the strained broth and add half pound chickpeas that have been soaked since the day before
5. Add sufficient water until everything is cooked
6. Add four white turnips and three or four carrots cut into pieces and cooked, along with chopped cabbage and a handful of washed rice
7. Roast a sufficient amount of tomato, then grind it
8. Slice four or five onion heads and fry them with the tomato in a bit of lard
9. Add this to the stew
10. Let it boil for ten or fifteen minutes and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Coctel (Cocktail)
body:
## Ingredients

- one glass of water
- two glasses of whisky or Old Tom
- one small glass of Angostura bitters
- a few drops of lemon

## Directions

1. Put one glass of water and two glasses of whisky or Old Tom in a glass
2. Add one small glass of Angostura bitters and a few drops of lemon
3. Moisten the small glasses with the same cocktail or pass them over some very white and sifted sugar
4. Serve the cocktails with their lemon peels
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Coctel (Cocktail)
body:
## Ingredients

- One glass of water
- Two glasses of whisky or Old Tom
- One small glass of Angostura bitters
- A few drops of lemon
- Sugar, very white and sifted
- Lemon peels

## Directions

1. Put one glass of water and two glasses of whisky or Old Tom in a glass
2. Add one small glass of Angostura bitters and a few drops of lemon
3. Moisten the small glasses with the same cocktail or pass them over a little very white and sifted sugar
4. Serve the cocktails in these glasses with their lemon peels
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Coliflor en Amarillo (Cauliflower in Yellow Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Salt
- Manteca (lard), one tablespoon
- Tomato
- Onion
- Garlic, one clove
- Water, one cup
- Bread powder, ground
- Achiote (annatto) for color
- Pimienta (pepper)
- Flour, one teaspoon (optional), dissolved in water

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflower as in previous recipes.
2. Fry in one tablespoon of manteca, tomato, onion and one clove of garlic.
3. Add the cauliflower with one cup of water, ground bread powder with achiote color and pepper.
4. If not using achiote, use one teaspoon of flour dissolved in water.
5. Serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Coliflor en Salsa de Harina (Cauliflower in Flour Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Salt
- Mantequilla (butter), one tablespoon
- Bread flour, one tablespoon
- Water, half a cup
- Bread powder
- Parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflower in pieces with salt.
2. Fry in one tablespoon of butter one tablespoon of bread flour.
3. Add half a cup of water without stopping to stir.
4. Put in the cauliflower and serve with bread powder and chopped parsley.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Coliflor Guisada (Stewed Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Oil or manteca, two to three tablespoons
- Garlic, three or four cloves
- Salt
- Egg, beaten
- White bread powder
- Lettuce leaves, green
- Olives
- Onions, cooked and separated into leaves
- Oregano powder, abundant
- Avocado, sliced

## Directions

1. In two to three tablespoons of oil or manteca, fry three or four cloves of garlic.
2. Have the cauliflower already cut into pieces and cooked with salt; dry it with a cloth.
3. Coat in beaten egg, then in white bread powder.
4. Fry in the same manteca where the garlic was fried.
5. When golden, place on green lettuce leaves on the platter.
6. Decorate with olives, cooked and separated onions, abundant oregano powder and avocado slices.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Compota de Durazno (Peach Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- apricots
- plums
- pears
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the peaches, apricots, plums, and pears, remove the pits
2. Place in a jar alternating layers of fruit and white sugar until the jar is full
3. Place in a bain-marie for three hours
4. Allow to cool before removing the jars to prevent them from breaking
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Conchas de Seso (Brain Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- garlic
- onion
- flour
- milk
- pepper powder
- one brain
- parsley
- oysters with their broth
- Parmesan cheese powder
- bread powder

## Directions

1. Fry butter, garlic and chopped onion
2. When it begins to brown, add a little flour and incorporate a little milk
3. Add pepper powder, chopped brain and parsley and oysters with their broth
4. Fill the shells, sprinkle Parmesan cheese powder and bread powder on top and put in the oven for half an hour
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Condes (Pastry Confection)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound of flour
- 4 egg whites
- a pinch of salt
- cold water as needed
- butter equal in weight to the dough
- 2 egg whites beaten
- 6 ounces of sugar
- a few drops of lemon

## Directions

1. Put half a pound of flour with four egg whites, a pinch of salt, and enough cold water to make a soft dough
2. Work it well, weigh it, and add the same weight of very firm butter
3. Roll out, place the butter in the middle and close the four corners
4. Roll out with the rolling pin, dust with flour and leave in the air for about five minutes
5. Fold again, roll out the same way four or five times
6. On the last fold, frost with two well-beaten egg whites, six ounces of sugar and a few drops of lemon
7. Cut into small pieces and place on baking sheets in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Conejo a la Valenciana (Valencian-Style Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- ham
- one glass sherry or red wine
- garlic cloves
- pepper powder
- bay leaves
- thyme
- two orange leaves
- mushrooms (if available)
- water

## Directions

1. After cleaning the rabbit well, lard it with ham
2. Once browned, add one glass of sherry or red wine, peeled garlic cloves, pepper powder, bay leaves, thyme, two orange leaves, and chopped mushrooms if available
3. Cover with water and let boil
4. When cooked, serve with the strained sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Conejo en Escabeche (Rabbit in Escabeche)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- pieces of ham
- pepper powder
- one and a half cups oil
- thyme
- bay leaf
- one cup water
- half cup vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. After cleaning the rabbit well, lard it with pieces of ham and pepper powder
2. Place over heat with one and a half cups of oil, thyme, and bay leaf
3. When it stops releasing juice, add one cup of water and half cup of vinegar with salt
4. Let it brown very slowly
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Conejo en Salsa (Rabbit in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- salt
- butter
- onion
- broth

## Directions

1. After cleaning and cooking the rabbit with salt, fry onions in butter
2. Add a little broth and place the rabbit quarters in to give a light boil
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Conejo Frito (Fried Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- salt
- a little white wine or sherry
- juice of one lemon
- bay leaf
- thyme
- pepper
- chopped garlic
- one tablespoon oil
- one tablespoon butter
- white bread crumbs

## Directions

1. After cleaning, place in a pan with salt, a little white wine or sherry, lemon juice, bay leaf, thyme, pepper, and chopped garlic
2. Once cooked, remove and fry in one tablespoon of oil and one of butter
3. Coat with white bread crumbs and let brown
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Conejo Guisado (Stewed Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- oil
- salt
- onion, chopped
- pepper
- nutmeg
- a small piece of ginger
- water
- a little vinegar
- a little saffron water
- bread, fried

## Directions

1. Clean the rabbit well and fry it in oil with salt
2. When it takes color, fry plenty of chopped onion in the same oil
3. Grind a little pepper, nutmeg, and a small piece of ginger and add with water to cover the rabbit
4. When somewhat tender, add a little vinegar and a little saffron water
5. Let it reduce and place on a platter, surrounding with fried bread slices
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Consomé (Clear Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- six liters of water
- one large tablespoon of salt
- eight pounds of very good bone
- one fat hen or three large fat chickens
- eight to ten leeks
- one sheep's head
- two bay leaves
- ten Castilian peppercorns
- one glass of dry sherry (optional)
- four egg yolks (optional) or one yolk per person (optional)

## Directions

1. Place six liters of water in a pot with one large tablespoon of salt, eight pounds of very good bone, one fat hen or three large fat chickens, eight to ten leeks, one sheep's head, two bay leaves, and ten Castilian peppercorns
2. Let it boil for four hours over steady heat
3. Remove from heat and let it cool
4. Skim off the fat very well and leave it in a cool place
5. The next day, heat it and serve it
6. If desired, add one glass of dry sherry or four egg yolks to make it a thin broth, or if preferred, add one yolk per person
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cortantes (Cut Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound of sugar
- 1 pound of flour
- zest of 1 lemon
- egg whites as needed for a soft dough
- a pinch of salt
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Put half a pound of sugar on a clean table with one pound of flour, the zest of a lemon and enough egg whites to make a soft dough
2. Add a pinch of salt, knead and then roll out with the rolling pin
3. Place on a baking sheet in the oven, and when partially cooked coat with egg yolk and return to the oven
4. When baked, remove, cut to the desired size and return to the oven to brown
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Costilla Adobada (Marinated Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork ribs
- Salt
- Sour orange juice
- Tomatillo
- Garlic cloves
- Oregano
- Cumin
- Castilian peppers
- Dried chile
- Achiote
- Vinegar
- Lard

## Directions

1. Season ribs with one ounce of salt and sour orange juice
2. Let sit overnight in this brine
3. The next day, grind a little tomatillo with two garlic cloves, oregano, cumin, Castilian peppers, a dried chile, and a small piece of achiote
4. Dilute with two tablespoons of vinegar and salt
5. Fry in a little lard
6. Rub the fried mixture on the ribs
7. Place in the sun for one or two days to eat roasted or in chimol
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Costilla en Adobo (Ribs in Adobo Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork ribs
- Salt
- Cooked or roasted tomato
- Garlic clove
- Oregano
- Achiote
- Dried chile
- French bread
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook the ribs with salt
2. Grind cooked or roasted tomato with a garlic clove, a little oregano, a small piece of achiote, a dried chile, and soaked French bread
3. Dissolve this mixture in the broth in which the meat cooked
4. Add the ribs and let them come to a boil two or three times
5. Serve hot with slices of cooked potatoes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Costilla Migada (Breadcrumb Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork ribs
- Bread powder
- Peppers
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Cook the ribs
2. Break them into pieces and fry
3. When they turn golden, add bread powder mixed with peppers and spice cloves
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Costillas Guisadas (Braised Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork ribs
- Lard or butter
- Garlic clove
- Tomatoes
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Clove
- Vinegar
- Lemon juice
- Bread powder
- Cheese

## Directions

1. Cut ribs to desired size
2. Fry in a pot with lard or butter, a clove of garlic, two ground tomatoes mixed with a little water, salt, pepper powder, clove, and three tablespoons of vinegar
3. Let simmer for two hours
4. Remove ribs and add lemon juice, bread powder, and cheese
5. Fry in a little lard
6. Serve as desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Costrada de Almendra y Huevo (Almond and Egg Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- water
- 12 egg yolks
- 1 pound almond, peeled and ground
- 1/2 glass sherry wine
- 1/2 ounce cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put one pound of sugar with a little water on the fire; when it reaches the hard-crack stage, let it cool
2. Add twelve well-beaten egg yolks and one pound of peeled and ground almond
3. Mix everything well and put back on the fire with one-half glass of sherry wine and one-half ounce of cinnamon
4. Remove when it pulls away from the pan, and if desired, put in the oven to brown
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Costrada de Papas (Potato Crust)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of peeled, cooked and ground potato
- three eggs
- four ounces of butter
- little salt
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. To one pound of peeled, cooked and ground potato add three eggs (white and yolk), four ounces of butter, a few grains of salt and sugar to taste
2. Mix everything very well and put in a pan coated with lard in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema (Cream Frosting)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound of lump sugar
- One teaspoon of vanilla
- Half pound of strawberries passed through a sieve
- Three egg whites
- Half pound of melted butter
- Whole ripe strawberries for decoration

## Directions

1. Place sugar, vanilla, and sieved strawberries in a copper pot
2. When sugar is melted, let it reach the caramel stage
3. Beat three egg whites until stiff peaks form
4. Lower the caramel and gradually mix the beaten egg whites with the caramel
5. When cold, mix in half pound of melted butter while continuing to beat
6. Cover the cake with this cream
7. Decorate on all sides with whole ripe strawberries
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema (Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk, sweetened to taste
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon bread flour
- a little sherry

## Directions

1. Heat a little sweetened milk
2. Add 1 tablespoon of butter and 1 tablespoon of bread flour so it reaches a regular consistency
3. When it thickens, add a little sherry
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema Chantilly (Chantilly Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- heavy cream, half liter
- gum tragacanth, half teaspoon
- sugar, two ounces

## Directions

1. Take half a liter of cream, half teaspoon of gum tragacanth and two ounces of sugar
2. Use as desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema Chantilly (Chantilly Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of cream
- half a teaspoon of gum tragacanth
- two ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. Use for whatever you desire
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema de Aguacate (Avocado Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- avocados
- mustard powder
- salt
- pepper powder

## Directions

1. Prepare the avocados by removing them from the shell
2. Place in a bowl and mash with a masher
3. Add a little foreign mustard powder, salt, and pepper powder
4. Beat well and serve immediately, as everything should be done close to serving time
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema de Almendra (Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 glasses cooked milk
- 2 tablespoons bread flour
- 0.5 pounds white sugar
- 4 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 drop bitter almond essence

## Directions

1. Put in 2 glasses of cooked milk 2 tablespoons of bread flour, 0.5 pounds of white sugar, 4 ounces of peeled and ground almonds, 4 egg yolks, and 1 drop of bitter almond essence
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema de Fresas (Strawberry Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- heavy cream (0.5 liter)
- powdered sugar (2 cups)
- strawberries (0.25 pound, lightly mashed)
- strawberries for serving (halved)
- muslin strip (for covering)
- ice and salt (for freezing)

## Directions

1. Whip 0.5 liter of cream until stiff.
2. Add 2 cups of powdered sugar and 0.25 pound of lightly mashed strawberries.
3. Place everything in a covered mold with a strip of muslin to prevent ice from penetrating it.
4. Place the mold in a bowl with ice and salt for 4 hours.
5. To serve, unmold and fill the cavity with halved strawberries mixed with whipped cream.
6. Decorate with leaves and serve with biscuits.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema de Leche (Milk Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two glasses of cooked milk
- Sugar to taste
- Salt to taste
- Three egg yolks
- One ounce of cornstarch or rice flour, soaked and ground
- Half teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Put two glasses of cooked milk with sugar to taste, salt, three egg yolks, and one ounce of soaked and ground cornstarch or rice flour
2. Strain through fine cloth
3. Place on heat, stirring constantly so it does not stick
4. Add half teaspoon of vanilla
5. Pour onto a plate; can be used for many pastries
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema de Piña (Pineapple Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of sugar
- One pineapple, ground
- Six egg yolks
- Half pound of ground almond

## Directions

1. Clarify two pounds of sugar
2. When at point, add the ground pineapple
3. When it sets, remove and when cool mix in six well-beaten egg yolks and half pound of ground almond
4. Return to the fire until it reaches the consistency of before
5. Place in a platter and in the oven to brown
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crema de Piña (Pineapple Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of sugar
- One pineapple, ground
- Six egg yolks, well beaten
- Half pound of ground almonds
- Butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Clarify two pounds of sugar
2. When it reaches the proper point, add the ground pineapple
3. When preserved, remove and let cool
4. Mix in six well-beaten egg yolks and half a pound of ground almonds
5. Return to heat until it reaches the consistency of ante
6. Place on a platter and in the oven to brown
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Criadillas a la Extremeña (Sweetbreads Extremadura Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweetbreads
- Salt
- Black pepper powder
- White bread powder
- Lard
- Rice
- Miltomate sauce, spicy

## Directions

1. Split the sweetbreads in half, being careful they come out very whole
2. Rub with salt and black pepper powder
3. Let sit for a quarter hour
4. Coat in white bread powder and fry in lard over very low heat
5. When golden, remove and serve with dry rice and a spicy miltomate sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Criadillas a la Marinera (Sweetbreads Seafarer Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper powder
- bread
- oil
- lettuce
- potatoes
- cheese
- sardines
- egg

## Directions

1. After cooking well and removing the thick skin, cut lengthwise or into slices as desired
2. Place in a dish with a little vinegar, salt and pepper powder
3. Leave for a quarter hour
4. Fry very thin slices of bread in oil and when golden remove and place on green lettuce leaves on a platter
5. Add a layer of sweetbreads, a layer of cooked potato slices with salt bathed in the sweetbread infusion
6. Cover with pieces of fresh cheese or powder of dry cheese with sardines and pieces of hard egg
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Criadillas a la Milanesa (Sweetbreads Milanese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- egg
- bread powder
- lard
- lettuce
- peas
- tomato
- onion

## Directions

1. After cooking, remove the thick skin that covers them
2. Cut lengthwise
3. Dry and coat in beaten egg, then in bread powder
4. Fry in hot lard
5. Place on tender lettuce leaves, garnish with well-cooked green peas, slices of peeled tomato without seeds and very thin onion slices
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Criadillas Asadas (Roasted Sweetbreads)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweetbreads
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Dried chile
- Tomates
- Onion
- Hierbabuena (mint)
- Sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Split the sweetbreads in half with the thick skin still on
2. Sprinkle with salt and a little vinegar and let sit for a quarter hour
3. Roast on a grill over very low heat
4. For the chirmol sauce: toast a dried chile in hot coals until golden and not burnt, remove the seeds, crush it dry with salt, then gradually add hot water so no skin remains
5. Once well dissolved, add two roasted and peeled tomatoes, a good amount of minced onion, hierbabuena, and sour orange juice
6. Serve the sweetbreads with the chirmol
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Criadillas Asadas (Roasted Sweetbreads)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut in half with the thick skin still attached
2. Season with salt and a little vinegar
3. Leave for a quarter hour
4. Then roast on a grill over very low heat
5. Serve with a chimol sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Criadillas en Salsa (Sweetbreads in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- tomato
- thyme
- dried chile
- vinegar
- water
- oil

## Directions

1. Cook as above and cut into slices
2. Grind some raw tomatoes with a sprig of thyme and a seeded and soaked dried chile
3. Dilute with a teaspoon of vinegar and water
4. Fry in one tablespoon of oil
5. When it has boiled sufficiently add the sweetbread slices
6. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Crocandi (Almond Brittle)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of brown sugar
- One teaspoon of butter
- Half pound of almonds
- Butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Place one pound of brown sugar with one teaspoon of butter on heat
2. Peel and toast half pound of almonds, then chop them
3. When the sugar no longer shows grains, add the almonds
4. Remove from heat and pour onto a buttered board
5. Shape and cut into desired figures
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Croquetas de Costilla (Rib Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork ribs
- Salt
- Pork meat
- Tomato
- Onion
- Garlic clove
- Vinegar
- Dry sherry
- White bread powder
- Peppers
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Butter
- Paper

## Directions

1. Cook the ribs with salt
2. Cut the bones to a span in length, scraping the meat so most of the bone is clean
3. Have one pound of cooked and finely chopped pork meat ready
4. Fry with peeled and chopped tomato, onion, and a garlic clove
5. When half fried, add two tablespoons of vinegar and two of dry sherry
6. Season with salt, a little white bread powder, about four peppers, two cloves, and a small piece of cinnamon
7. Place the bone on a piece of white paper spread with butter
8. Fold the paper or tie with twine
9. Place on a platter in a low oven or over a grill with low heat
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cuajada de Almendra (Almond Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- one glass of milk
- one pound of sugar
- three ounces of peeled and ground almonds

## Directions

1. Sweeten one glass of milk with one pound of sugar
2. Add three ounces of peeled and ground almonds
3. Place over fire and let it reach the consistency of paste
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cuajada de Avellanas (Hazelnut Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of hazelnuts
- Half pound of peeled and toasted almonds
- One pound of sugar
- Nine eggs
- One teaspoon of cinnamon powder
- Half pound of candied chilacayote, chopped

## Directions

1. Grind the hazelnuts and almonds in the machine
2. Add the sugar, eggs, cinnamon powder, and chopped candied chilacayote
3. Mix everything well
4. Pour into a greased mold
5. Place in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cuajaditas (Little Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of bread flour or salporrita flour
- three eggs
- salt grains
- two egg yolks
- four ounces of sugar
- five ounces of lard
- anise grains

## Directions

1. Mix the flour, eggs, salt grains, yolks, sugar, lard, and anise grains
2. Knead very well for half an hour
3. Place small balls on baking trays with sprinkles on top
4. Bake in the oven for ten minutes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Cusanitos (Small Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- four egg yolks
- four ounces of butter
- four ounces of lard
- a pinch of salt
- four ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour, four egg yolks, four ounces of butter, four ounces of lard, a pinch of salt, and four ounces of sugar.
2. Knead well.
3. Place in a mold and make small rings.
4. Put in a hot oven.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Delicias (Delights)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of peeled almonds (dry)
- 3 ounces of sifted sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 egg yolk (if needed)
- juice of 1 lemon
- syrup at proper stage for coating

## Directions

1. Grind one pound of peeled almonds (dry)
2. Add three ounces of well-sifted sugar
3. Mix this with one egg and if too stiff add one yolk with the juice of a lemon
4. Place on tins in the form of mantecado
5. Bake in the oven
6. When golden, coat with proper stage syrup
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Dessert de Frambuesa (Raspberry Dessert)
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries without liquid (2 cups)
- bread crumbs (2 cups)
- cinnamon powder (0.5 teaspoon)
- butter (1 tablespoon)
- sugar (2 tablespoons)
- whipped cream sweetened and thick (for serving)

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar and cinnamon together.
2. In a buttered platter, spread half of the bread crumbs.
3. Cover with raspberries.
4. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon mixture and dot with pieces of butter.
5. Cover with the remaining bread crumbs, sugar, and butter.
6. Place in the oven until golden brown.
7. Serve with sweetened, thick whipped cream.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Donis (Fried Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a glass of cream
- one cup of sugar
- one egg
- flour as needed
- two teaspoons of Royal baking powder
- sugar powder
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat the cream with one cup of sugar and one whole egg.
2. Add flour as needed.
3. Add two teaspoons of Royal baking powder.
4. Make a soft dough.
5. Roll out with a rolling pin and cut in the shape of rings.
6. Fry the pastries.
7. When done, coat with sugar powder and cinnamon.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Dulce de Huevo (Egg Sweet)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and one half of sugar
- nine egg yolks
- six ounces of butter
- juice of one pineapple

## Directions

1. Place one pound and one half of sugar over heat
2. When it reaches the point, let it cool
3. Stir in nine egg yolks passed through a strainer
4. When at medium point add six ounces of butter and the juice of one pineapple
5. Remove when it detaches from the pan
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Duraznos en su Jugo (Peaches in Their Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches, apricots, plums, pears, or other ripe fresh fruit
- white sugar
- benzoate (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the fruit
2. Pit the fruit
3. Place in a jar alternating layers of fruit and white sugar until the jar is full
4. Seal tightly so no water enters
5. Place in a water bath for three hours over even heat
6. Lower heat and let cool
7. Optional: add a little benzoate to preserve longer
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Egg and Ham Omelette (Tortilla de Huevo con Jamón)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Eggs
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut into long, thin pieces a piece of ham, whether domestic or foreign.
2. Beat the eggs (white and yolk).
3. Season with salt.
4. Form the omelette and fry in hot butter.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Eggs Chilean Style (Huevos a la Chilena)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water
- Butter
- Flour
- Parsley
- Pepper powder

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs for fifteen minutes.
2. Remove them and place in cold water.
3. Peel them and cut them into whole slices.
4. Put a pan on the fire with a small teaspoon of butter.
5. Add a small teaspoon of flour, stirring constantly so it does not lump.
6. Chop a little parsley and add pepper powder.
7. Cover the egg slices with this mixture and serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Eggs on a Plate (Huevos al Plato)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Cooked ham slices
- Eggs
- Salt
- Cheese powder

## Directions

1. Grease a pan with butter and place in it a layer of cooked ham slices.
2. Break the eggs on top, ensuring they remain whole.
3. Add salt and cheese powder.
4. Put in a moderate oven.
5. When the whites are cooked, serve the eggs.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Eggs Oriental Style (Huevos a la Oriental)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Bread powder
- Cheese powder
- Eggs
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Nutmeg
- Butter

## Directions

1. Grease a pan with butter.
2. Place in it a layer of bread powder and cheese powder (half of each).
3. Break the eggs on top.
4. Add salt, pepper powder and nutmeg.
5. Add cheese powder and a tablespoon of butter.
6. Place in the oven or between two fires.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Eggs Viennese Style (Huevos a la Vienesa)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Bread
- Butter
- Lettuce leaves
- Parmesan cheese powder

## Directions

1. Fry the eggs in oil, ensuring they come out very whole.
2. Add salt and pepper powder to them.
3. Remove them with a skimmer with great care.
4. Place them on slices of bread browned in butter.
5. Arrange them on a platter on tender lettuce leaves.
6. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese powder on top.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ejotes a la Inglesa (Green Beans English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- salt to taste
- melted butter
- parsley, finely chopped
- lemon juice
- pepper powder

## Directions

1. Cook the green beans with salt to taste, making sure they stay green.
2. While still a little hot, toss with a little melted butter, very finely chopped parsley, lemon juice and pepper powder.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ejotes a la Saltadora (Sautéed Green Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- water
- salt
- half cup water
- half cup vinegar
- pepper powder
- two tablespoons oil
- thin onion slices
- peeled and seeded tomato slices

## Directions

1. Cook the green beans as before, taking care to boil for only a quarter hour so they do not turn black
2. Place on the platter
3. Mix half a cup of water and half a cup of vinegar, pepper powder, salt and two tablespoons of oil
4. Bathe the green beans with this mixture
5. Add very thin onion slices, peeled and seeded tomato slices
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ejotes en Ayahuaxte (Green Beans in Ayahuaxte Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- whole green beans
- salt
- bicarbonate
- pepitoria
- piece of cinnamon
- Castilian peppercorns
- piece of tortilla or tamalito soaked
- Chiapa peppercorns

## Directions

1. Cook the whole green beans with salt and a little bicarbonate
2. Toast some pepitoria, grind it with a piece of cinnamon and some Castilian peppercorns, with a piece of soaked tortilla or tamalito
3. If preferred, grind only some Chiapa peppercorns, always adding hot pepper to taste
4. Dissolve with a little of the same water in which the green beans were cooked
5. Add the green beans to this
6. If desired serve raw seasoned with salt, otherwise fry to eat it hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ejotes en Huevo (Green Beans in Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- whole green beans
- salt
- beaten egg
- hot lard
- cooked tomato sauce
- onion
- chile

## Directions

1. Cook the whole green beans with salt
2. Remove and dry with a clean cloth
3. Wrap in beaten egg
4. Fry in hot lard
5. Cover with a sauce made of cooked tomato, onion and chile, mashed and seasoned with salt
6. Add this sauce to the green beans
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ejotes en Recado Frito (Green Beans in Fried Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- salt
- tomato
- onion
- garlic clove
- one tablespoon ground breadcrumb powder
- Castilian peppercorns
- two cloves
- piece of cinnamon
- piece of achiote
- one cup hot water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop the green beans and cook as before
2. Fry tomato, onion and one garlic clove
3. Once fried, add the green beans
4. Add one tablespoon of ground breadcrumb powder with some Castilian peppercorns, two cloves, a piece of cinnamon and a piece of achiote
5. Add one cup of hot water
6. Just before serving add one teaspoon of vinegar
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ejotes Guisados (Stewed Green Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- water
- salt
- bicarbonate
- one tablespoon oil
- peeled tomato
- chopped onion
- piece of green chile
- sardines
- pepper powder

## Directions

1. Cook the green beans in boiling water with salt and a little bicarbonate
2. Fry peeled tomato, chopped onion and a piece of green chile in one tablespoon of oil
3. Add the green beans to this mixture
4. Place on the platter
5. Cover with sardines and pepper powder
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Empanadas (Stuffed Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of bread flour
- one pound of lard
- pound and a half of melted lard in which fine achiote is dissolved
- four ounces of sugar
- water
- six chayotes
- two pounds of potatoes
- ten or twelve large carrots
- green beans, chopped
- peas
- a little baking soda
- one pound of good dried fish or cod
- two ounces of lard
- six large tomatoes
- four onions
- salt
- four ounces of sugar
- six tablespoons of sweet bread powder ground with four cloves
- six Castilian peppers
- a small piece of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Moisten two pounds of bread flour and one of lard with pound and a half of melted lard in which fine achiote is dissolved, four ounces of sugar and a little water
2. Knead for half an hour so the dough remains quite soft
3. After being well kneaded, let it rest for three hours; if possible it is better to make it from the night before
4. Chop six chayotes, two pounds of potatoes, ten or twelve large carrots
5. Cook everything separately, with salt
6. Cook chopped green beans and peas, all that is green with a little baking soda, being careful they do not fall apart
7. Soak one pound of good dried fish or cod
8. Put a pan on the fire with two ounces of lard; chop six large tomatoes and four onions
9. Once this is fried, add it to the well-drained vegetables, salt, four ounces of sugar, six tablespoons of sweet bread powder ground with four cloves, six Castilian peppers and a small piece of cinnamon, add the fish well broken into small pieces
10. Once everything is well mixed, remove from the fire
11. From the wet dough make thirty parts, according to the size desired for the empanadas, stretch with the wooden pin and fill with the vegetables, fold, crimp the edge and place on baking sheets in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Encanelados (Cinnamon Candies)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of sugar
- four egg yolks
- half pound of well-ground almonds
- sugar
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Put half pound of sugar on the fire to reach thin-thread stage.
2. Add four egg yolks and half pound of well-ground almonds, stirring constantly.
3. When it pulls away from the pan, place on a bed of sugar and cinnamon powder.
4. Grind and form into pieces.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Enchilada a la Americana (Enchilada American Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four dried pasilla chiles, seeded and soaked
- Four Castilian peppercorns
- Four cloves of spice
- A small piece of cinnamon
- Water
- One tablespoon of lard
- One pound of beef sirloin, partially cooked and chopped
- Peeled tomato
- Onion
- Pepper powder
- Clove
- Cinnamon
- One teaspoon of vinegar
- Thin tortillas
- Grated hard cheese
- Sliced radish
- Tender lettuce, chopped
- Whole lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Grind four dried pasilla chiles, seeded and soaked, with four Castilian peppercorns, four cloves of spice, and a small piece of cinnamon.
2. Mix this with a little water.
3. Fry in one tablespoon of lard.
4. Chop one pound of partially cooked beef sirloin and fry it with peeled tomato and onion.
5. Season with pepper powder, clove, cinnamon, and one teaspoon of vinegar.
6. Fill thin tortillas with this mixture, roll them into tubes, and thoroughly coat with the first sauce.
7. Arrange on a platter to maintain their shape.
8. Cover with the remaining sauce.
9. Sprinkle with grated hard cheese, radish slices, and chopped tender lettuce.
10. Surround the platter with whole lettuce leaves.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Enchiladas con Picado (Enchiladas with Chopped Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tortillas
- Salt
- Pork meat, partially cooked and chopped
- One tablespoon of vinegar
- Pepper powder
- Clove

## Directions

1. Fry tortillas with salt if not made from masa as in previous recipes.
2. Once fried, remove them.
3. Chop a little partially cooked pork meat.
4. Half-fry it with one tablespoon of vinegar, pepper powder, and clove seasoning.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Enchiladas con Picado (Enchiladas with Minced Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tortillas
- Pork meat, partially cooked
- Vinegar, one tablespoon
- Pepper powder
- Clove powder
- Salt
- Tender pacaya buds in vinegar
- Olives
- Capers
- Hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Fry the tortillas with salt, or if not made from masa as in the previous recipes, fry them and remove them.
2. Mince some partially cooked pork meat; fry it halfway, adding one tablespoon of vinegar, pepper powder and clove, seasoned with salt.
3. Cover the enchiladas with this meat, add tender pacaya buds in vinegar, olives, capers and slices of hard-boiled egg.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Enchiladas con Picado (Other Enchiladas with Minced Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat, partially cooked
- Peeled tomato
- Chopped onion
- Pepper powder
- Half a teaspoon of vinegar
- Salt
- Chopped pickled vegetables
- Tortillas
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare a minced mixture of partially cooked pork meat, fry it halfway with peeled and chopped tomato and onion, add pepper powder and half a teaspoon of vinegar, season with salt, add chopped pickled vegetables.
2. Cover half of the tortilla with this, fold it to wrap it in egg as in the previous recipe, serving it with the same sauce.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Enchiladas con Salsa de Tomate (Enchiladas with Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Masa dough, well ground with salt
- Lard
- Pork meat, well fried
- Peeled tomato
- Chopped onion
- A little vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Three roasted tomatoes
- One guaque chile, fried
- Castilian peppercorns
- One teaspoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Grind the masa well with salt, form into tortillas neither thick nor thin.
2. Fry them in hot lard, remove when golden, and sprinkle lightly with water.
3. Prepare a mixture of well-fried pork meat with peeled tomato and chopped onion.
4. Season with a little vinegar, salt, and pepper powder.
5. Place the meat mixture on the tortilla to desired thickness.
6. Grind three roasted tomatoes with one fried guaque chile and some Castilian peppercorns.
7. Fry this sauce with salt and one teaspoon of vinegar.
8. Coat the enchiladas with this sauce and serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Enchiladas de Queso (Cheese Enchiladas)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tortillas with good skin
- Fresh cheese, in good quantity
- Salt
- Lard for frying
- Peeled tomato
- Chopped onion
- Pepper powder of Castile
- Capers
- Four to six chopped olives
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Take tortillas with good skin, remove a small piece and through that opening fill them with plenty of fresh cheese, sprinkle with salt and fry in hot lard.
2. Place them on the platter and in the same lard (if there is much, remove some, keeping a small tablespoon), fry the peeled tomato and chopped onion.
3. When well fried, add a little salt, Castile pepper powder, some capers, four or six chopped olives; cover the enchiladas with this, making sure the sauce is not too thick, and serve with a little chopped parsley.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Enchiladas Pollonas (Chicken Enchiladas)
body:
## Ingredients

- tortillas
- lard
- four pasilla chiles
- four mulato chiles
- four ancho chiles
- three roasted onions
- two ounces sesame seeds
- toasted French bread
- four roasted tomatoes
- ten capers
- chile in vinegar
- two cloves garlic
- one pound pork meat
- salt
- olives
- lettuce
- radishes

## Directions

1. Fry the tortillas in sufficient lard.
2. Grind four pasilla chiles, four mulato chiles, four ancho chiles, three roasted onions, two ounces of sesame seeds, toasted French bread, four roasted tomatoes, ten capers, four pieces of chile in vinegar and two cloves of garlic, along with toasted sesame seeds.
3. Cook one pound of pork meat with salt, then chop it finely.
4. Once the meat is fried, place it on the tortillas and cover with the sauce.
5. Top with chopped olives and garnish with fresh lettuce and radishes.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Encurtidos (Pickled Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small onions
- Water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Peppercorns
- Cloves
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Beet
- Carrot
- Green beans
- Pacaya
- Chayote
- Cauliflower
- Peas
- Tender green beans
- Green beans
- Cabbage
- Escarole
- Ginger
- Laurel powder
- Oregano
- Green chiles

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into a cross shape a few small onions
2. Place them in boiling water; the next day place them in the sun
3. Then put them in very good vinegar with salt, four peppercorns, three cloves, a sprig of thyme, a bay leaf and let them soak for one or two days
4. Cook beet in steam, chop carrots, green beans, pacaya, chayote, cauliflower, peas, tender green beans, peeled green beans
5. Cook all in water with salt so they do not fall apart, remove them and parboil chopped cabbage and tender escarole
6. When they have drained all the water, place them in a dish with half water and half vinegar, salt, a piece of crushed ginger and a sprig of thyme
7. After two days add one spoonful of bay leaf powder, oregano and peppercorns, add some whole green chiles or two
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Enrollado de Almendra (Almond Roll)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve ounces of sugar
- little water
- half a pound of almonds
- one egg white
- a little milk
- three egg whites
- two egg yolks
- two ounces of sugar
- four ounces of flour
- one full teaspoon of Royal baking powder
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Put the sugar on the fire with very little water
2. When it reaches hard crack stage, add the half pound of almonds ground with one egg white and a little milk
3. When it reaches the consistency of before, let it cool
4. Add two egg whites
5. For the dough, beat three egg whites until stiff peaks form
6. When well set, mix in the egg yolks with two ounces of sifted sugar, four ounces of flour and one full teaspoon of Royal
7. Once well mixed, put in a greased flat baking pan and bake in a hot oven for five minutes
8. As soon as it is cooked, cover the dough with the almond mixture, fold in two, cover with the rest of the almond mixture and put in the oven to brown
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada a la Jardinera (Garden Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrot
- Chayote
- Green beans
- Beet
- Potato
- Onion
- Lettuce leaves
- Escarole
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Oil
- Pepper powder

## Directions

1. Chop the carrot very finely, chayote, green beans, and beet
2. Cook each vegetable separately with salt
3. Chop the potato very finely; once cooked, place it in a round platter with tender lettuce leaves
4. Then place a slice of carrot, a slice of green bean that should be very green, a slice of potato and one of beet; if the platter is round, arrange them in circles; if it is long, place the rows lengthwise, making sure they are very well arranged with varied colors
5. The day before, cooked escarole pieces should have been placed in vinegar and salt; these whole pieces, without losing their shape, are placed in the middle of the colors
6. Prepare one cup half water and half vinegar, and three spoonfuls of oil with salt and pepper powder
7. Cover the salad with this dressing and serve as desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada Americana (American Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- vegetables
- three eggs
- three cooked egg yolks
- one raw egg yolk
- melted butter
- one cup of milk
- salt
- cream

## Directions

1. Chop the vegetables and cook separately so they come out green
2. Prepare a sauce as follows: cook three eggs, three cooked egg yolks and one raw yolk, break them up very well and gradually add melted butter
3. Mix very well, add one cup of milk, a little salt and mix in the vegetables which should be very well cooked
4. If there is cream, add one cup
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada Corona Real (Royal Crown Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- mayonnaise sauce
- green pepper slices
- carrot rounds
- green beans

## Directions

1. Wash a lettuce carefully so it does not fall apart
2. Cut the tip very straight so it can stand upright
3. Cover with mayonnaise sauce and decorate the whole with green pepper slices, round pieces of carrots and green beans
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada de Coliflor (Cauliflower Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Oil
- Onion
- Oregano

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflower in large pieces or very finely chopped, as desired
2. Once cooked, place it in the platter and bathe it with a dressing composed of half water and half vinegar, salt, two spoonfuls of oil
3. Decorate with onion slices blanched in boiling water and sprinkle with oregano powder
4. The same is done for beet salad and carrot salad
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada de Frijol Blanco (White Bean Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- White beans
- Salt
- Garlic head
- Water
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Pepper powder
- Onion

## Directions

1. Cook the white beans with salt and one garlic head
2. When well cooked, remove it and prepare a dressing with half water and half vinegar, salt, two large spoonfuls of oil, pepper powder and plenty of onion in very thin slices blanched in boiling water
3. Place the beans in this dressing, mix very well, cover it and let it soak in this infusion for two hours before serving immediately
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada de Güisquil (Chayote Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chayote
- Salt
- Baking soda
- Vinegar
- Water
- Oil
- Pepper powder
- Onion
- Oregano

## Directions

1. Peel the chayote, chop it into small cubes or in marquesotes, as desired
2. Cook it in water with salt and a little baking soda so it comes out green
3. Prepare in the salad bowl half a cup of vinegar and half water, salt, pepper powder and two spoonfuls of oil
4. Place the cooked chayote in this dressing
5. Place on top onions that have been partially cooked in water with salt and vinegar, halved and sprinkled with oregano powder
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada de Lechuga con Piña (Lettuce and Pineapple Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pineapple
- Water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Tomato
- Onion
- Lettuce
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Chop a piece of pineapple very well and place it in the salad bowl with half a cup of water and half vinegar, salt, pepper powder, peeled tomato and chopped onion
2. Place the lettuce, which should already be finely chopped, in this dressing
3. If desired, add a little sugar, according to the taste of the people
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada de Lechuga con Tomate (Lettuce and Tomato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lettuce
- Tomatoes
- Onion
- Hard-boiled egg
- Water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Oil

## Directions

1. Chop the lettuce as finely as possible
2. Peel large tomatoes, remove the seeds and cut them into slices
3. Place in a bowl a layer of lettuce, one of tomato, another of lettuce and another of onion; alternate the layers this way, making sure the last one is of tomato and onion
4. Add slices of hard-boiled egg
5. Moments before serving, bathe it with a dressing composed of half a cup of water and half vinegar, salt, pepper powder and two spoonfuls of oil; if this is added before, the lettuce discolors and will look very bad
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada de Papas con Cebolla (Potato and Onion Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Salt
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper powder
- Three or four onion heads

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes well and cook them with salt
2. Prepare in a bowl one cup of oil, one of vinegar, pepper powder and three or four onion heads sliced very thinly
3. Once the potatoes are cooked, peel them and slice them, trying to keep them as hot as possible
4. Mix everything well, cover it and let it rest a few minutes before serving immediately
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada de Papas con Mantequilla (Potato Salad with Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large potatoes
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Butter
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Wash very large potatoes, peel them and slice them
2. Prepare in a bowl half a cup of vinegar and one of oil, two large spoonfuls of melted butter and a small bunch of very finely chopped parsley
3. Place the well-drained potatoes in this dressing
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada de Repollo (Cabbage Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Salt
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper powder
- Lettuce leaves
- Onion
- Water

## Directions

1. Chop the cabbage very finely
2. Cook it with salt so that it does not fall apart
3. Prepare in a bowl a dressing of oil and vinegar with salt and pepper powder
4. Place the cabbage in this dressing
5. Decorate with green lettuce leaves and onion slices that have been blanched in boiling water
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada de Tomate (Tomato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes
- Onion
- Water
- Parsley
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper powder

## Directions

1. Peel large tomatoes and remove the seeds
2. Place in the salad bowl a layer of tomatoes and one of onion slices blanched in boiling water; alternate the layers this way, making sure the last one is of onion
3. Cover with chopped parsley and a little water, vinegar, two spoonfuls of oil, salt and pepper powder
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada Italiana (Italian Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- sliced peeled cucumber
- slices of cooked lobster
- slices of cooked potato
- beets
- cooked fresh fish
- salt
- capers
- peppercorns
- bell peppers
- mayonnaise
- peeled tomatoes without seeds

## Directions

1. Arrange layers in a salad bowl: sliced peeled cucumber, slices of cooked lobster, slices of cooked potato, a layer of beets, and a layer of cooked fish, all cooked with salt
2. If desired, add capers and peppercorns and bell peppers
3. Cover with a good mayonnaise
4. Garnish with slices of peeled tomato without seeds
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada Mexicana (Mexican Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- avocados
- tomato
- cream cheese
- small onions
- vinegar
- sweet bell pepper
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- lettuce

## Directions

1. Slice the avocados into rounds.
2. Slice tomato without seeds.
3. Cut cream cheese into strips.
4. Prepare small onions cooked in vinegar.
5. Peel and slice the bell pepper into rings.
6. Place everything in a salad bowl and dress with vinegar, oil, salt and pepper.
7. Garnish with lettuce.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada Rusa (Russian Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrot
- chayote
- green beans
- potatoes
- bicarbonate of soda
- peas
- tender beans
- salt
- chicken or tender hen
- vinegar
- oil
- pepper powder
- parsley
- one large tomato
- onion
- French mustard mayonnaise
- hard egg
- red bell peppers
- olives
- capers

## Directions

1. Chop carrot, chayote, green beans, potatoes in slices; cook everything separately, adding a little bicarbonate of soda to the greens
2. also cook peas and tender beans, all with salt, and likewise a clean chicken with salt
3. if there is no chicken, use a tender hen; when cooked, chop into very whole pieces
4. prepare on a platter one cup of vinegar, one of oil, pepper powder, chopped parsley, one large peeled and seeded tomato, one peeled and chopped onion
5. in this place the vegetables well mixed with the chicken, season with salt and cover with a mayonnaise made with French mustard
6. decorate with hard egg, cut lengthwise or in slices, long pieces of red bell peppers, olives and capers
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensalada Tartara (Tartar Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- green beans
- chayote
- cabbage
- carrots
- potatoes
- turnips
- one chicken
- salt
- pepper
- oil
- vinegar
- one glass of cream
- two egg yolks
- olives
- capers
- pickled foreign onions
- lettuce
- parsley
- sweet peppers

## Directions

1. Cook peas, green beans, chayote, cabbage, carrots, potatoes, turnips, and one chicken.
2. Season everything with salt, pepper, oil, and vinegar.
3. Beat one glass of cream with two egg yolks.
4. Cover the vegetables with this cream mixture.
5. Add olives, capers, pickled foreign onions, and chopped chicken.
6. Garnish with lettuce, parsley, and sweet peppers.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensoletado de Almendra (Almond Sunburst)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces sugar
- 4 ounces ground almonds
- 4 ounces melted butter
- ladyfingers (chiquiadores)
- sherry
- sweetened milk
- cream
- raisins and almonds or cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Clarify 12 ounces of sugar
2. When it reaches high point, add 4 ounces of ground almonds and 4 ounces of melted butter
3. Put to boil lightly
4. Put in a platter a layer of ladyfingers soaked in sherry and sweetened milk and a layer of cream
5. Alternate the layers trying to make the last one cream
6. Decorate with raisins and almonds or cinnamon powder
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ensoletado de Huevo (Egg Sunburst)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 4 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 0.5 cup milk
- ladyfingers (chiquiadores)
- honey
- dry sherry
- cream
- toasted and chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Clarify 1 pound of sugar and strain it
2. Return to the fire
3. When it reaches the point, lower the heat and let it cool
4. Then add 10 egg yolks
5. Return to the fire until it reaches the point of before (ante)
6. Add 4 ounces of peeled and ground almonds dissolved in 0.5 cup of milk
7. Cover the platter with ladyfingers soaked in honey and dry sherry
8. Put a layer of cream, then a layer of ladyfingers and alternate the layers
9. Try to make the last layer cream
10. Decorate with toasted and chopped almonds
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Entomatado de Carne (Tomato-Braised Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked meat
- lard
- tomatoes, peeled
- parsley, a little
- garlic, small cloves, roasted

## Directions

1. Quarter the cooked meat and lightly soften it
2. Fry in lard
3. Chop peeled tomatoes and add to the meat with a little parsley and some roasted small garlic cloves
4. When it has simmered, remove the parsley and garlic
5. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Escabeche de Carne (Pickled Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Lemon juice
- Ham
- Garlic
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Thyme
- Water
- Bay leaves
- Vinegar
- Ginger
- Pickles
- Oil
- Olives
- Capers
- Lettuce
- Radishes

## Directions

1. Rub a loin very well with salt, pepper powder, and a bit of lemon juice
2. Lard it with ham, garlic, pepper powder, cloves, and cinnamon
3. Cook with a sprig of thyme, salt, water, two bay leaves, vinegar, ginger, cloves, and pepper
4. When cooked, let it cool and slice
5. Add pickles, plenty of oil, vinegar, salt, pepper powder, olives, and capers
6. Garnish with lettuce and tender radishes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Escalapt de Papas (Potato Scallop)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- cheese
- milk
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the potatoes and slice them.
2. Butter a pan, put a layer of potato and a layer of cheese cut into pieces and small pieces of butter, alternating layers with the last one being potato.
3. Cover with equal parts water and milk, add salt to taste, and bake in the oven.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Escalopes (Loin Scaloppine)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- salt
- pepper powder
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- cooked peas
- pickles
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut the loin into slices, not too thin, and flatten with a mallet
2. Season with salt and pepper powder
3. Coat in beaten egg and then in bread crumbs
4. Place on a baking sheet in the oven
5. When golden, garnish with cooked peas
6. Serve with pickles and mushrooms
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Escalops de Marrano (Pork Escalops)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loin
- salt
- sweet bell pepper
- onion
- garlic
- vinegar
- avocados
- oil
- lettuce
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean the pork loin well and season with salt, sweet bell pepper, onion, garlic and vinegar, covering the meat with this mixture.
2. Place in a hot oven or over high heat to brown.
3. Once cooled, slice into rounds.
4. Grind some avocados and mix with oil, vinegar, salt and finely chopped lettuce.
5. Place the salad on the plate, top with the meat and garnish with potato slices.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Españoletas (Spanish Almond Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 4 ounces of sugar
- 8 egg whites
- half a pound of sifted flour
- dry sifted sugar for topping
- chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat eight yolks with four ounces of sugar
2. When very thick, mix with the eight egg whites that will be very well beaten as for nougat
3. Add half a pound of sifted flour, moving it very gently so it doesn't fall
4. Have papers coated with butter on tin sheets
5. Drop spoonfuls in desired size
6. Dust with dry sifted sugar and pieces of chopped almonds on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espinacas con Huevo (Spinach with Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- water
- salt
- three tablespoons of butter
- juice of one lemon
- pepper powder
- five hard boiled eggs
- three eggs for sauce
- egg yolks
- one tablespoon of melted butter
- one quarter part of milk
- one quarter part of cream

## Directions

1. Cook the spinach with water and salt, when well cooked remove the water until it drains well, chop very finely
2. Put three tablespoons of butter, one of lemon juice, salt and pepper powder to taste
3. Cook five minutes on low heat, empty into a platter and garnish with chopped hard boiled eggs and serve with the following sauce:
4. Cook three eggs, immediately remove the yolks, break them up very well with salt to taste, one tablespoon of melted butter, one quarter part of milk and one quarter part of cream
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espinazo en Adobo (Backbone in Adobo)
body:
## Ingredients

- split backbone
- salt
- some raw tomatillo
- garlic
- one cooked guaque chile
- oregano
- cumin
- Castilian pepper
- a small piece of achiote
- one soaked French roll

## Directions

1. Cook the split backbone with salt
2. Grind some raw tomatillo, better if small, with garlic, one cooked guaque chile, oregano, cumin, Castilian pepper, a small piece of achiote and one soaked French roll
3. Thin with the broth
4. Put a pan on the fire, remove all the broth in which the backbone was cooked and in this fry the recado, adding the meat pieces
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espinazo en Apazote (Backbone in Epazote Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- split backbone
- salt
- two cooked tomatoes
- peppers
- a small piece of achiote
- one dried chile
- fine cornmeal
- broth
- washed epazote branch

## Directions

1. Cook the split backbone with salt
2. Grind two cooked tomatoes with peppers, a small piece of achiote and a dried chile
3. Thicken with a little finely ground cornmeal and thin with a little broth
4. Add to the backbone with a branch of epazote washed in boiling water
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espinazo en Frijol Blanco (Backbone in White Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans
- water
- salt
- head of garlic
- split backbone
- three or four roasted tomatoes
- two onion heads
- four roasted garlic cloves
- one dried chile
- oregano
- cumin
- Castilian pepper
- a small piece of achiote
- one soaked French roll

## Directions

1. Cook the beans in cold water, and when they have boiled for two hours remove the water and add fresh clean water
2. Add salt and a head of garlic
