3. When more than cooked add the split backbone
4. Grind three or four roasted tomatoes, two onion heads, four roasted garlic cloves, one dried chile, oregano, cumin, Castilian pepper, a small piece of achiote and one soaked French roll
5. Thin with a little water or the same broth, stirring so it does not stick
6. Serve not too thin
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espinazo en Pepián (Backbone in Pepián Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- backbone
- salt
- one quarter ounce of pepitoria seeds
- one ounce sesame seeds
- some tomatillo
- two toasted French rolls
- Castilian pepper
- a small piece of cinnamon
- some green cilantro
- one guaque chile

## Directions

1. Cook the backbone with salt
2. Toast one quarter ounce of pepitoria and one ounce of sesame seeds, some tomatillo, about two toasted French rolls according to the amount of meat and grind all with Castilian pepper, a small piece of cinnamon, a little green cilantro, one guaque chile
3. Thin with the same broth
4. Fry and add the backbone
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espinazo en Pepián de Jocotenango (Backbone in Jocotenango Pepián)
body:
## Ingredients

- split backbone
- salt
- rice
- tomatillo
- green cilantro
- twenty Chiapa peppers
- same amount of Castilian pepper
- one toasted guaque chile
- one roasted onion

## Directions

1. Cook the split backbone with salt
2. Toast a little rice according to the amount of meat and some tomatillo
3. Once toasted, grind with some green cilantro, twenty Chiapa peppers and the same amount of Castilian pepper, one toasted guaque chile and one roasted onion
4. Grind the rice with water but very finely, thin with a little broth and add to the pot where the backbone is, making sure it is not too thin and not too thick
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espinazo en Pepián Dulce (Backbone in Sweet Pepián Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- half ounce pepitoria seeds
- half ounce sesame seeds
- some sweet bread
- two guaque chiles or dried chiles
- some tomatillo
- a large piece of cinnamon
- twelve cloves
- equal quantity of Castilian pepper
- a small piece of very burned banana peel
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Toast half an ounce of pepitoria, half ounce of sesame seeds, some sweet bread, two guaque or dried chiles and some tomatillo
2. Grind all with a large piece of cinnamon, twelve cloves, equal amount of Castilian pepper, a small piece of very burned banana peel
3. Thin with the same broth
4. Fry and add the backbone, seasoning with salt and sugar to taste
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espumillas (Puffed Candy)
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites
- sugar syrup
- pearl sugar (gragea)

## Directions

1. Beat exactly as the previous recipe, same quantity and everything
2. Moisten some sheet metal leaves and make the espumillas round or long as desired
3. Put pearl sugar on top and put in the oven for ten minutes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espárragos a la Milanesa (Asparagus Milanese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- one large tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon breadcrumb flour
- water
- salt
- nutmeg powder
- pepper

## Directions

1. Lightly fry the asparagus in one large tablespoon of butter, ensuring they remain very whole, and remove them and place on the platter
2. In the same butter fry one tablespoon of breadcrumb flour, add water, stirring so it does not become lumpy
3. Season with salt, nutmeg powder and pepper
4. Cover the asparagus with this sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espárragos a la Milanesa (Asparagus Milanese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- one large tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon bread flour
- water
- salt
- nutmeg powder
- pepper

## Directions

1. Lightly fry the asparagus in one large tablespoon of butter, ensuring they remain whole
2. Remove and place on a serving plate
3. In the same butter, fry one tablespoon of bread flour
4. Add water, stirring constantly to prevent lumps
5. Season with salt, nutmeg powder, and pepper
6. Cover the asparagus with this sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espárragos a la Parmesana (Asparagus Parmesan Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- asparagus
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Put a dish coated with butter, place a layer of asparagus and a layer of grated Parmesan cheese
2. Continue putting layers of both things, making sure the last one is cheese
3. Cook in the oven or over two fires
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espárragos en Mantequilla (Asparagus in Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons butter
- asparagus
- pepper powder

## Directions

1. Put three tablespoons of butter in a skillet
2. Add the asparagus, taking care not to break them
3. Serve hot, bathing them with the melted butter and pepper powder
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Espárragos Guisados (Stewed Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- one tablespoon oil or butter
- three garlic cloves
- two Castilian peppers
- two cloves
- a small piece of fried French bread
- water
- one tablespoon vinegar

## Directions

1. If fresh, cook the chopped asparagus with salt; if canned, simply chop it
2. Fry three garlic cloves in one tablespoon of oil or butter until golden, then remove
3. Add the asparagus
4. Grind the garlic with two Castilian peppers, two cloves, and a small piece of fried French bread
5. Dissolve this with half a cup of water and one tablespoon of vinegar
6. Add this mixture to the asparagus, let it come to a light boil, and serve
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Estofado (Braised Meat Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- rib pieces
- backbone
- brisket
- tomato
- onion
- garlic
- thyme
- clove
- pepper
- cinnamon
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- bread powder

## Directions

1. Cut pieces of rib, backbone and brisket
2. Place in a dish with plenty of diced tomato, onions cut in quarters, peeled garlic, a sprig of thyme, clove, pepper, cinnamon, equal parts water and vinegar to cover the meat and one tablespoon of salt
3. Leave overnight
4. The next day place on very low heat
5. If desired leave it juicy and sprinkle with bread powder; if not, serve it dry
6. This can also be made with all the spices ground
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Fiambre (Cold Meat and Vegetable Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- chayote
- cabbage
- carrot
- green beans
- buxnayes
- peas
- baking soda
- pacaya shoots
- vinegar
- small onions
- water
- saffron
- cooked beets
- chickens
- salt
- cured tongues
- sausages
- dried beef
- blood sausages
- eggs
- chicken broth
- ham
- fish
- cod
- shrimp
- olive oil
- pepper powder
- English mustard
- parsley
- creole mustard
- tomatoes
- black and red chorizo
- salami slices
- dry cheese
- fresh cheese
- red peppers
- olives
- capers
- salmon
- chives
- pickles
- canned lobsters
- green chiles
- lettuce leaves
- pacaya shoots
- cooked beets

## Directions

1. Chop chayote, cabbage, carrot, green beans, and buxnayes; cook each vegetable separately with salt
2. Add a little baking soda to the peas; very tender pacaya shoots are cooked without chopping
3. Do all this the day before
4. Two days before, put peeled small onions in vinegar, cut in a cross and blanched with boiling water
5. If desired, color some with saffron and others with a piece of cooked beet
6. Cook one or two chickens with salt according to the amount of fiambre
7. Cook one or two cured tongues in the same broth; add twenty or thirty sausages, two pounds of lean dried beef, about ten blood sausages, ten eggs
8. When all is well cooked, let cool
9. Then chop, making sure everything comes out very whole; slice the tongue, slice the egg or lengthwise, cut the chicken in small pieces or chunks of desired size
10. Strain the chicken broth well, cover and let cool
11. Cut ham from the leg, whether York or local variety, well cooked
12. Soak two pounds of very good fish, or if not, cod, in water overnight
13. The following day cook and chop it, removing all the bones
14. Peel with boiling water a pound of the largest shrimp available and cook with the fish
15. Prepare in a large platter a bottle of very good vinegar; it is better to use acetic acid or wine vinegar
16. Prepare a bottle of very clean cold water, the chicken broth, an ounce of salt, a bottle of the finest olive oil, half an ounce of pepper powder, two small jars of English mustard, a bundle of parsley and one of creole mustard with four or five ground onions, ten or twelve peeled tomatoes
17. Mix everything well, add all the vegetables stirring them with the chopped meats, setting aside separately the slices of black and red chorizo, egg and blood sausage
18. Arrange on a platter putting on top slices of salami, slices of chorizo, egg, pieces lengthwise of dry cheese, small pieces of fresh cheese, strips of red peppers, olives and capers, the onions in vinegar, its pieces of salmon, chives, pickles in mustard, canned lobsters and green chiles
19. Decorate the platter with tender lettuce leaves, pacaya shoots and designs of cooked beets
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Flan de Naranja (Orange Flan)
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 eggs
- juice of 4 sweet oranges
- 0.5 pound of sugar

## Directions

1. In nine eggs add the juice of four sweet oranges and half a pound of sugar
2. Beat for a quarter hour
3. Pour into a mold and let set for one hour until it sets
4. Place in a water bath, cover with a sheet and place hot coals on top to brown it, or place in a hot oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Flor de Ayote Rellena (Stuffed Squash Blossom)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked pork meat
- tomato
- onion
- breadcrumbs
- Castilian pepper
- vinegar
- salt
- egg
- hot lard
- fried tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Chop a little cooked pork meat
2. Fry with tomato and onion and add its dust of bread with Castilian pepper, a teaspoon of vinegar and salt
3. Fill the squash blossom with this mixture
4. Wrap in egg and fry in hot lard
5. Serve with fried tomato sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Francés en Leche (French Bread in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- water
- four tablespoons of yeast
- one glass of milk
- one teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Put one pound of flour with the necessary water to make it smooth, well mixed with four tablespoons of yeast
2. Let it sit all day
3. In the evening dissolve this with one glass of milk, one teaspoon of salt and the necessary flour to make a compact dough
4. Put the dough in molds and the next day put in a very hot oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Fresco de Cacao (Cacao Refreshment)
body:
## Ingredients

- One ounce of toasted cacao
- One ounce of soaked rice
- A quarter ounce of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Grind one ounce of toasted cacao, one ounce of soaked rice with a quarter ounce of cinnamon
2. Strain and sweeten to taste
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Fresco de Guanábana (Soursop Refreshment)
body:
## Ingredients

- One guanábana
- Sugar to taste
- Water

## Directions

1. Peel the guanábana
2. Put it in infusion with sugar water to taste for three or four hours
3. Then beat it well and serve
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Fresco de Piña (Pineapple Refreshment)
body:
## Ingredients

- One yellow pineapple
- Six to eight glasses of water
- A quarter ounce of Chiapa pepper powder
- One pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Chop one yellow pineapple very finely
2. Put it in a container with six to eight glasses of water and a quarter ounce of Chiapa pepper powder
3. Let it infuse with one pound of sugar for one night
4. The following day, if too acidic, add more water and sugar
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Fricasé de Pollo (Chicken Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of butter
- one teaspoon of flour
- one glass of water
- a little pepper powder
- a little nutmeg powder
- one lemon or a little vinegar
- tender mushrooms (if available)
- chicken quarters
- salt to taste
- three egg yolks
- a few drops of lemon juice or vinegar
- artichoke hearts, cooked (if no mushrooms)
- lemon

## Directions

1. Put two ounces of butter with one teaspoon of flour in a pan; as soon as it melts add one glass of water.
2. Add a little pepper powder, a little nutmeg powder, and one lemon or a little vinegar.
3. If there are tender mushrooms, add them.
4. Add the chicken quarters with salt to taste.
5. Let it boil for three quarters of an hour.
6. Just before serving, add three egg yolks well beaten with a few drops of lemon juice or vinegar.
7. If using mushrooms, add them when the chicken is half cooked; if not, use cooked artichoke hearts.
8. To keep the chicken meat white, brush it with lemon.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Blanco a la Leonesa (White Beans Leonese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- White beans, cooked
- Large white chicharrones
- Salt
- Butter
- Pickled vegetables, chopped
- Chorizo negro slices
- Cooked ham pieces
- Oregano powder
- Two roasted tomatoes
- Thyme sprig
- One small teaspoon vinegar
- Water

## Directions

1. Cook the beans as previously described
2. Cook separately some large white chicharrones with salt
3. Line a pan with butter and in it layer white beans, a layer of chicharrones mixed with chopped pickled vegetables, slices of black chorizo, pieces of cooked ham, and oregano powder, then another layer of white beans, taking care that this is the last
4. Grind two roasted tomatoes with a sprig of thyme, dissolve with one small teaspoon of vinegar and a little water seasoned with salt
5. Cover the beans with this, place on two fires so it comes to a boil
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Blanco con Bretones (White Beans with Breton Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans
- water
- salt
- tomato, peeled
- onion
- garlic clove, one
- Breton beans, pre-cooked
- achiote
- flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Boil the white beans and after boiling for one hour, drain the water
2. Add fresh boiling salted water
3. When the beans are well cooked, make a fried mixture of peeled tomato, onion, and a garlic clove
4. Add the beans with the pre-cooked Breton beans
5. Add a little achiote for color
6. Add half a teaspoon of flour dissolved in cold water
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Blanco con Bretones (White Beans with Breton Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans
- water
- salt
- peeled tomato
- onion
- garlic clove
- breton vegetables
- achiote (annatto)
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook the white beans and after boiling for one hour, drain the water and add fresh boiling water with salt.
2. When the beans are well cooked, make a fried mixture of peeled tomato, onion, and a garlic clove.
3. Add the beans and the breton vegetables that have been cooked beforehand to this mixture.
4. Add a bit of achiote for color and half a teaspoon of flour dissolved in cold water.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Blanco con Bretones (White Beans with Broccoli Rabe)
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans
- tomato
- onion
- achiote
- flour
- bretones (broccoli rabe)
- black chorizo
- ham

## Directions

1. Cook the white beans well
2. Fry them with a fried mixture made of chopped tomato and onion
3. Add a bit of achiote coloring
4. Add a bit of flour dissolved in water
5. Add the bretones that have been chopped and cooked beforehand
6. Add chopped black chorizo and pieces of ham
7. Let it boil a few times and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Blanco con Chicharrones (White Beans with Pork Rinds)
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans
- large white pork rinds
- salt
- butter
- pickled vegetables, chopped
- slices of black chorizo
- pieces of cooked ham
- oregano powder
- two roasted tomatoes
- a sprig of thyme
- one teaspoon of vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Cook the beans as previously described
2. Cook separately some large white pork rinds with salt
3. Grease a pan with butter and layer white beans in it
4. Add a layer of pork rinds mixed with chopped pickled vegetables, slices of black chorizo, pieces of cooked ham, and oregano powder
5. Add another layer of white beans, ensuring this is the last layer
6. Grind two roasted tomatoes with a sprig of thyme
7. Dissolve with one teaspoon of vinegar and a little water seasoned with salt
8. Cover the beans with this mixture
9. Place over two fires to bring to a boil
10. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Blanco en Recado de Adobo (White Beans in Adobo Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- White beans
- Two roasted tomatoes
- Garlic clove
- Oregano
- Roasted onion
- Achiote (optional for heat)
- French bread, soaked
- Water

## Directions

1. Cook the beans as previously described
2. Grind two roasted tomatoes with one garlic clove, a little oregano and a roasted onion, with a little heat if desired, a piece of achiote and a piece of soaked French bread
3. Dissolve everything with water
4. Add the beans when the mixture comes to a boil and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Blanco Guisado (Stewed White Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- White beans
- Cold water
- Boiling water
- Garlic head with salt
- One tablespoon lard
- Peeled tomato
- Chopped onion
- Two or three tablespoons grated white bread powder
- Castilian peppercorns

## Directions

1. Put the beans in cold water and after boiling for one hour on even heat, remove that water
2. Add boiling water with a head of garlic and salt
3. When cooked, remove the beans
4. Fry in one tablespoon of lard, peeled tomato and chopped onion
5. When fried, add a little of the water in which the beans cooked and two or three tablespoons of grated white bread powder mixed with some Castilian peppercorns
6. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Colado (Strained Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans, cooked with salt
- one head of garlic
- two spoonfuls of lard
- one onion, well chopped
- onion head and stalk

## Directions

1. Cook beans well with salt and one head of garlic
2. Remove and grind or mash with a masher
3. Strain with the same broth
4. Heat a pan over the fire with two spoonfuls of lard and one well-chopped onion (head and stalk)
5. As soon as it takes on color without burning, add the beans
6. Let it boil until the fat disappears
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Colado con Mantequilla (Strained Beans with Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- two tablespoons of butter
- one onion, chopped
- Parmesan cheese powder

## Directions

1. Soak the beans in water the night before
2. When well cooked, grind them very fine
3. Heat a pan with two tablespoons of butter and one chopped onion
4. Add the beans
5. If too dry, add another tablespoon of butter
6. Serve with Parmesan cheese powder around the plate
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol con Cecina (Beans with Dried Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of beans
- one pound of dried beef
- two spoonfuls of lard
- about twenty miltomates, raw and ground
- a few Castilian peppers
- a small piece of dough
- water

## Directions

1. Cook the beans
2. At the first boil add one pound of dried beef
3. When both things are cooked, add two spoonfuls of lard
4. Add about twenty raw and ground miltomates with a few Castilian peppers
5. Add a small piece of dough, dissolve with a little water
6. Let it boil for another quarter hour
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol con Chicharrones (Beans with Pork Rinds)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- some large pork rinds, preferably pine cone shaped and ears
- one tablespoon of flour
- water
- one branch of epazote
- salt
- alternatively, salted pig's feet prepared two or three days in advance

## Directions

1. Cook the beans as previously described
2. When half-cooked, add some large pork rinds, preferably pine cone shaped ones and ears
3. When everything is cooked, add one tablespoon of flour dissolved in a little water
4. Add one branch of epazote washed with boiling water
5. Season well with salt
6. Serve hot
7. Alternatively, pig's feet that have been salted two or three days in advance can be used instead of pork rinds
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol con Puntas de Güisquil (Beans with Chayote Tips)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- one bunch of chayote tips
- one tablespoon of lard
- one tablespoon of flour
- water

## Directions

1. Cook the beans as previously described
2. When the beans are more than cooked, take one bunch of chayote tips
3. Remove all the thin strings and peel them very well
4. Chop them and add to the beans with one tablespoon of lard
5. Dissolve one tablespoon of flour in a little water
6. Let it boil until cooked
7. Stir the beans so they burst and do not come out with the shell
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frijol Negro Volteado (Turned Over Black Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- black beans, cooked
- two ounces of lard or two large spoonfuls
- four or five tablespoons of the bean broth
- fried plantain slices
- washed butter or slices of fresh cheese

## Directions

1. After the beans are well cooked, grind them very fine
2. Heat a pan with two ounces of lard or two large spoonfuls
3. Put the beans in with about four or five tablespoons of the same broth
4. Cook on very low heat until dry
5. If necessary, add a bit more lard
6. When well fried, place on a plate, turning it so it falls whole
7. Serve with fried plantain slices, washed butter or slices of fresh cheese
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Fritura de Pollo y Guisantes (Chicken and Pea Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of flour
- half a tablespoon of Royal baking powder
- half a teaspoon of salt
- quarter teaspoon of paprika
- 2 eggs
- half a cup of milk
- 1 cup of cooked chicken cut into pieces
- 1 cup of cooked peas
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Sift all dry ingredients
2. Beat egg yolks until thick
3. Mix in the milk
4. Add the chicken and peas
5. Fold in the egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
6. Drop spoonfuls into hot fat in a skillet
7. Remove and drain on waxed paper
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Frituras de Papa (Potato Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of potatoes
- salt
- two and one half ounces of flour
- equal quantity of powdered sugar
- drops of lemon or orange juice

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of potatoes with salt
2. When cooked and cold, grind them and add two and one half ounces of flour, equal quantity of powdered sugar, a few drops of lemon or orange juice
3. Knead everything very well for ten minutes, roll out with a rolling pin, cut with small molds and fry in hot lard
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas (Crackers)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound flour
- three eggs
- four ounces butter
- two ounces lard
- four ounces sugar
- one tablespoon Royal baking powder
- one cup milk

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour, three eggs, four ounces of butter, two ounces of lard, four ounces of sugar, one tablespoon of Royal, and one cup of milk together well.
2. Roll out the dough several times with a rolling pin to regular thickness.
3. Cut out crackers and place in the oven.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas Americanas (American Crackers)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- three eggs
- four ounces of butter
- two ounces of lard
- four ounces of sugar
- one cup of milk
- one teaspoon of Royal baking powder

## Directions

1. Put together the flour, eggs, butter, lard, sugar, milk and Royal
2. If there is no milk, use six eggs instead of three
3. Work everything very well
4. Stretch out with a rolling pin
5. Cut the crackers in the shape desired
6. Put in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas de Avena (Oatmeal Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter, one tablespoon melted
- sugar, one cup
- eggs, two
- oatmeal, two and a half cups
- Royal baking powder, two teaspoons
- salt, half teaspoon
- vanilla, one teaspoon

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar with the butter, add the eggs one by one, beating well after each egg
2. Add the oatmeal, Royal, salt and vanilla
3. Mix everything well and drop by spoonfuls onto a greased baking dish, leaving them separated so they have room to spread
4. Bake in a moderate oven for ten minutes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas de Limón (Lemon Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds of flour
- one pound of ground and sifted sugar
- eight egg yolks and four egg whites
- six ounces of butter
- two cloves
- one ounce of cinnamon powder
- zest of one lemon
- one teaspoon of Royal baking powder
- chopped almonds
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Mix one and a half pounds of flour, one pound of ground and sifted sugar, eight egg yolks and four egg whites.
2. Add six ounces of butter, two cloves, one ounce of cinnamon powder, and lemon zest.
3. Work well with one teaspoon of Royal baking powder.
4. Roll out with a rolling pin and cut with a mold that has a little hole.
5. Place on greased baking sheets.
6. Sprinkle chopped almonds and granulated sugar on top and bake.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas Número Cinco (Cookie Number Five)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound of flour
- Four ounces of sugar
- Three eggs
- Quarter of a glass of milk
- Half teaspoon of Royal
- One quarter ounce of cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Mix everything well by spoonfuls
2. Place on buttered baking sheets
3. Put in the oven
4. If desired, place a raisin in the middle of each
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas Número Cuatro (Cookie Number Four)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound of flour
- Four ounces of sugar
- Two eggs
- One level teaspoon of Royal
- Half glass of milk
- A few grains of salt
- One tablespoon of butter

## Directions

1. Roll out the dough with a rolling pin
2. Cut cookies
3. Bake like the previous ones
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas Número Dos (Biscuits Number Two)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces of butter
- 3 eggs
- zest of 1 lemon
- 6 ounces of finely ground sugar
- 0.5 pound of bread flour

## Directions

1. Beat four ounces of butter until beaten
2. Add three eggs, the zest of a lemon, six ounces of finely ground sugar and half a pound of bread flour
3. Knead everything very well, roll out with the rolling pin
4. Cut the biscuits into small squares or whatever shape desired, place them on baking sheets
5. With a clean stick form little holes and place in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas Número Siete (Cookie Number Seven)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- Two ounces of butter
- One level teaspoon of salt
- One tablespoon of Royal
- One glass half water and half milk

## Directions

1. Mix everything very well
2. Cut cookies
3. Form small holes with a stick
4. Put in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas Número Tres (Biscuits Number Three)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound of flour
- 5 egg yolks
- 5 ounces of ground sugar
- 2 ounces of butter
- 3 ounces of lard
- 1 level teaspoon of Royal baking powder
- 0.5 glass of milk cooked with plenty of cinnamon
- 2 ounces of chopped and peeled almonds

## Directions

1. Put half a pound of flour on a table with five yolks, five ounces of ground sugar, two ounces of butter, three ounces of lard, one level teaspoon of Royal baking powder, half a glass of milk cooked with plenty of cinnamon, and two ounces of chopped and peeled almonds
2. Knead very well, roll out with the rolling pin, cut the biscuits and with a stick form the little holes
3. Then place in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas Número Tres (Cookie Number Three)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound of flour
- Five egg yolks
- Five ounces of ground sugar
- Two ounces of butter
- Three ounces of lard
- One level teaspoon of Royal
- Half glass of milk cooked with plenty of cinnamon
- Two ounces of chopped and peeled almonds

## Directions

1. Place flour on a board
2. Add egg yolks, ground sugar, butter, lard, Royal, cinnamon milk, and chopped almonds
3. Knead very well
4. Roll out with a rolling pin
5. Cut cookies
6. Form small holes with a stick
7. Put in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas Número Uno (Biscuits Number One)
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 ounces of flour
- 6 ounces of washed and melted butter
- 4 ounces of powdered sugar
- 1 ounce of cinnamon powder
- 0.5 bottle of cooked milk
- 2 eggs beaten as for sponge cake with one additional yolk

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well together
2. Let rest for two hours
3. Then drop by teaspoonfuls into a pastry machine that has been preheated with alcohol
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Galletas Saladas (Salted Crackers)
body:
## Ingredients

- one half pound of flour
- one half tablespoon of salt
- one tablespoon of butter
- one teaspoon of Royal baking powder
- one half glass of milk

## Directions

1. Mix everything well together
2. Stretch with a rolling pin, cut the crackers and make little holes in them with a stick
3. Place in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina a la Española (Spanish-Style Hen)
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen or chicken, not too cooked nor too tough
- Peas
- Carrot pieces
- Potato pieces
- Butter, oil or lard (sufficient)
- Chopped tomato
- Onion in very thin slices
- One can of diced pimientos morrones with all its juice
- Broth in which the hen or chicken was cooked
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cook a hen or chicken that is not too cooked nor too tough.
2. Parboil peas, carrot pieces, and potato pieces.
3. In sufficient butter, oil or lard, fry the chopped tomato, onion in very thin slices, and one can of diced pimientos morrones with all its juice; fry it very well.
4. Add the broth in which the hen or chicken was cooked and the quarters of it.
5. Let it simmer for half an hour well covered and add the vegetables with the chicken and serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina a la Francesa (Chicken French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- oil
- lard or butter
- ham
- tomato
- garlic
- parsley
- saffron water
- water
- pickled vegetables

## Directions

1. After cleaning the chicken well, brown it in oil, lard or butter
2. When it has taken on a golden color, remove it and chop ham very finely, tomato, peeled garlic and chopped parsley, and fry it in the oil left from the chicken
3. Add a little saffron water and water until the chicken is covered
4. When cooked, serve with pickled vegetables
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina a la Japonesa (Chicken Japanese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken, cut in pieces
- 1 tablespoon of butter or lard
- ham, chopped
- garlic cloves
- parsley, chopped
- water and salt
- 1 ounce of toasted almonds
- 1/2 ounce of ground almonds
- 1 tablespoon of toasted flour, lightly browned

## Directions

1. Place the chicken pieces in a pan with butter or lard.
2. Add chopped ham.
3. When half fried, add garlic cloves and chopped parsley.
4. Cover with water and salt.
5. Add toasted almonds and half ground, along with the toasted flour.
6. When cooked, serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina a la Pamela (Chicken Pamela)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken, well cleaned
- 2 garlic cloves, chopped
- onion, chopped
- butter or lard
- water and salt
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 drops of vinegar

## Directions

1. Heat butter or lard and brown the chopped garlic and onion.
2. Add the well-cleaned chicken, cover with water and salt, and let simmer until cooked.
3. Before serving, beat 3 egg yolks with 3 drops of vinegar.
4. Serve hot with the egg mixture.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina Caprichosa (Capricious Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- wooden sticks
- chicken breast
- chicken liver
- chicken gizzard
- beef tongue
- ham
- almonds
- salt
- pepper powder
- vinegar
- bay leaves
- thyme
- garlic head
- leeks
- onion
- butter

## Directions

1. After plucking and cleaning the chicken well, remove the skin with great care so it does not break
2. Place wooden sticks in the middle in a cross so it does not dry out
3. Cut into pieces the breast, liver, gizzard and all the meat of the chicken with small pieces of cooked beef tongue, pieces of ham cut lengthwise, some peeled and chopped almonds, and season with salt, pepper powder and a little vinegar
4. Fill the chicken with this mixture and remove the sticks
5. Sew it closed, giving it the desired shape, and wrap it tightly in a cloth so it keeps the shape given to it
6. Place in a serving dish covering it with water, bay leaves, thyme, a garlic head, a bunch of leeks and onion
7. When cooked, remove it; whether you want to serve it cold or hot, always press it; if serving hot, after pressing rub it with butter and place in the oven to brown
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina en Adobo a la Francesa (Chicken in French Adobo Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's foot
- salt
- 1/2 pound of chopped ham
- 1 chicken, well cleaned
- Castilian pepper powder
- clove
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook a calf's foot with salt until more than cooked.
2. Remove it and debone.
3. Place 1/2 pound of chopped ham in a pan, put the well-cleaned chicken in it, and let fry for about ten minutes.
4. Add the deboned calf's foot, Castilian pepper powder, clove, cinnamon, and salt.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina en Adobo a la Francesa (Chicken in French Adobo Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal leg
- salt
- half pound of chopped ham
- one chicken
- pepper powder
- cloves
- cinnamon
- garlic
- one glass of white wine
- two carrots
- six onions
- four ounces of lean ham cut into small pieces

## Directions

1. Cook a veal leg with salt until more than cooked, then remove it
2. Place half pound of chopped ham in a serving dish
3. Put the well-cleaned chicken in this and let it fry for about ten minutes
4. Add the deboned veal leg, pepper powder, cloves, cinnamon, salt, chopped garlic and one glass of white wine
5. Cover and let cook over very even heat
6. After one and a half hours of boiling, add two carrots cut in pieces, about six chopped onions and about four ounces of lean ham cut into small pieces
7. When serving, remove all the fat
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina en Arroz (Chicken with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- salt
- rice
- saffron water
- chayote (güisquil)
- carrot
- potatoes
- tomato
- garlic
- onion
- oregano powder

## Directions

1. Clean the chicken well and cook it with salt
2. When cooked, remove the chicken and in the broth add rice according to the number of people, well washed but not soaked as it will fall apart
3. Add a little saffron water, chayote, carrot, potatoes cut into small pieces, a fried mixture of peeled and chopped tomato, garlic clove and onion
4. Place over very gentle heat without stirring so it does not fall apart
5. Just before serving, add the chicken in pieces and oregano powder
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina en Fricasé (Chicken Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- chopped onion
- one cup milk
- one tablespoon flour or cornstarch
- nutmeg
- capers
- chicken or hen

## Directions

1. Fry the chicken or hen in butter with chopped onion
2. When it begins to brown, add one cup of milk
3. Add one tablespoon of flour or cornstarch
4. Add a little nutmeg and some capers
5. Cook until done
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina en Nogada (Chicken in Walnut Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- salt
- two red tomatoes
- one guaque chile
- two egg yolks
- chicken liver
- Castilian peppers
- cinnamon
- saffron
- achiote
- one francés soaked in vinegar
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken with salt
2. Roast two red tomatoes and grind them with one cooked guaque chile, two egg yolks and the well-cooked chicken liver with some Castilian peppers, a small piece of cinnamon, a little saffron, a small piece of achiote, and one francés soaked in vinegar
3. Dissolve all this with the same broth, fry it and add the pieces of chicken
4. When serving, put chopped parsley on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina en Pebre (Chicken in Pebre Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- butter
- salt
- lemon juice
- parsley
- pepper powder
- bay leaves
- oil
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. After cleaning the chicken well, place it on a spit and coat inside and out with melted butter and salt
2. Place near a very gentle fire
3. Prepare some melted lard or butter with lemon juice and bathe the chicken constantly with this, turning it at the same time
4. When it has taken on a medium color, place it in a serving dish with one tablespoon of butter, chopped parsley, pepper powder, salt and two bay leaves, the fat that has accumulated with garlic, one cup of oil and enough water to cover the chicken
5. Let it cook; when serving, remove all the spices and in the remaining broth, dissolve about two egg yolks which should give a light boil and with this cover the chicken
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina en Piña (Hen with Pineapple)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hen, cut into pieces
- butter or lard
- pineapple slices
- pepper powder
- clove
- cinnamon
- 1 pound of ham
- sausage
- pickles
- chopped almonds
- whole olives
- 1 glass of red wine
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Grease a dish with butter or lard
2. Place a layer of hen pieces of desired size
3. Add a layer of pineapple slices, removing the hard center
4. Add pepper powder, clove and cinnamon
5. Add 1 pound of ham, sausage, pickles, chopped almonds, whole olives and 1 glass of red wine
6. Cover with water and salt
7. Let it boil for two hours over very gentle heat
8. If making with cooked hen, add only the glass of red wine without water and let it boil for half an hour
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina en Salsa de Almendras (Chicken in Almond Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- twenty roasted almonds
- four well-cooked egg yolks
- three roasted and seeded pasilla chiles
- three cloves
- about eight Castilian peppers
- one small piece of cinnamon
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Grind the roasted almonds, well-cooked egg yolks, roasted and seeded pasilla chiles with three cloves, about eight Castilian peppers and a small piece of cinnamon
2. Dissolve with the broth in which the chicken was cooked, fry in oil and add one tablespoon of vinegar
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina Frita (Fried Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- pork loin
- cooked ham
- pepper powder
- oregano powder
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Debone the chicken as with the others
2. Chop a little pork loin with a piece of cooked ham cut into long strips, add pepper powder, oregano powder and one teaspoon of salt
3. Mix all this well with the meat and fill the chicken with it
4. Wrap it in cloth and cook with water, salt and plenty of oregano powder
5. After two hours, press it and serve cold
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina Frita (Fried Hen)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hen
- pork loin
- cooked ham cut in long strips
- pepper powder
- oregano powder
- salt

## Directions

1. Debone the hen as described previously
2. Chop a bit of pork loin with a piece of cooked ham cut in long strips
3. Add pepper powder, oregano powder, and a teaspoon of salt
4. Mix everything very well with the meat
5. Stuff the hen with this mixture
6. Wrap it and put it to cook with water, salt and plenty of oregano powder
7. After two hours, press it and serve cold
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina Mechada (Larded Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- ham
- chorizo
- olives
- garlic
- pepper powder
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- oil
- lard or butter
- tomatoes
- onion
- capers

## Directions

1. After cleaning the chicken well, lard it with ham, chorizo, olives, chopped garlic and pepper powder
2. Place in a serving dish and cover with water and one cup of vinegar with salt
3. Cook for two hours, remove and let it drain
4. Then fry in oil, lard or butter
5. When it has taken color, grind about three roasted tomatoes and one roasted onion head and dissolve with the broth in which the chicken was cooked
6. Just before serving, add olives and capers
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gallina Rellena (Stuffed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- ham
- meat
- onion
- mint leaves
- cilantro leaves
- two hard-boiled eggs
- pepper
- saffron
- salt
- lemon or sour orange juice
- two raw eggs
- butter
- bay leaves

## Directions

1. After cleaning the chicken well, debone it leaving all the meat
2. Chop a little ham and fry it with pieces of meat, onion cut lengthwise, about two mint leaves, some cilantro leaves, two chopped hard-boiled eggs, a little pepper ground with saffron
3. Remove from heat, season with salt, a little lemon or sour orange juice and add two raw eggs
4. Fill the chicken with this mixture, rub it with salt, butter, two bay leaves and chopped onion and place in the oven, taking care to baste it so it comes out juicy
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Garbanzo (Chickpeas in Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of chickpeas
- water
- sifted ash
- one pound of sugar clarified
- panela
- cinnamon strip

## Directions

1. Soak one pound of chickpeas overnight
2. The next day put a pot on the fire with one liter of water and one cup of well-sifted ash
3. Add the chickpeas
4. When the skin comes off lower the heat, after a quarter hour wash and return to the fire with one liter of cold water
5. When cooked remove
6. Have clarified one pound of sugar, if making with panela it should be quite white; clarify half a cup
7. When both are cold mix and put on the fire, which should be moderate
8. When the chickpeas begin to reach medium heat add a cinnamon strip
9. Do not forget that the honey and chickpeas must be cold, otherwise they turn out hard
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Garbanzos a la Española (Chickpeas Spanish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickpeas
- Beef tripe, clean and cooked with salt
- One tablespoon lard
- Four to six peeled and chopped tomatoes
- Large onion slices, very thin
- Pepper powder
- Chopped parsley
- Vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare the chickpeas as previously described
2. Have beef tripe cooked with salt
3. Fry in a pan with one tablespoon of lard the peeled and chopped tomatoes with slices of large onion, very thin
4. When everything is well fried, mix in the chickpeas and tripe cut into small pieces
5. Add pepper powder and chopped parsley, and if desired, a small teaspoon of vinegar
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gateau (Layered Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- half a pound of sifted sugar
- four egg yolks
- a small glass of coffee essence
- ladyfinger biscuits (chiqueadores)

## Directions

1. Beat the butter, mix in the sifted sugar
2. When it becomes cream, add the egg yolks and beat gently
3. Gradually add the coffee essence
4. In a dish, cover the bottom with ladyfingers
5. Add a layer of cream and another of ladyfingers, alternating
6. Ensure the last layer is ladyfingers
7. Leave in a cold place
8. The next day, place the dish in hot water to turn out easily
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gaznates (Fried Pastry Tubes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of flour
- juice of 2 sweet oranges
- 5 eggs
- a little milk
- a pinch of salt
- lard for frying
- marzipan, candied yam, or candied citron for filling
- honey beaten to stiff peaks
- colored tissue paper

## Directions

1. Moisten one pound of flour with the juice of two sweet oranges, five eggs, a little milk, and a pinch of salt
2. Knead very well and let rest for half an hour
3. Roll out with the rolling pin and cut strips the width of a finger
4. Wrap them around cardboard tubes not too thick to give them a curled shape and fry them in hot lard
5. When fried, carefully remove the tube and fill with marzipan, candied yam, or candied citron
6. Have honey beaten to a very stiff peak, frost with this and cover the holes with finely chopped colored tissue paper for a nice appearance
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gazpacho
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of French bread
- garlic
- lard
- cooked belly pork
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Fry very thin slices of French bread
2. Spread with ground garlic
3. Line a pan with lard and place a layer of French bread and a layer of thin strips of well-cooked belly pork alternately, making sure the last layer is French bread
4. Season with a bit of salt, pepper, vinegar, and oil
5. Place on very low heat
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gazpacho (Spanish Cold Soup Bread Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of French bread
- garlic
- lard or butter
- belly strips, well cooked
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper powder
- oil

## Directions

1. Fry very thin slices of French bread
2. Spread with ground garlic
3. Grease a skillet with lard or butter
4. Place a layer of French bread, then a layer of very thin strips of cooked belly, and so on successively, making sure the last layer is French bread
5. Bathe with a little thin vinegar, salt, pepper powder, and oil
6. Cover and place on very low heat or put in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gazpacho Catalán (Catalan Gazpacho)
body:
## Ingredients

- paprika
- ham in small pieces
- 15 cloves of garlic
- 3 roasted tomatoes
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- French bread

## Directions

1. Place paprika and ham pieces in a soup tureen
2. Grind 15 cloves of garlic and 3 roasted tomatoes together
3. Add water, vinegar, and salt to the ground mixture
4. Cut French bread into small pieces
5. Mix everything together and serve
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gelatina (Aspic Gelatin)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens, three
- pork feet, four, very white
- lemon juice, from two lemons
- fish glue, one sheet
- foreign gelatin, one tablespoon
- chicken pieces
- assorted vegetables, well cooked
- shrimp, well cleaned
- hard-boiled eggs
- asparagus tips

## Directions

1. Boil three chickens and four very white pork feet until they fall apart
2. Remove and let cool in a very cool place
3. The next day, remove all the fat and strain through a fine cloth
4. Place on the fire, add juice from two lemons, one sheet of fish glue, and one tablespoon of foreign gelatin
5. When well thickened, pour into a mold with small chicken pieces, all kinds of well-cooked vegetables, well-cleaned shrimp, hard-boiled egg pieces, and asparagus tips
6. Place the mold on ice until this jelly sets
7. Can be placed on fried eggs for a very nice presentation and exquisite flavor
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gelatina (Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- salt
- one bay leaf
- a few drops of lemon
- one packet of gelatin or two sheets of isinglass
- cooked chicken, cut into pieces
- vegetables, finely chopped and dried
- shrimp, well cleaned
- salted tongue, sliced

## Directions

1. Cook two chickens with salt and one bay leaf.
2. When well cooked, remove the chickens and reserve the broth.
3. The next day, skim the fat, bring to a boil with a few drops of lemon.
4. Add one packet of gelatin or two sheets of isinglass.
5. Prepare in a mold small pieces of cooked chicken, finely chopped and dried vegetables, well-cleaned shrimp, and sliced salted tongue.
6. Pour the aspic over everything and if urgent, place on ice to set.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gelatina (Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- salt
- bay leaf
- lemon juice
- gelatin packet or two isinglass leaves
- cooked chicken pieces
- finely minced and dried vegetables
- cleaned shrimp
- slices of salted tongue

## Directions

1. Cook two chickens with salt and a bay leaf until well cooked
2. Remove the chickens and save the broth
3. The next day, degrease the broth
4. Bring to a boil with a few drops of lemon juice
5. Add a packet of gelatin or two isinglass leaves
6. Prepare a mold with pieces of cooked chicken, finely minced and dried vegetables, well-cleaned shrimp, and slices of salted tongue
7. Pour the gelatin over these ingredients
8. If urgent, place on ice to set; otherwise, let set overnight
9. Alternative: the aspic can also be made with a piece of pig's head, a chicken, and a beef leg
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gelatina de Mango (Mango Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- mangoes
- one glass of mango juice
- one glass of sugar

## Directions

1. Scrape the mangoes and pass them through a strainer
2. Put one glass of mango juice and one of sugar together
3. Place over heat until it reaches the high stage
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Gelatina de Naranja (Orange Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- one glass of orange juice
- one glass of sugar

## Directions

1. Place over heat one glass of orange juice and one of sugar until it reaches the point
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Glacé para Budines (Glaze for Puddings)
body:
## Ingredients

- Powdered sugar (one and a half cups)
- Butter (half a tablespoon)
- Hot milk (two tablespoons)
- Vanilla (half a teaspoonful)

## Directions

1. Add butter to the hot milk.
2. Slowly add the powdered sugar.
3. Add the vanilla and, without letting it reach too high a point, remove from the heat and beat until it forms a cream.
4. This can be used to cover any pastry.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Granadillas (Passion Fruits)
body:
## Ingredients

- granadillas
- one and a half pounds of sugar clarified per ten granadillas
- coconut and pineapple paste
- fish roe
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Peel the granadillas and remove everything inside, making sure not a single seed remains
2. After washing very well, put them on the fire in boiling water, turning them on all sides so they blanch well
3. Remove and place in cold water
4. Have clarified one and a half pounds of sugar for ten granadillas
5. Put the granadillas in the syrup until they heat through
6. If desired serve in the syrup; if not, remove and when all the honey has drained, fill with coconut and pineapple paste or fish roe, sprinkling cinnamon powder on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Guacamole Mexicano (Mexican Guacamole)
body:
## Ingredients

- avocado
- tomato
- onion
- cilantro
- salt

## Directions

1. Grind the avocado.
2. Add finely chopped tomato, onion and cilantro.
3. Add salt last so it does not blacken.
4. Top with tomato slices, onion slices and chile for garnish.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Guiso de Carne (Meat Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- lemon
- pepper
- salt
- onion, chopped
- lard, a little
- wine

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into slices and place in a dish with lemon, pepper, and salt
2. Let sit for three hours
3. Add chopped onion, a little lard, and wine
4. Place in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Guiso de Carnero (Mutton Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- vinegar
- salt
- garlic
- paprika
- bay leaves
- oregano powder
- water
- tomatoes

## Directions

1. Take the meat well washed and place in a dish with vinegar, salt, crushed garlic, one tablespoon of paprika, bay leaves and oregano powder
2. Leave overnight
3. The next day add water to cover the meat and about three tomato halves
4. Let cook
5. When serving, strain the sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Güicoyes Rellenos (Stuffed Chayotes)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender chayotes
- white beans cooked
- small shrimp
- cumin
- achiote

## Directions

1. Cook the small chayotes and hollow them out
2. Have white beans cooked with small shrimp
3. Fry them and in their prepared sauce grind cumin and achiote
4. Fill the chayotes with this
5. If desired, grind a little bean to make a sauce and serve it
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Güisquil Guisado (Stewed Chayote)
body:
## Ingredients

- güisquil
- salt
- one teaspoon lard
- peeled tomato
- chopped onion

## Directions

1. Peel the güisquil, chop into small squares or marquesotitos, cook with salt
2. Fry in one teaspoon of lard, peeled tomato with chopped onion
3. Add the güisquil to this
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Güisquil Guisado (Stewed Chayote)
body:
## Ingredients

- Güisquil (chayote)
- Salt
- Manteca (lard)
- Tomato, peeled
- Onion, chopped
- Vinegar, half a teaspoon
- Bread powder
- Fresh cheese, abundant

## Directions

1. Peel the güisquil and chop it into small cubes or marquesote pieces.
2. Cook with salt.
3. Fry in a teaspoon of manteca, peeled tomato with chopped onion.
4. Add the güisquil with half a teaspoon of vinegar and bread powder.
5. When serving, cover with abundant fresh cheese.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Güisquiles Rellenos (Stuffed Chayotes)
body:
## Ingredients

- güisquiles
- salt
- one pound pork, partially cooked
- one tablespoon lard
- peeled tomato
- onion
- garlic clove
- one tablespoon vinegar
- one tablespoon sherry
- pepper powder
- clove or cinnamon
- three eggs
- bread flour
- flour
- lard
- peeled tomato
- chopped onion
- one teaspoon ground bread powder
- achiote
- Castilian peppercorns

## Directions

1. Peel the güisquiles and make slices neither thick nor thin
2. Cook with salt taking care they do not fall apart
3. Remove and let drain
4. For two güisquiles, chop one pound of partially cooked pork and fry in one tablespoon of lard with peeled tomato, onion and one chopped garlic clove
5. Add one tablespoon of vinegar, if available one tablespoon of sherry, salt, pepper powder, clove or cinnamon, a touch of sweetness to taste if desired
6. When fried, place one tablespoon of this on one slice of güisquil, cover with another slice
7. Beat three eggs, first the whites and then the yolks, add a little salt and bread flour
8. Dust the güisquil with flour so it does not peel, wrap the güisquiles in the egg and fry in hot lard
9. If desired serve alone, if not, with a sauce made of peeled tomato and chopped onion fried in a little lard, add one teaspoon of ground bread powder with a little achiote and some Castilian peppercorns
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Habas a la Napolitana (Fava Beans Neapolitan Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Green fava beans
- Salt
- Water
- Ham slices
- Chorizo slices
- Pork loin slices
- One tablespoon oil
- Peeled tomato
- Onion
- Green chile, chopped
- French bread
- Ten capers

## Directions

1. Peel the green fava beans, split them in half and cook in salted water, making sure the water is boiling when they are added
2. When cooked, remove them and in that same water cook ham, chorizo, and pork loin, all sliced
3. In one tablespoon of oil, fry peeled tomato, onion and chopped green chile
4. Grind a piece of fried French bread with about ten capers, dissolve with a little water and add to the fried preparation
5. Add the fava beans and all the meats that have been cooked
6. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Habas con Carne de Marrano (Fava Beans with Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fava beans
- Pork meat, cooked with salt
- Pork rib or loin
- Peeled tomato
- Chopped onion
- One tablespoon flour
- Pepper powder
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Soak the fava beans as previously described, taking care they come out very whole
2. Have pork meat (rib or loin) cooked with salt
3. Fry peeled tomato with chopped onion
4. Add the meat and fava beans to this and one tablespoon of flour dissolved in the same broth
5. Serve with pepper powder and chopped parsley
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Habas en Adobo (Fava Beans in Adobo Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Green fava beans, peeled
- Salt
- Two cooked tomatoes
- Garlic clove
- Oregano
- Small onion head, roasted
- Achiote, hot
- French bread, soaked
- One tablespoon lard
- Pork loin or rib, cooked (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the peeled green fava beans with salt
2. When cooked, remove them
3. Grind two cooked tomatoes, one garlic clove, a little oregano, a roasted small onion head, a hot piece of achiote, and a piece of soaked French bread
4. Dissolve this in the broth in which the fava beans cooked and fry in one tablespoon of lard
5. Add the cooked fava beans to this sauce; serve alone if desired, or with pieces of cooked pork loin or rib
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Habas Fritas (Fried Fava Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- Green fava beans, cooked and peeled
- One small teaspoon lard
- Tomato
- Onion, chopped
- One small teaspoon grated white bread
- A little vinegar

## Directions

1. After cooking and peeling the green fava beans, fry them in a pan with one small teaspoon of lard, tomato and chopped onion
2. Add the fava beans with one small teaspoon of grated white bread and a little vinegar
3. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Habas Secas Guisadas (Dried Fava Beans Stewed)
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried fava beans
- Water
- Salt
- One tablespoon lard
- Roasted tomato
- Castilian peppercorns
- Achiote
- Masa, finely ground
- Epazote
- Hot water

## Directions

1. Leave the fava beans in water overnight
2. The next day, peel them and, very well washed, put in cold salted water, taking care not to stir so they do not become hard
3. When they burst, add one tablespoon of lard
4. Grind a roasted tomato with some Castilian peppercorns, a piece of achiote and a piece of finely ground masa
5. Dissolve with a little water and add to the fava beans with a sprig of epazote washed with boiling water
6. Stir and leave neither very thick nor very thin
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Helados de Canela (Cinnamon Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ounces of cinnamon
- A quarter ounce of palo de la vida
- One liter of water
- Twelve ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. The day before, bring two ounces of cinnamon with a quarter ounce of palo de la vida to a boil in one liter of water
2. The following day, strain it with twelve ounces of sugar
3. Put in the ice cream maker and set like the others
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Helados de Crema (Cream Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter of cooked milk
- Ten fresh egg yolks
- Twelve ounces of sugar
- Two ounces of unsalted butter
- Two ounces of ground almond
- Half an ounce of vanilla
- Ice and two pounds of salt for the freezer

## Directions

1. Strain the cooked milk and put the ten egg yolks and twelve ounces of sugar into it
2. Add two ounces of unsalted butter
3. Strain and bring to a boil for ten minutes
4. Let cool
5. Add two ounces of ground almond and half an ounce of vanilla
6. Put in the ice cream maker covered with ice and two pounds of salt
7. Turn until the ice creams are well set
8. Serve plain if desired, or with a tablespoon of strawberry ice cream on top, according to taste
9. Alternatively, use only four egg yolks and half an ounce of cornstarch per liter of milk for a more economical version, though less flavorful
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Helados de Fresa (Strawberry Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of strawberries
- One pound of sugar
- Clear water

## Directions

1. Strain one pound of strawberries with one pound of sugar in very clear water, for fifteen ice creams
2. Put in the ice cream maker and set like the previous recipes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Helados de Leche (Milk Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter of milk
- Half an ounce of cinnamon
- Twelve ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. Bring one liter of milk to a boil with half an ounce of cinnamon and twelve ounces of sugar
2. When it has boiled, remove from heat and let cool
3. Strain and put in the ice cream maker to set like the previous recipe
4. If desired, serve with a tablespoon of cinnamon snow on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Helados de Mamey (Mamey Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of mamey
- One pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Grind one pound of mamey
2. Strain it with one pound of sugar
3. Put in the ice cream maker and set like the other ice creams
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Helados de Piña (Pineapple Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pineapple, preferably yellow
- One liter of water
- One pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Grind one pineapple, making sure it is yellow
2. Strain it with one liter of water and one pound of sugar
3. If too acidic, add more water
4. Put in the ice cream maker and set
5. The same is done with soursop ice cream, except beat it with the water very well and strain with the sugar like the previous ones
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Higos (Figs)
body:
## Ingredients

- figs
- water
- sifted ash or bicarbonate of soda
- one and a half pounds of sugar clarified per twenty-five figs
- high-point honey

## Directions

1. Put in cold water and peel with a piece of glass or on a grater
2. Make three cuts down the middle
3. Blanch in a little water with about six tablespoons of sifted ash, or if not, in water with a teaspoon of bicarbonate of soda
4. When blanched, remove and wash very well in two or three waters
5. For twenty-five figs clarify one and a half pounds of sugar
6. When cold and strained, add the figs
7. Put on the fire until they heat through
8. If desired serve in syrup; if not, remove from the honey and when dry, coat with high-point honey
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hilachas (Shredded Meat Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- cutaxte (a cut of meat)
- salt
- tomato, ground
- dried chile
- onion with stalk
- manteca (lard)
- water
- french bread or white bread
- cold tortillas
- achiote (annatto)
- cooked potatoes, sliced

## Directions

1. Put the cutaxte to cook; after four or five hours of boiling (add salt before cooking), it will be well done
2. Slice it lengthwise, making thin slices, and fry in very hot manteca
3. Grind together a little tomato, a dried chile, and an onion with its stalk, all cooked
4. Remove the hilachas and in the same manteca fry this mixture, thickening it with a little water, salt, and two tortillas soaked in cold water
5. If desired, add a bit of achiote coloring and sliced cooked potatoes with plenty of manteca
6. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hilachas Jocotecas (Shredded Meat Jocotec Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked miltomate (tomatillos)
- garlic clove
- oregano

## Directions

1. Make like the previous hilachas, with the only difference being that you grind only cooked miltomate, a garlic clove, and a little oregano, without grinding bread or tortilla into it
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hojuelas (Fried Pastry Wafers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of flour
- 5 eggs
- a little salt
- 1 teaspoon of butter
- butter for frying
- half water and half white honey for coating or half a pound of clarified sugar

## Directions

1. Make a well in one pound of flour
2. Place five eggs, a little salt, and one teaspoon of butter
3. If too stiff, add another egg
4. Knead for half an hour until the dough is very fine
5. Roll out with a rolling pin
6. Cut with a small mold in desired shapes
7. Fry in hot butter
8. Place on a platter with a coating of half water and half white honey, or if not, clarify half a pound of sugar; when at proper stage coat the wafers with this
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hongos (Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms, umbrella or button mushrooms
- oil
- onion
- garlic, one clove
- salt
- pepper powder
- broth or water, small quantity

## Directions

1. Take the mushrooms, whether umbrella or button mushrooms, wash them well
2. Heat oil in a pan on the fire
3. Fry onion and one clove of garlic in this oil, when it browns add the mushrooms with salt and pepper powder
4. Add broth or water in very small quantity
5. After ten minutes they can be served with stewed meat, chicken, kidneys, liver or alone
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hongos en Salsa (Mushrooms in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomato sauce
- garlic
- onion
- cumin
- salt
- cooked beef tongue
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Prepare a tomato sauce with plenty of garlic, onion, cumin, and salt
2. Fry with sliced cooked beef tongue and already fried mushrooms
3. Mix the sauce with the mushrooms and tongue
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Horchata de Canela (Cinnamon Horchata)
body:
## Ingredients

- Six ounces of cinnamon
- One ounce of palo de la vida
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. The day before, bring six ounces of cinnamon with one ounce of palo de la vida to a boil
2. Strain and add sugar to taste
3. This quantity yields twenty-five glasses of horchata
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Horchata de Leche (Milk Horchata)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of pepitoria, peeled like for marzipan
- Four ounces of peeled almond
- Four ounces of melon seed, soaked in hot water
- Sugar to taste
- Four liters of water
- Four liters of milk

## Directions

1. Peel half a pound of pepitoria like for marzipan and grind very fine
2. Grind four ounces of peeled almond
3. Grind four ounces of melon seed soaked in hot water
4. Strain with a little clean water
5. Add sugar to taste, four liters of water and four liters of milk
6. Alternatively, it can be made by grinding four ounces of soaked rice, half a pound of pepitoria and half an ounce of cinnamon, straining it only with sugar and water
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos a la Duquesa (Duchess Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of milk
- 4 ounces of flour or cornstarch
- 4 ounces of butter
- juice from a can of asparagus tips
- 4 ounces of grated Parmesan cheese
- eggs (two per person)

## Directions

1. Make a white sauce with the milk, flour or cornstarch, and butter
2. Put some sauce on a tray or platter
3. Place asparagus tips on top
4. Cover with the remaining sauce and Parmesan cheese
5. Place whole or split eggs in the sauce
6. Brown in the oven or over two fires
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos a la Duquesa (Eggs Duchess Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- milk
- four ounces butter
- four ounces bread flour or cornmeal
- canned asparagus tips
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Hard boil the eggs by boiling them for ten minutes, then place in cold water and peel.
2. Prepare a white sauce with one quarter glass of milk, four ounces of butter and four ounces of flour or cornmeal, adding the juice from a can of asparagus tips.
3. Place some white sauce in a baking dish, add the asparagus tips and cover with the remaining white sauce.
4. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and place in the oven to brown.
5. Arrange the whole or halved eggs around it.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos a la Orden (Eggs to Order)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Lard, one tablespoon
- Salt
- Lettuce leaves
- Ham slices, from York or domestic, well cooked
- Fried potato slices, preferably soufflé

## Directions

1. Fry or break the eggs in hot lard, either in an egg dish or in a small pan, put the pan on the fire with one tablespoon of lard.
2. When well hot, break the egg, making sure it stays very whole, add a little salt on the yolk and with a spoon baste with very hot lard until it is covered with a small white film that forms.
3. If fried in an egg dish, remove with a spoon, and if not, with a skimmer so they come out whole and well white, place them on the platter with lettuce leaves, slices of ham, whether from York or domestic and very well cooked, and slices of well fried potato, so much better if they are soufflés.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos a la Ranchera (Ranch-Style Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Lard
- Peeled tomato
- Onion
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. They can be made fried in lard, making sure they come out very white; fry a peeled and chopped tomato, with very thin slices of onion; season with salt and one tablespoon of water; cover the eggs with this to serve, serve them hot.
2. They can also be made by frying peeled and chopped tomato, with onion, add salt, one or two tablespoons of water, in this drop the eggs making sure the boiling is even, so they come out whole: serve to the taste of the person, if desired tender or well cooked.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos a la Vienesa (Viennese-Style Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Oil for frying
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Bread slices, browned in butter
- Tender lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Fry the eggs in oil, making sure they come out very whole; add salt and pepper powder, remove with a skimmer with great care, place them on slices of bread browned in lard, place them on a platter on leaves of tender lettuce and add pepper powder.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos al Gratén (Eggs au Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- onion, chopped
- tomato sauce, cooked
- mushrooms, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper powder
- parsley, chopped
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Hard-boil the eggs, peel them and cut in half
2. Heat butter in a pan with chopped onion, when it takes on the color of tea add a little cooked tomato sauce, finely chopped mushrooms, salt, pepper powder, chopped parsley and chopped egg yolks
3. Mix everything well and fill the egg whites with this mixture
4. Put a little butter and bread crumbs on top
5. Bake in the oven for five minutes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos Chimbos (Preserved Egg Yolks)
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty-four egg yolks
- Two pounds of very white sugar at medium point
- One egg white for clarifying
- A few drops of lemon
- One stick of cinnamon
- Raisins and chopped almonds for garnish
- Nougat

## Directions

1. Beat the twenty-four egg yolks until they are very stiff
2. Pour into a greased pan and place in the oven
3. Insert a stick; if it comes out clean, it is done
4. Pour onto a very clean cloth and leave covered until the next day
5. Remove the bottom skin
6. Cut into the desired shape, preferably large quarters made into sponge cake
7. Clarify two pounds of very white sugar at medium point with one egg white and a few drops of lemon
8. Boil the eggs in this syrup for half an hour, because if they boil longer they turn black
9. Just before removing them, add a stick of cinnamon
10. Coat with the syrup after removing the cinnamon and garnish with raisins and chopped almonds
11. If desired, let all the syrup drain very well and arrange in the platter in alternating layers of nougat and egg, finishing with nougat and covered with cinnamon powder
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos Espirituales (Spiritual Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- marquesote soaked in honey and muscatel wine
- one pound of sugar
- four egg yolks
- one half glass of milk or wine
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Place wet marquesote in honey and muscatel wine on a platter
2. Place one pound of sugar over heat until it reaches the soft-ball stage
3. Remove when cold and add four egg yolks beaten in one half glass of milk or wine
4. Place back over heat stirring constantly until it becomes hard
5. Place this on the marquesote with cinnamon powder on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos Húngaros (Hungarian Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread, sliced
- oil or butter
- ground tomato
- sweet pepper or dried pepper
- eggs
- bacon, pieces

## Directions

1. Fry slices of bread in oil or butter
2. In the same fat that remains fry ground tomato with sweet or dried pepper
3. Cover the bread with this sauce and place a fried egg on top with pieces of well-fried bacon
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos Pasados por Agua (Soft-Boiled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Put the eggs in a small pewter pot, cover with boiling water; if the vessel is on the fire, remove them after three minutes; if not, keep them five minutes in hot water.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos Poches (Poached Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Parmesan cheese, 4 ounces
- Butter, 4 ounces
- Heavy cream, 3 1/2 ounces
- Flour, 2 ounces
- Gruyere cheese, 1 ounce
- Salt, 1 ochavo ounce
- Pepper powder, 1/2 ochavo ounce
- Onion, 1 head
- Carrot, 1 head
- Celery, 1 branch
- Milk, 1 cup
- Thyme, 1 sprig
- Bay leaf
- Hierbabuena (mint)
- Eggs, according to the number of people
- Bread for canapés or sandwiches
- Vinegar
- Nutmeg
- Optional: bacon or goose liver

## Directions

1. Brown in 2 1/2 ounces of butter: the onion, celery and carrot, for five minutes.
2. Wet everything with one cup of milk, add a sprig of thyme, a bay leaf, onion, hierbabuena, salt and pepper powder; let it boil ten minutes, pass the sauce through a strainer, add the cream making sure it is sufficiently thick, add the grated Parmesan cheese.
3. Prepare square canapés of bread, sandwiches or cold bread from a long mold; brown them in a little butter.
4. Break the eggs in a little boiling water with a little vinegar; place one egg on each slice of bread.
5. Spread a dish with a little lard, put a layer of sauce, a layer of bread slices with the egg (when breaking the egg, add salt, pepper powder and nutmeg), a layer of bread and grated Gruyere cheese.
6. Put on the fire to boil five minutes. If desired, spread the fried bread before putting the egg on it with bacon or goose liver.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Huevos Revueltos (Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Lard, one tablespoon
- Chopped tomato
- Salt
- Pepper powder

## Directions
