1. Put a pan on the fire with one tablespoon of lard, chop a tomato and when it is half fried, break the eggs with salt and a little pepper powder and scramble with much lightness in the lard so they come out tender, cooked, but not hard.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado (Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- liver, one pound
- lard or oil
- onion, two ounces
- water, very little
- potatoes, optional
- carrot, chopped and cooked, optional
- peas, optional

## Directions

1. Wash the liver well, peel and cut into pieces
2. Heat lard or oil in a pan on the fire
3. Lightly fry the liver in this
4. For one pound of liver, have two ounces of onion parboiled with very little water, letting it boil just a few times so it comes out tender
5. If desired add potatoes, chopped and cooked carrot, and also peas
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado a la Italiana (Liver Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Liver
- Salt
- Black pepper powder
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the liver well
2. Cut into thin strips
3. Rub with salt, black pepper powder, and a little vinegar
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado a la Italiana (Liver Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- white wine
- vinegar
- oil
- onion
- tomato
- garlic
- pepper
- parsley
- salt

## Directions

1. Place the liver in water, peel and wash it very well.
2. Cut into small pieces and add sufficient white wine and a little good vinegar.
3. Heat a pan with oil, onion, tomato, garlic, pepper, parsley and salt.
4. Add the liver when the pan is hot, being careful not to let it boil much as it becomes tough.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado a la Italiana (Liver Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- white wine
- Castilian vinegar
- oil
- peeled tomatoes
- onion
- garlic
- parsley
- pepper powder

## Directions

1. Clean the liver well and cut it into pieces
2. Place in a bowl and dissolve it by adding white wine little by little to the desired consistency
3. Add a little Castilian vinegar
4. Prepare a pan with sufficient oil, peeled tomatoes, onion, garlic, parsley, and pepper powder
5. Fry this mixture and when done, add the liver
6. Let it boil for just a few minutes so it does not become tough
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado a la Italiana (Liver Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- salt
- pepper powder
- vinegar
- butter
- onion
- red bell peppers
- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Well wash the liver and cut it into thin strips
2. Rub it with salt, pepper powder and a little vinegar
3. Let it sit in this brine for half an hour
4. Place a pan on the fire with butter
5. Fry thinly sliced onion and as soon as it is fried, add the liver
6. Then add red bell peppers cut lengthwise
7. When it has taken on flavor, add one tablespoon of flour dissolved in half a cup of water
8. Let it boil for ten minutes
9. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado a la Milanesa (Liver Milanese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- salt
- pepper powder
- flour
- butter
- lettuce leaves
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Wash the liver and season it with salt and pepper powder
2. Coat it in flour and fry it in hot butter over very low heat
3. When golden, remove it and fry it on tender lettuce leaves and potatoes cooked plain
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado a la Tirolesa (Liver Tyrolean Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- two slices French bread
- butter
- onion
- garlic
- tomatoes
- parsley
- liver
- salt
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Fry two slices of French bread in butter until golden, then remove them
2. In the same butter fry chopped onion, one clove of garlic, three peeled chopped tomatoes and a little parsley
3. Grind the well-fried French bread and dissolve it with water
4. Let it boil
5. Have ready well-fried slices of liver with salt and some well-browned slices of potato
6. Place the liver on a platter and arrange the potatoes around it
7. Cover everything with the hot sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado con Apazote (Liver with Epazote)
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- salt
- Castilian peppercorns
- achiote
- chile
- masa
- butter
- apazote

## Directions

1. Put the liver to cook with salt, cut into pieces
2. Grind some Castilian peppercorns, a small piece of achiote, a small piece of chile and a little masa, as fine as possible
3. Dissolve this in the same broth in which the liver cooked and place it in a dish with a little butter
4. Add the liver with a branch of apazote that has been previously washed with boiling water
5. This can also be made by grinding only Chiapa pepper, without adding epazote or Castilian peppercorns
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado de Carnero en Revolcado (Lamb Liver in Red Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb liver
- Salt
- Miltomate
- Cooked tomato
- Guaque or dried chile
- Oregano
- Cumin
- Castilian pepper
- Achiote
- French bread

## Directions

1. Cook the liver with salt
2. Grind miltomate and cooked tomato with a guaque or dried chile, oregano, cumin, Castilian pepper, a small piece of achiote and a soaked piece of French bread
3. Dissolve this mixture in the same broth
4. Fry and add the liver cut into pieces
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado en Chanfaina (Liver in Chanfaina Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked liver
- cooked tomatillo
- chile
- hard cooked liver
- a small piece of cinnamon
- Castilian peppers
- a small piece of achiote
- a soaked French roll

## Directions

1. Cook the liver
2. Grind cooked tomatillo, chile, a piece of hard cooked liver, a small piece of cinnamon, some Castilian peppers, a small piece of achiote and a soaked French roll
3. Thin with the same broth
4. Fry and add the finely minced liver
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado en Chanfaina (Liver in Chanfaina Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- salt
- tomatillo
- guaque chile
- cumin
- Castilian peppercorns
- saffron
- cinnamon
- achiote
- French bread

## Directions

1. Cook the liver with salt
2. Grind some tomatillo with a guaque chile, a little cumin, six Castilian peppercorns, a little saffron, a small piece of cinnamon, a small piece of achiote, a piece of cooked liver and a piece of soaked French bread
3. Dissolve everything with a little of the broth in which the liver cooked
4. Fry everything and add the liver chopped into small pieces
5. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado en Recado (Liver in Recado Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb liver
- Salt
- Castilian pepper
- Dried chile
- Achiote
- Epazote

## Directions

1. Cook the liver with salt, cut into pieces
2. Grind a very fine paste with Castilian pepper, a dried chile and a small piece of achiote
3. Dissolve this in the same broth and add to the liver with a branch of epazote washed in hot water
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado en Recado (Liver with Recado Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- achiote
- Castilian pepper
- two roasted tomatoes
- chile
- lard
- epazote branch

## Directions

1. Grind a very fine paste with achiote, some Castilian peppers, two roasted tomatoes and chile
2. Put a pan on the fire with lard and a branch of epazote, then the recado and the liver cut into pieces
3. It can also be made grinding only Chiapa pepper, but without epazote or Castilian pepper
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado en Revolcado (Liver in Revolcado Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked tomato
- tomatillo
- garlic
- oregano
- peppercorns
- cumin
- chile
- achiote
- French bread
- liver

## Directions

1. Grind cooked tomato and tomatillo, one clove of garlic, a little oregano, some peppercorns, a little cumin, chile to taste, a small piece of achiote and a soaked French bread
2. Dissolve this in the broth in which the liver cooked
3. Fry it and add the liver in small pieces or in long slices
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado Frito (Fried Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb liver
- Salt
- Flour or bread powder
- Lard
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Take the hard part of the liver and slice it
2. Season with salt, coat with flour or bread powder
3. Fry in hot lard
4. Serve with potatoes cooked plain
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado Guisado (Braised Pork Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked pork liver
- tomato
- onion
- parsley
- garlic
- cooked ham
- pepper powder
- salt
- vinegar
- cooked potato slices

## Directions

1. Chop the cooked liver and fry with tomato, onion, parsley, chopped garlic, and cooked ham
2. Add pepper powder, salt, and a tablespoon of vinegar
3. Serve with slices of cooked potato
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado Guisado (Stewed Pig's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked pig's liver
- tomato
- onion
- parsley
- minced garlic
- cooked ham
- pepper powder
- salt
- one tablespoon vinegar
- cooked potato slices

## Directions

1. Mince the cooked liver and fry it with tomato, onion, parsley, minced garlic and cooked ham
2. Add pepper powder, salt, one tablespoon of vinegar
3. Serve with slices of cooked potato
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado Mechado (Larded Pork Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard part of pork liver
- ham
- garlic
- pepper powder
- parsley
- salt
- butter
- hot sauce

## Directions

1. Take the hard part of the liver and lard with ham, garlic, pepper powder, and chopped parsley
2. Rub with salt and butter and bake in the oven
3. Serve with hot sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado Mechado (Stuffed Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard part of liver
- ham
- garlic
- pepper powder
- minced parsley
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Take the hard part of the liver and stuff it with ham, garlic, pepper powder and minced parsley
2. Rub with salt and butter
3. Put in the oven
4. Serve with spicy sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado Relleno (Stuffed Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four ounces of pork meat
- Two ounces of beef
- Onion
- Tomato
- Chorizo
- Ham
- Hard-boiled egg
- Black pepper powder
- Cinnamon
- Clove
- Capers
- Pickled vegetables
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Lard or butter
- Liver

## Directions

1. Chop four ounces of pork meat and two ounces of beef that have been parboiled
2. Fry with onion and tomato
3. Add chorizo, parboiled ham, chopped hard-boiled egg, black pepper powder, cinnamon, clove, capers, pickled vegetables, wine, vinegar, and salt
4. Fry partially
5. Stuff the liver (whether lamb, pork, or beef) with this mixture
6. Sew with thick thread
7. Coat with lard or butter and salt
8. Bake in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígado Relleno (Stuffed Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces pork meat
- two ounces beef
- onion
- tomato
- chorizo
- ham
- hard-boiled egg
- pepper powder
- cinnamon
- clove
- capers
- pickled vegetables
- wine
- vinegar
- salt
- lard or butter
- liver (lamb, pork, or beef)

## Directions

1. Chop the pork and beef meat, which should be parboiled, and fry them with onion and tomato
2. Add chorizo, parboiled ham, chopped hard-boiled egg, pepper powder, cinnamon, clove, capers, pickled vegetables, a little wine, vinegar and salt
3. When half fried, stuff the liver with this mixture, whether it be lamb, pork or beef liver
4. Sew the liver with thick thread
5. Coat it with lard or butter with salt and place it in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígados en Revolcado (Liver in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked pork livers
- tomato
- miltomate
- garlic
- oregano
- chile

## Directions

1. Cook the livers and chop to desired size
2. Grind cooked tomato and miltomate with garlic, oregano, and chile
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Hígados en Revolcado (Liver in Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked livers
- cooked tomato
- cooked tomatillo
- garlic
- oregano
- chile
- a small piece of achiote
- one soaked French roll

## Directions

1. Cook the livers and mince them to desired size
2. Grind cooked tomato and tomatillo with garlic, oregano, chile, a small piece of achiote and a soaked French roll
3. Thin this mixture with the same broth
4. Fry it and add the liver to the sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ichintal en Adobo (Ichintal in Adobo Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- ichintal, well washed
- salt
- about two cooked tomatoes
- one head of roasted onion
- a little oregano
- one clove of garlic
- a small piece of achiote
- one dry cooked chile
- a piece of French bread, soaked
- one tablespoon of lard

## Directions

1. Peel the ichintal and chop into small pieces
2. Cook with salt
3. Grind about two cooked tomatoes, one roasted onion head, a little oregano, one clove of garlic, a small piece of achiote, one dry cooked chile, and a piece of soaked French bread
4. Dissolve with the same broth
5. Fry in one tablespoon of lard
6. Add the pieces of ichintal to this
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ichintal en Chirmol (Ichintal in Chirmol Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- ichintal
- salt
- two tomatoes, peeled
- slices of onion
- green or dry chile

## Directions

1. Peel the ichintal as in the previous recipe
2. Cook with salt along with two peeled tomatoes, slices of onion, and green or dry chile
3. When everything is cooked, mash the tomato
4. Leave the chile whole to eat as desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Ichintal en Huevo (Ichintal in Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- ichintal
- salt
- two eggs, beaten
- tomato, cooked
- one head of onion, peeled and scored
- one dry green chile
- lard

## Directions

1. Peel the ichintal and cook with salt
2. Remove and dry
3. Wrap in beaten egg (for ten slices of ichintal, beat two eggs)
4. Prepare a sauce of cooked tomato with one peeled onion head scored and one dry green chile
5. After wrapping the ichintal in beaten egg, fry in hot lard
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Jamón (Cured Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ham leg, as hot as possible after removal
- 1 ounce saltpeter
- 1 pound salt
- 2 to 3 pounds salt for the pan
- 8 days of smoke exposure
- water
- 2 hours boiling water
- 1 bottle red wine
- water to cover the leg
- 6 bay leaves
- sprigs of thyme
- 1 bunch parsley
- 1 bunch leeks
- 1 ounce Castilian pepper

## Directions

1. While the leg is still very hot (just removed), prick it with a fork and flatten it very well with a mallet
2. Mix 1 ounce of saltpeter with 1 pound of salt and rub the leg very hard with this mixture
3. Place it in a pan on 2 to 3 pounds of salt
4. The next day, rub it with the same salt and continue rubbing it daily with the same salt for twenty days
5. After twenty days, expose it to smoke for eight days
6. After this time, place it in a pan, cover with water and bring to a boil for two hours
7. Remove that water and place it back on the fire with 1 bottle of red wine and water until the leg is covered
8. Add about 6 bay leaves, sprigs of thyme, 1 bunch of parsley, 1 bunch of leeks, and 1 ounce of Castilian pepper
9. Let it boil for two hours, turning it over after one hour of boiling
10. Serve cold
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Jamón a la Ligera (Light Cured Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ham leg
- water
- 2 pounds salt
- 1 pound saltpeter
- juice of 12 sweet oranges
- juice of 10 sour oranges
- 1 ounce Castilian pepper
- 1/2 ounce cloves
- 2 pounds ice
- bay leaves
- parsley
- orange leaves

## Directions

1. Prick the leg with a fork as in the previous recipe and cover with water, 2 pounds of salt, 1 pound of saltpeter, the juice of 12 sweet oranges and 10 sour oranges, 1 ounce of Castilian pepper and 1/2 ounce of cloves
2. Keep it in this brine for 5 or 6 days, pressing it every night
3. The next day return it to the same brine with 2 pounds of ice
4. After this time, put it to cook in the same brine with bay leaves, parsley and orange leaves
5. After four hours, remove it, turning it over every hour
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Jamón a la Ligera (Lightly Prepared Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pig's leg
- salt
- saltpeter
- pepper powder
- juice of six sweet oranges
- six bay leaves
- one small bunch of thyme
- one bunch of leeks (if available)
- tomato
- one dried chile, seeded

## Directions

1. Take one pig's leg and for two days rub it thoroughly with salt and saltpeter.
2. On the third day, put it to cook with sufficient water, pepper powder, the juice of six sweet oranges, six bay leaves, one small bunch of thyme and, if available, one bunch of leeks.
3. After it is well cooked, if desired, place it in the oven.
4. Prepare a sauce by cooking or roasting sufficient tomato, grind it very well with one seeded dried chile, and after grinding well, fry it in oil or lard.
5. Serve the leg with this sauce.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Jocotes en Miel (Jocotes in Honey Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- jocotes (30)
- sugar (1 pound) or white panela (0.25 pound)

## Directions

1. Put a pot of water on the fire.
2. When boiling, add the jocotes that have been diced beforehand.
3. When they are partially cooked, transfer them to clarified honey or syrup.
4. For 30 jocotes, use 1 pound of sugar; if using panela, use one-quarter that amount, but it must be white.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche con Almendra (Almond Milk Dessert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 glasses milk
- 12 ounces white sugar
- 3 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- cinnamon powder for garnish
- chopped almonds for garnish

## Directions

1. Place milk, sugar, and ground almonds in a saucepan
2. Stir over very low heat until it reaches the consistency of a dessert
3. Decorate with cinnamon powder and chopped almonds
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche con Coco (Milk Dessert with Coconut)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound white sugar
- 4 glasses milk
- 1 coconut, well ground
- cinnamon strips
- 1 orange leaf
- 4 ounces peeled and ground almonds

## Directions

1. Place sugar, milk, ground coconut, cinnamon strips, orange leaf, and peeled and ground almonds in a saucepan
2. Peel almonds with boiling water
3. Place over heat until it reaches proper consistency
4. When serving, remove the cinnamon and orange leaf
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche con Durazno (Milk Dessert with Peaches)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- drops of lemon juice
- 8 very ripe peaches, peeled with small cuts on sides
- 1.5 glasses cooked milk
- 4 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 small glass sherry
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Place sugar over fire and clarify with drops of lemon juice
2. Return to fire and add eight very ripe peeled peaches with small cuts on sides
3. Leave until the syrup is very thick
4. Remove peaches and arrange well-drained on a platter
5. In a saucepan, place 1.5 glasses cooked milk with peeled and ground almonds and egg yolks
6. Place over fire and after boiling ten minutes, add the peach syrup
7. When it reaches proper consistency, add one small glass of sherry
8. Cover the peaches with this mixture, sprinkle with cinnamon powder, and place in the oven with heat on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche con Piña (Milk Dessert with Pineapple)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds sugar
- 6 glasses milk
- 6 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 1 pineapple, well ground

## Directions

1. Place sugar in a vessel with six glasses of milk and peeled and ground almonds
2. When it begins to thicken, add the well-ground pineapple
3. Let it reach proper consistency
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche con Piña (Milk with Pineapple)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds sugar
- 6 glasses milk
- 6 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 1 pineapple, well ground

## Directions

1. Put 1.5 pounds of sugar in a dish with 6 glasses of milk and 6 ounces of peeled and ground almonds
2. When it begins to set, add 1 well-ground pineapple
3. Let it reach the point of before (ante)
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche de Almendras (Almond Milk Dessert with Caramelized Top)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 glasses milk
- 0.5 pound sugar
- 2 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 ounces peeled and toasted almonds, finely chopped
- powdered sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Place milk, sugar, peeled and ground almonds, and egg yolks over fire
2. When it begins to thicken, add the peeled and toasted chopped almonds
3. When it reaches proper consistency, pour into a platter
4. Once cold, sprinkle with very dry powdered sugar
5. Using a very hot iron and cinch, caramelize the top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche de Mamey (Mamey Milk Dessert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 glasses milk
- 1 pound sugar
- 12 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 1 mamey, ground and strained
- almonds and raisins for garnish

## Directions

1. Place sugar, peeled and ground almonds, and ground and strained mamey in three glasses of milk
2. Place over heat until it reaches proper consistency
3. Decorate with almonds and raisins
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche Guisada (Stewed Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 glasses milk
- 2 ounces sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Put in a pot over fire 3 glasses of milk, 2 ounces of sugar, 3 egg yolks, 2 ounces of peeled and ground almonds, and 1 teaspoon of vanilla
2. Let it boil until it reaches the point
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche Imperial (Imperial Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 glass cooked milk
- 1 full teaspoon potato starch
- 2 ounces peeled and ground almonds

## Directions

1. Put 6 ounces of sugar in a pot with 5 egg yolks strained with 1 glass of cooked milk
2. Add 1 full teaspoon of potato starch and 2 ounces of peeled and ground almonds
3. Put on the fire to reach the point of before (ante)
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche Negra (Black Milk Dessert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds sugar
- 1 pound zapote, strained
- 2 ounces pitted raisins
- 1 small glass sweet sherry wine
- 12 ounces sugar
- 3 glasses milk
- 2 ounces unpeeled toasted almonds, ground
- 0.25 ounce cinnamon

## Directions

1. Clarify 1.5 pounds of sugar and place over heat until it reaches proper point
2. Add strained zapote with pitted raisins and one small glass of sweet sherry wine
3. Cook over low heat for half an hour
4. In another vessel, combine 12 ounces sugar, 3 glasses milk, ground unpeeled toasted almonds with cinnamon
5. Mix everything well and place over fire
6. When it has boiled for a quarter hour, mix in the zapote and bring to proper consistency
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche Nevada (Snowy Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 glass milk
- 1 glass cream
- 4 ounces sugar
- ladyfingers (chiquiadores)
- dragées

## Directions

1. Beat 1 glass of milk and 1 glass of cream very well for half an hour with 4 ounces of sugar
2. Put a layer of ladyfingers in a platter and a layer of this cream
3. Alternate the layers, trying to make the last one cream
4. Place dragées on top and serve
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche Quemada (Burnt Milk Dessert)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 glasses milk
- 1 ounce cornstarch or 1 ounce rice, soaked, well washed, ground and strained

## Directions

1. Place cornstarch or prepared rice in two glasses of milk
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Leche Quemada (Burnt Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 glasses milk
- 1 ounce cornstarch or 1 ounce soaked and ground rice
- 3 egg yolks
- cinnamon sticks or orange peel or 1/2 teaspoon vanilla
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put 1 ounce of cornstarch or 1 ounce of soaked, well-washed, ground and strained rice in 2 glasses of milk with 3 egg yolks
2. Put on the fire with some cinnamon sticks or an orange peel, or 1/2 teaspoon of vanilla
3. When it has boiled sufficiently, pour into a serving platter
4. Once cold, sprinkle with powdered sugar
5. With a hot iron or band, brown it
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lechón Adobado (Marinated Suckling Pig)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four to six pasilla chiles
- three or four roasted garlic cloves
- cumin
- oregano
- piece of French bread soaked in vinegar
- water
- suckling pig

## Directions

1. Soak four to six pasilla chiles and remove the seeds and grind them with three or four roasted garlic cloves, cumin, oregano and a piece of French bread soaked in vinegar
2. Dissolve everything with half a liter of water and place the piglet, already cleaned like the previous recipes, in this
3. Put on very low heat until it consumes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lechón al Horno (Roasted Suckling Pig)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two-month-old piglet
- Milk with bread (sweetened)
- Chopped parsley
- Sweet wine
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Pepper powder
- Butter
- Water
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Garlic head
- Parsley
- Mint
- Bundle of onions
- Leek (one, if available)
- Pork meat (two pounds for stuffing)
- Salt
- Green beans
- Carrots
- Potatoes
- Peas
- Onion
- Tomato
- Raisins (two ounces, pitted)
- Almonds (one ounce)
- Olives
- Capers
- Pitted prunes
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Clove powder
- Pepper
- Cinnamon
- Sherry wine (one small glass)

## Directions

1. Prepare a two-month-old piglet and feed it sweetened milk with bread for eight days.
2. Two days before killing, give it chopped parsley with sweet wine.
3. The day before, give it only water.
4. To kill the animal, slaughter it carefully without opening it much.
5. When it dies, be careful to sew up the wound.
6. Have boiling water ready and pour it on the animal.
7. When clean, prick it with a fork and rub it with salt, vinegar, and pepper powder inside and out.
8. If making it without stuffing: sew it closed and put it in the oven brushed with butter, with two cups of water, bay leaves, a sprig of thyme, a garlic head, parsley, mint, a bundle of onions, and puerro if available.
9. Put it in the oven and take it out every half hour to baste it with its juices.
10. If making it with stuffing: parboil two pounds of pork meat with salt; when cooked, mince it. Separately, parboil green beans, carrots, potatoes in small pieces, and peas.
11. Make a fried mixture with plenty of onion and tomato; add the meat, vegetables, about two ounces of pitted raisins, one ounce of almonds, olives, capers, pitted prunes, a little vinegar and sugar to taste, a little clove powder, pepper and cinnamon, and one small glass of sherry wine.
12. Stuff the piglet and prepare it as above.
13. The animal should be killed one day before.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lechón en Gratén (Suckling Pig au Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig
- two ounces of almonds
- two ounces of peeled hazelnuts
- Castilian pepper
- piece of nutmeg
- water
- thread
- cloth
- platter
- bottle of dry sherry
- two orange leaves
- sprig of thyme
- two bay leaves
- butter
- salad or sauce

## Directions

1. Clean it thoroughly like the previous recipe; debone it if desired, or leave it without deboning
2. Grind two ounces of almonds and two of peeled hazelnuts with Castilian pepper and a piece of nutmeg, wash the mortar with a little water, rub the piglet with this mixture and sew with thread
3. Place in a cloth very tightly wrapped, put it in a platter where it has plenty of room and cook with a bottle of dry sherry, two orange leaves, a sprig of thyme and two bay leaves
4. After two hours of simmering, remove it, rub with butter and put in the oven to brown
5. Serve with salad or sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lechón Relleno (Stuffed Suckling Pig)
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig no more than one month old
- milk
- salt
- corn
- water from rapadura
- potatoes
- soap
- lemon
- fork
- bay leaf powder
- black pepper powder
- melted butter
- stick
- vinegar
- cup of dry sherry
- four pounds of pork loin
- lard
- peeled tomato
- onion
- garlic cloves
- bread powder
- cinnamon
- clove
- black pepper
- chayote
- carrots
- green peas
- olives
- capers
- peeled almonds
- raisins
- lettuce
- Russian salad

## Directions

1. Choose a suckling pig no more than one month old and feed it for forty days only with sweetened milk, corn cooked with salt, water from rapadura and boiled potatoes
2. After this time, kill the piglet by cutting its throat, cover it with cloth and having a vessel with boiling water prepared beforehand, plunge the piglet in it and scrape with a dull knife or a piece of tin until it is very white
3. When well cleaned, wash with soap, open it down the middle and remove everything that is liver and tripe and wash with soap and lemon
4. Prick it with a fork and rub with salt, bay leaf powder, black pepper powder and melted butter inside and out
5. Put a stick in its mouth so it does not close and leave it in this brine overnight, adding a cup of vinegar and one of dry sherry
6. The next day, partially cook four pounds of pork loin
7. Once cooked, remove and chop very well
8. In a pan put a little lard, peeled tomato, onion and two well-peeled garlic cloves, add the meat with half a cup of good vinegar, a glass of dry sherry, bread powder ground with cinnamon, clove and black pepper
9. When half-fried, drain and add chayote, potatoes, carrots and green peas, chopped and well cooked with salt, olives, capers, peeled almonds and raisins
10. Well mixed, stuff the piglet with this, sew with thick thread and in a platter with the same brine put it in the oven, taking care to baste it with its juice every ten minutes
11. When well cooked, after about two hours, decorate with lettuce and serve with Russian salad
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lechón Relleno de Verdura (Stuffed Suckling Pig with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces almonds
- 2 ounces hazelnuts
- 2 ounces peeled walnuts
- Castilian peppers
- salt
- a little nutmeg
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- chayote
- string beans
- potatoes
- carrots
- tender peas
- lard
- peeled and chopped tomato
- onion
- 1 tablespoon butter
- breadcrumb powder
- pepper
- clove
- cinnamon
- cooked chicken pieces
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1 additional ounce ground almond

## Directions

1. Clean the suckling pig exactly as described in previous recipes
2. Grind together 2 ounces almonds, 2 ounces hazelnuts, 2 ounces peeled walnuts with Castilian peppers, salt, and a little nutmeg
3. Mix this with 2 tablespoons melted butter
4. Prick the suckling pig all over with a fork and rub it inside and out with this mixture, leaving it overnight
5. The next day, cut into very small pieces: chayote, string beans, potatoes, and carrots in sufficient quantity and cook with salt and tender peas
6. Once cooked, remove and place in a strainer to drain
7. Put a pan with lard on the fire and add plenty of peeled and chopped tomato with onion
8. Add the vegetables with 1 tablespoon butter, breadcrumb powder, pepper, clove, and cinnamon
9. Add pieces of cooked chicken and 1 tablespoon vinegar to the filling
10. Fill the suckling pig with this mixture and sew it closed with thick thread
11. Place in a baking dish well coated with butter, with 1 additional ounce ground almond, basting it constantly with its juices while cooking
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua a la Africana (Pork Tongue African Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tongue
- long strips of ham
- Castilian pepper powder
- salt
- olive oil
- bay leaves
- parsley
- two pimientos morrones
- water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue well and lard it with long strips of ham that go through to the other side
2. Arrange the ham strips neatly for good appearance
3. Rub the ham strips with Castilian pepper powder and salt
4. Fry the tongue in a tablespoon of oil and when golden add salt, two bay leaves, parsley, two diced pimientos morrones, a cup of water, and half a cup of vinegar
5. Let it cook until done and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua a la Cordobesa (Tongue Córdoba Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked tongue
- Lard or butter
- Peeled tomatoes
- Onion
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Water
- Vinegar
- Black pepper powder
- Capers
- Olives
- Cooked potatoes

## Directions

1. Place lard or butter in a pan
2. Chop two or three peeled tomatoes, onion, and one peeled garlic clove
3. Add finely chopped parsley
4. When fried, add a little water, one teaspoon of vinegar, and black pepper powder
5. Add the sliced tongue to this mixture
6. Add capers, chopped olives, and slices of cooked potatoes
7. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua a la Jardinera (Tongue Gardener Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Green beans
- Carrot
- Chayote
- Peas
- Fava beans
- Potatoes
- Salt
- Baking soda
- Lard or butter
- Flour
- Cooked milk
- Black pepper powder
- Nutmeg
- Cooked tongue

## Directions

1. Prepare tender green beans, carrot, chayote, peas, fava beans, and potatoes, cut into small pieces as desired
2. Cook with salt, adding a little baking soda to keep the vegetables green
3. Drain when cooked
4. Heat some lard or butter in a pan and add one tablespoon of flour
5. Before it browns, add one cup of cooked milk, black pepper powder, and nutmeg
6. Add the cooked vegetables and bring to a light boil
7. Arrange the sliced tongue around the edge of a platter
8. Place the sauce in the center
9. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua a la Marinera (Sailor's Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked tongue
- butter or lard
- cooked potatoes
- salt
- tomato
- onion
- garlic
- parsley
- broth

## Directions

1. Once the tongue is well cooked, slice it.
2. Place it in a dish with butter or lard.
3. Make a layer of cooked potatoes salted and sliced.
4. Add a layer of tongue and a preparation made of peeled tomato, onion, garlic and chopped parsley with a little of the broth in which the tongue was cooked.
5. Add another layer.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua a la Marinera (Tongue Sailor Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked tongue
- Butter or lard
- Cooked potatoes
- Salt
- Peeled tomato
- Onion
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Broth from cooking the tongue
- Black pepper powder
- Olives
- Capers

## Directions

1. Slice the well-cooked tongue
2. Place in a serving dish with butter or lard
3. Layer with sliced cooked potatoes seasoned with salt
4. Make a sauce with peeled tomato, onion, garlic, and chopped parsley, adding some of the broth in which the tongue was cooked
5. Layer alternately with tongue and the sauce, ensuring the last layer is potatoes
6. Add black pepper powder, chopped olives, and chopped capers to the sauce
7. Place over two fires to bring to a boil two or three times
8. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua a la Mexicana (Pork Tongue Mexican Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tongue
- two dried chiles, cooked and pitted
- three roasted tomatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Fry the tongue as in the previous recipe
2. Grind two cooked dried chiles without seeds with three roasted tomatoes
3. Add to the tongue with a cup of water
4. Let it cook for two hours
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua a la Milanesa (Pork Tongue Milanese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked pork tongues
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- lard
- vegetables or fried potatoes

## Directions

1. After the tongues are well cooked, remove them and coat in beaten egg
2. Then coat in bread crumbs
3. Fry in hot lard
4. When golden, serve with vegetables or fried potatoes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua de Marrano a la Marinera (Pork Tongue in Mariner's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tongue
- one-quarter ounce of pepitoria
- one ounce of toasted and ground sesame
- pepper
- clove
- cinnamon
- small toasted tomatoes
- toasted chile
- lard
- bread
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue well and fry it whole
2. When it has browned, add one-quarter ounce of pepitoria and one ounce of toasted and ground sesame with pepper, clove, and a small piece of cinnamon
3. Add small toasted tomatoes with a toasted chile fried in lard and a small piece of bread
4. Dissolve all this in a cup of water and add to the tongue
5. Let it simmer for two hours
6. Serve in its hot sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua en Adobo (Tongue in Adobo)
body:
## Ingredients

- miltomate
- tomato
- guache chile
- lard
- cooked tongue
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook a little miltomate, tomato and soaked guache chile.
2. Fry it in a little lard and dissolve with water.
3. Add the well-cooked tongue, either in slices or whole, with potatoes.
4. Serve hot, or if for travel, serve cold.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua en Escabeche (Pickled Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned tongue
- Salt
- One quarter ounce of saltpeter
- Bay leaves
- Black pepper powder
- Nutmeg
- Thyme
- One cup of good quality vinegar
- Oil
- Vegetable salad greens

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue and cook with salt until well done
2. Coat with one quarter ounce of saltpeter
3. Place in a serving dish with two bay leaves, black pepper powder, a little nutmeg, a sprig of thyme, one teaspoon of salt, and one cup of good quality vinegar
4. Cover and leave out in the night air for three nights, turning it every day
5. On the fourth day, place under a press
6. Serve sliced with a vegetable salad prepared with plenty of oil and vinegar
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua en Estofado (Stewed Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue
- water
- vinegar
- onions
- tomatoes
- thyme
- bay leaves
- Castilian pepper
- cloves
- cinnamon
- pickles in vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue as in the previous recipes.
2. Place it in a dish with water, one cup of vinegar, four or five onions cut in cross, four peeled and halved tomatoes, a sprig of thyme, two bay leaves, eight or ten Castilian peppers, the same amount of cloves, a small piece of cinnamon and some pickles in vinegar.
3. Let it boil for five or six hours.
4. Remove all spices, let it brown and serve with a salad of your choice.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua en Naranja (Pork Tongue in Orange Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tongue
- juice of two sweet oranges
- vinegar
- clove
- pepper
- cinnamon
- bay leaf
- sprig of thyme
- tender green beans

## Directions

1. Fry the tongue and when it browns, add juice of two sweet oranges, a little vinegar, clove, pepper, a small piece of cinnamon, a bay leaf, and a sprig of thyme
2. When cooked, remove all the spices
3. Serve with tender cooked green beans
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua en Pepitoria (Tongue in Pepitoria Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pepitoria
- sesame
- chile
- pasa or guaque
- miltomate
- small peppers
- cinnamon
- cloves
- white bread
- lard
- cooked tongue
- egg

## Directions

1. Toast pepitoria, sesame, chile, pasa or guaque and a little miltomate.
2. Grind all this with small peppers, a small piece of cinnamon, two cloves and half a white bread.
3. Fry this in a little lard.
4. Once the tongue is cooked, make slices not very thin, dry them well and wrap them in beaten egg.
5. Fry in hot lard over low heat, ensuring they come out golden.
6. Leave them in the sauce to give a light boil and serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua en Piña (Pork Tongue in Pineapple Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tongues
- ground pineapple
- water
- vinegar
- pepper
- clove
- cinnamon
- salt
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. Fry the tongues and when they have browned, add ground pineapple with a cup of water
2. Add half a cup of vinegar, pepper, clove, cinnamon, salt, and a bay leaf
3. When cooked, remove all the spices
4. Serve either dry or in its juice
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua en Salsa de Tomate (Tongue in Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked tongue
- cooked tomato
- guache or pasa chile
- thyme
- lard
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Once the tongue is well cooked, slice it.
2. Grind a little cooked tomato with a guache or pasa chile and a sprig of thyme.
3. Fry it in a little lard and add the tongue slices.
4. Serve with a little vinegar.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua Fría (Cold Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned tongue
- Half ounce of salt
- One quarter ounce of saltpeter
- Castilian black pepper
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Nutmeg
- Ginger
- Onion
- Garlic
- Pork meat
- Brown bell peppers
- Linen cloth
- Water
- Puerros (leeks)
- Orange leaves
- Mustard sauce with oil, vinegar, and olives
- Dried chile and roasted tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue well and split it lengthwise as if to stuff it
2. Rub with half ounce of salt and one quarter ounce of saltpeter
3. Grind together a little Castilian pepper, two bay leaves, a sprig of thyme, a bit of nutmeg, and a bit of ginger
4. Rub the tongue again with this mixture
5. Place in a dish with very thinly sliced onion and three or four garlic cloves sliced lengthwise
6. Leave for three days in this mixture
7. Add thin slices of pork meat and strips of brown bell peppers to the same sauce and use to fill the tongue
8. Sew with thick thread, wrap well in linen, and cook with water, onions, garlic, leeks, two bay leaves, two orange leaves, and one tablespoon of salt for six hours over steady heat
9. Press when hot and serve the next day with a mustard sauce made with plenty of oil, vinegar, and olives, or with a dried chile and roasted tomato sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua Guisada (Braised Pork Tongue with Spices)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tongue
- two dried chiles, soaked and pitted
- roasted garlic clove
- six grains of Castilian pepper
- six cloves
- piece of bread, fried
- cinnamon
- water
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue well and brown in lard
2. When it browns, add two soaked and pitted dried chiles with a roasted garlic clove, six grains of Castilian pepper, six cloves, and a fried piece of bread with cinnamon, all well ground
3. Dissolve in a cup of water and add to the tongue
4. Let it simmer for two hours
5. When serving, remove all the spices; if desired, add whole peeled potatoes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua Guisada (Braised Pork Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tongue
- raw miltomate
- garlic clove
- cumin
- pepper
- dried chile
- water

## Directions

1. Fry the tongue and when it has browned, grind a little raw miltomate with a garlic clove, a little cumin, pepper, and a cooked dried chile
2. Dissolve in a cup of water and add to the tongue
3. Let it cook until done
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua Guisada (Stewed Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked tongue
- Lard or butter
- Peeled and chopped tomato
- Onion
- Garlic
- Pineapple
- Black pepper powder
- Butter
- Breadcrumb powder
- Pickled vegetables
- Water
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice the well-cooked tongue and fry it
2. When golden in color, remove and set aside
3. In the same lard, fry the peeled and chopped tomato, onion, garlic, finely chopped pineapple, and black pepper powder
4. Butter a casserole dish and layer alternately: tongue, fried sauce, breadcrumb powder, and pickled vegetables, ensuring the last layer is breadcrumbs
5. Pour a cup of equal parts water and vinegar over the layers
6. Place over two fires to bring to a boil
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua Mechada (Stuffed Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned tongue
- Long strips of ham
- Garlic
- Salt
- Black pepper powder
- Water
- Bay leaves
- Oregano powder
- Vinegar or sherry

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue well
2. Prepare ham strips with finely chopped garlic, salt, and black pepper powder
3. Stuff the tongue by inserting the ham strips so they come out the other side, keeping them even
4. Place the tongue in a pot with sufficient water, salt, two bay leaves, oregano powder, plenty of garlic, and a cup of vinegar or sherry (if using sherry, do not add oregano)
5. Cook until tender
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua Rellena (Stuffed Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned tongue
- Water
- Salt
- Thyme
- Oregano
- Black pepper
- Clove
- Garlic
- Cinnamon
- Half pound of pork meat
- Butter
- Tomatoes
- Onion
- Vinegar
- Sherry
- Cinnamon
- Clove
- Sweet optional
- Pitted olives
- Potatoes
- Almonds
- Green beans
- Chayote
- Carrot
- Green peas

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue and cook with sufficient water, salt, thyme, oregano, pepper, clove, garlic, and cinnamon
2. When well cooked, remove and cool
3. Split the tongue lengthwise
4. For the filling: parboil half a pound of pork meat, chop finely, and fry in a pan with one tablespoon of butter, two or three chopped tomatoes, onion, and garlic
5. Add two tablespoons of vinegar, two of sherry, black pepper powder, cinnamon, and clove; add a touch of sweetness if desired
6. Add pitted olives, potatoes, and sliced almonds
7. Mix in parboiled and chopped potatoes, green beans, chayote, carrot, and green peas
8. Fill the tongue with this mixture
9. Sew closed with thick thread, coat well with butter, and place in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua Salitrada (Salted and Saltpeter Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue
- saltpeter
- salt
- water
- bay leaves
- leeks
- parsley
- thyme
- orange leaves
- Castilian pepper

## Directions

1. Beat the tongue on a stone, place it over coals and when the skin inflates remove it and scrape the tongue with a knife until very clean.
2. Finish cleaning with a cloth without wetting it.
3. Prick it with a fork and rub the tongue vigorously with half an ounce of saltpeter and one ounce of salt.
4. Beat it with a mallet so it comes out very tender.
5. The next day rub it again with more salt.
6. Continue rubbing with salt for eight consecutive days, keeping it in a well-covered container.
7. After eight days, cook it with sufficient water, four or six bay leaves, a bunch of leeks, a bunch of parsley, a branch of thyme, four or six orange leaves and Castilian pepper powder.
8. When well cooked, which will be after six hours, remove it and serve cold with a salad of your choice, preferably potato salad.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lengua Salitrada a la Ligera (Light Salted Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- tongues
- water
- salt
- saltpeter
- bay leaves
- leeks
- parsley
- thyme
- orange leaves
- Castilian pepper
- wine

## Directions

1. Clean the tongues as in the previous recipe.
2. Place them in a container and cover with very clean water, four pounds of salt and two ounces of saltpeter.
3. Four to six tongues can be cured this way.
4. Keep them thus for three days, ensuring they are well covered and the container is left in the open air.
5. Add all the same ingredients as in the previous recipe but cook with the same water and one bottle of wine.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lentejas Guisadas (Stewed Lentils)
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- water
- salt
- tomato
- onion
- chorizo
- ham
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook the lentils in water with salt
2. Prepare a fried mixture of chopped tomato and onion
3. Fry the lentils with this mixture, adding chorizo and cooked ham and a bit of flour dissolved in water
4. Let it boil a few times and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Limones (Lemons in Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- large lemons with thick rind
- water
- salt
- bicarbonate of soda
- clarified honey
- cinnamon strips

## Directions

1. Peel the lemons with a piece of glass or a grater, taking care to remove only the fuzz and keeping them green
2. Cut in half and remove the juice and pith
3. Blanch in hot water with one tablespoon of salt and one teaspoon of bicarbonate of soda
4. When well blanched remove and place in cloth
5. Keep under the stream of water for six days
6. Remove and place to heat in clarified honey as with the others
7. Add cinnamon strips
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo a la Jardinera (Loin Gardener Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked loin
- tomato
- onion
- broth
- mixed vegetables, chopped and cooked
- ground white bread
- pepper
- achiote
- green chiles

## Directions

1. After cooking the loin, cut it as before and fry
2. Fry tomato and onion; when well fried, add some broth, the loin, and all kinds of chopped and cooked vegetables
3. Add ground white bread with pepper and a small piece of achiote
4. Add green chiles and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Adobado (Seasoned Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- one-quarter ounce of salt
- garlic clove
- broth from the cooked meat
- flour
- achiote
- parsley
- cooked potato slices

## Directions

1. Season the loin with one-quarter ounce of salt and a garlic clove
2. When well fried, add broth from the cooked meat
3. Mix a tablespoon of flour with achiote coloring
4. Place the loin with chopped parsley and cooked potato slices in the sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo de Don Julio (Don Julio's Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- a small loin
- salt
- pepper powder
- a little vinegar
- flour
- butter
- ripe tomato
- tender onion stalks
- garlic head
- parsley
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. After cleaning the loin well, rub it with salt and pepper powder mixed with a little vinegar, pricking it very well with a fork
2. Let it sit for an hour
3. Then coat it in flour and fry it in butter
4. When browned, place it in a saucepan
5. Cook some ripe tomato, a bunch of tender onion stalks, a garlic head, and a bunch of parsley; strain and cover the meat with this
6. Place on very low heat and serve hot, adorning it with lettuce leaves
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo de Marrano (Pork Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loin
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- two bay leaves
- water
- oil
- lard
- lettuce
- tomatoes
- two dried chiles, seeded

## Directions

1. Season the pork loin with salt, pepper, clove and two bay leaves.
2. The next day, add a glass of water to it and, with the pot well covered, cook it.
3. When well cooked, fry it in oil and lard.
4. When golden, place it on a platter, either whole if desired or in slices.
5. Garnish with lettuce.
6. Fry some well-chopped tomatoes and grind two seeded dried chiles.
7. Dissolve the chiles with a little of the broth in which the loin was cooked and cover the meat with this sauce to serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo de Marrano en Salsa (Pork Loin in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loin
- large tomatoes
- salt
- onion
- mint leaves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the pork loin on a grill, turning it until it is browned
2. Roast some large tomatoes, remove the skin, and mash them with a masher
3. Add salt, onion, and some chopped mint leaves to the tomatoes
4. Add vinegar if desired
5. Slice the pork loin and place it in the sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo de Res a la Francesa (French Beef Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- garlic, finely chopped
- salt
- butter
- potatoes, whole and peeled

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef loin and mix with finely chopped garlic seasoned with salt
2. Tie well with hemp twine
3. Place in a baking pan with a little butter, salt to taste, and whole peeled potatoes
4. Put in the oven
5. Take care to baste it with its juice
6. When cooked, place on a plate decorated with lettuce and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo en Adobo (Loin in Adobe Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin or very tender brisket
- Ground garlic
- Roasted tomatoes
- Clove
- Pepper
- A small piece of chile
- Water

## Directions

1. Make slices of loin or very tender brisket
2. Fry with ground garlic
3. Grind roasted tomatoes with clove, pepper, and a small piece of chile
4. Add to the meat with the necessary water to cook it over two fires
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo en Adobo (Loin in Adobe Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pieces of meat
- Salt
- Pepper
- Ground garlic
- Vinegar
- Lard
- Onion
- Ham
- Green chile
- Roasted and ground tomatoes
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Sour orange juice
- Flour
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut pieces of meat and season with salt, pepper, ground garlic, and a bit of vinegar
2. Let sit overnight
3. The next day, fry the slices or pieces in lard with onion, ham, and finely chopped green chile and roasted, ground tomatoes
4. Dissolve in water and add enough to cover the meat, along with two bay leaves, a sprig of thyme, the juice of a sour orange, and a tablespoon of flour browned in lard or butter
5. Cook over moderate heat for about two hours
6. Serve with potatoes cooked plain
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo en Harina (Loin in Flour Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin or tender meat
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- One teaspoon of butter or lard
- Tomatoes
- Onions
- Slices of chorizo
- Ham
- One sprig of thyme
- One bay leaf
- One tablespoon of cold flour

## Directions

1. Make slices of loin or tender meat and place in a pot with water, salt, pepper powder, and butter or lard
2. Grind raw tomatoes and onions and add to the meat
3. Add slices of chorizo, ham, a sprig of thyme, and a bay leaf
4. When it has reduced somewhat, add one tablespoon of cold flour
5. Place over two fires so it cooks very slowly, ensuring even heat
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo en Vino (Loin in Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- lomo (loin)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- sal (salt)
- manteca (lard)
- agua caliente (hot water)
- vino tinto (red wine)

## Directions

1. Clean and pound the loin
2. Keep in vinegar and salt overnight
3. The next day, fry in very hot lard
4. When golden, add a little hot water and after a moment the same quantity of vinegar
5. When consumed, add the same quantity of red wine
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Enrollado (Rolled Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin
- Garlic
- Oregano
- Onion
- One sprig of thyme
- Salt
- A little lemon juice
- Water
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Tenderize a loin well
2. Grind garlic, oregano, onion, and a sprig of thyme
3. Add salt and a little lemon juice
4. Coat the loin very well with this mixture
5. Roll up well, tying with twine
6. Put to cook with equal parts water and vinegar
7. When cold, cut into slices and serve with any salad
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Enrollado (Rolled Pork Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- a loin of pork
- salt
- lemon
- pepper
- strips of bacon
- black sausage
- capers
- chopped parsley
- onion
- powdered pepper

## Directions

1. Pound a pork loin and leave in brine with salt, lemon, and pepper
2. Spread on a table and cover with strips of bacon, crumbled black sausage, salt, capers, chopped parsley, onion, and powdered pepper
3. Roll up, wrap in cloth, and cook with salt and aromatic herbs
4. Once cooked, place in a press to serve cold, sliced
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Enrollado con Jamón (Rolled Loin with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin
- Ground garlic
- Pepper powder
- Clove
- Salt
- Ham
- Water
- Vinegar
- Onion
- Celery
- Butter
- Avocado
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Oregano powder
- Green chile
- Onion
- Oil
- Pickles
- Cheese

## Directions

1. Tenderize the loin and add ground garlic, pepper powder, clove, and salt
2. Place plenty of ham along the edge
3. Roll the loin up very well, tie it, and put to cook with water, vinegar, onion, celery, and butter
4. When cooked, serve with guacamole made as follows: chop the avocado very well, add salt, pepper powder, oregano powder, green chile, chopped onion, oil, and finely chopped pickles
5. Mix very well, add plenty of cheese on top, and serve
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Fingido (Mock Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- lomo de res (beef loin)
- marrano (pork)
- pimienta (pepper)
- jengibre (ginger)
- tomillo (thyme)
- yemas de huevo (egg yolks)
- rebanada de mantequilla (slice of butter)
- copita de vino tinto (small glass of red wine)
- mantequilla (butter)
- caldo (broth)
- mostaza (mustard)

## Directions

1. Grind equal quantities of beef loin and pork
2. Add small amounts of pepper and ginger, the same of thyme, egg yolks, a slice of butter and a small glass of red wine
3. Make small pieces and place in a buttered dish with red wine
4. Cook over fire with a little broth and serve with mustard
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Frito (Fried Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- tomatoes
- onion
- green chiles
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook the loin and fry it
2. When golden, remove the lard
3. Cook tomatoes with onion, green chiles and some potatoes if available, or just potatoes if not
4. Bathe the loin with this sauce and serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Guisado (Stewed Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin
- Ham
- Garlic cloves
- Pepper powder
- Butter or oil
- Water
- Vinegar
- French bread
- Tomato
- Onion
- Pulque or vinegar

## Directions

1. Lard the loin with ham, garlic cloves, and pepper powder
2. Brown in butter or oil
3. When it takes on color, add water and vinegar to cover the meat
4. Fry a piece of French bread, tomato, and onion; grind these and dissolve in a little pulque (or use vinegar if pulque is not available)
5. Bring to a boil with the meat
6. Serve as preferred, either in slices or whole
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Guisado (Stewed Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- lomo (loin)
- manteca o mantequilla (lard or butter)
- ajo (garlic)
- ramita de tomillo (sprig of thyme)
- hoja de laurel (bay leaf)
- agua (water)
- sal (salt)

## Directions

1. Make some cuts in the loin that go through to the other side
2. Fry in lard or butter
3. When golden, add a garlic clove, a sprig of thyme, a ground bay leaf and dissolve with enough water to cover the meat
4. Season with salt and let reduce
5. Serve dry if desired or in its own juices
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Marinado (Marinated Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- vinegar
- one head of garlic
- oregano powder
- pepper powder
- clove
- two bay leaves
- salt
- one glass of water
- oil
- lard
- lettuce
- tomatoes
- two dried chiles

## Directions

1. The day before, clean the loin very well and place in a pot covered with vinegar, one peeled and crushed head of garlic, a little oregano powder, pepper powder, clove, two bay leaves and salt to taste
2. The next day add one glass of water and with the pot well covered, put to cook
3. When well cooked, fry in oil and lard
4. When golden, place on a platter, whole if desired or in slices, garnish with lettuce
5. Fry some finely chopped tomatoes, grind two seeded dried chiles, dissolve with a little broth in which the loin was cooked and bathe with this to serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Mechado (Larded Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Strips of ham
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Butter or margarine for browning
- Pepper
- Cinnamon
- Dried chile pepper
- Oregano
- Thyme
- One bay leaf
- Water
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare strips of ham with salt and pepper powder
2. Lard the loin with these strips and place in a pot to brown in butter or margarine
3. Once it has taken on a golden color, grind a little pepper, cinnamon, dried chile pepper, oregano, thyme, and a bay leaf
4. Toast all spices and dissolve in a little water and vinegar
5. Add to the meat
6. Leave overnight and the next day put to cook over two fires
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Migado (Breadcrumb Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- salt
- butter
- bread crumbs
- potato puree

## Directions

1. Rub the loin with salt and butter
2. Coat with bread crumbs and bake in the oven
3. Baste with its own juice to prevent it from drying out
4. Serve with mashed potatoes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Relleno (Stuffed Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin
- salt
- French mustard
- chayote
- green beans
- carrot
- potato
- peas
- tomato
- onion
- ground white bread
- pepper
- clove
- vinegar
- olive oil
- bay leaves
- thyme

## Directions

1. Open a loin down the middle and rub lightly with salt and French mustard
2. Chop chayote, green beans, carrot, and potato; cook with tender peas
3. Fry everything with tomato and onion
4. When half-fried, add ground white bread with pepper, clove, and a tablespoon of good vinegar
5. Fill the loin with this mixture, then rub with salt and a small glass of oil
6. Place in a dish with half a cup of vinegar, two onion heads, two bay leaves, and a sprig of thyme
7. Bake in the oven, basting frequently
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Relleno (Stuffed Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Pork
- Chorizo
- Ham
- Capers
- Fried seasoning mixture
- Water
- Red wine

## Directions

1. Mince beef and pork, plenty of crumbled chorizo, and minced ham
2. Mix in capers and fried seasoning
3. Fill the loin with this mixture
4. Tie very well with twine
5. Place in a pot with equal parts water and red wine
6. Once cooked, serve with lettuce and tender radishes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Relleno (Stuffed Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork loin, parboiled and chopped
- Lard or butter
- Garlic
- Onion
- Green chile, chopped
- Tomato
- Chorizo
- Ham
- Chopped parsley
- Almonds
- Capers
- Hard-boiled egg
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Wine
- Sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Fry the chopped parboiled pork loin in lard or butter with garlic, onion, chopped green chile, and tomato
2. Add to this mixture the chorizo, ham, chopped parsley, almonds, capers, hard-boiled egg, a little oil, vinegar, and a little wine
3. Add a bit of sugar if desired, otherwise only acid
4. Butterfly the loin and fill it with this mixture, sewing it with long stitches
5. Cook with water, a glass of red wine or sherry, and a tablespoon of butter
6. When well cooked, serve with a sauce made as follows: fry in oil or butter garlic, chopped green chile, roasted tomato, and ground capers; when fried, add pepper powder, salt, a small piece of French bread fried in lard, a bit of vinegar, and water so that the sauce comes out thin
7. Serve cold or hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Lomo Relleno con Pickles (Loin Stuffed with Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Loin
- Pickle mustard
- Pickles
- Bell peppers
- Water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Bay leaf
- Tomato
- Onion

## Directions

1. Butterfly a loin well and spread the mustard from the pickles
2. Cut the pickles lengthwise along with bell peppers
3. Fill the loin with this mixture, sew it closed
4. Cook with water, vinegar, salt, bay leaf, tomato, and onion
5. Serve with sauce or salad
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Longanizas (Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- lard
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut meat and lard into small pieces
2. Season with salt to taste
3. Fill thin, clean intestines with the mixture
4. Tie to the desired length
5. Place in the sun
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarelas Guisadas (Stewed Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- oil from the mackerel
- peeled tomato, chopped
- onion, chopped
- garlic, chopped
- parsley, chopped
- vinegar
- water
- salt
- pepper powder
- olives
- capers
- cooked potato slices

## Directions

1. Remove the oil from the mackerel and use it to fry the peeled chopped tomato, onion, garlic and parsley
2. When well fried, add a spoonful of vinegar and a little water
3. Season with salt and pepper powder and add olives and capers
4. Cover the mackerel with this mixture
5. Serve if desired with sliced cooked potatoes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macaroni Omelette (Tortilla con Macarrones)
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Eggs
- Cheese powder

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni broken up.
2. When cooked, remove and drain all the water.
3. Have the egg beaten as in the previous recipes.
4. For three eggs use one ounce of macaroni.
5. Mix well with cheese powder.
6. Mix with the egg.
7. Make the omelette and fry.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarons (Almond Macaroons)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups of sugar
- 3 egg whites
- 1 pound of peeled and ground almonds (dry)
- butter for coating
- flour for dusting

## Directions

1. Place three cups of sugar and three egg whites in a vessel
2. Beat until stiff
3. Add one pound of peeled and ground almonds (dry)
4. Coat tin leaves with butter
5. Drop spoonfuls of this paste onto the leaves
6. Before placing the dough, dust the tin with bread flour
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarrones a la Francesa (French Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- salt
- water
- gruyère or parmesan cheese, grated
- bread powder

## Directions

1. Boil the macaroni in salted water
2. When cooked, place in a strainer to drain
3. Place in a platter
4. Add a little grated gruyère or parmesan cheese and mix well
5. Add bread powder and another little amount of cheese powder
6. Put in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarrones a la Francesa (French Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni in water with salt
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarrones a la Francesa (French-Style Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Milk
- Salt
- Butter
- Parmesan cheese powder

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni with milk and a few grains of salt
2. When cooked, remove and butter a pan
3. Place a layer of macaroni, a layer of Parmesan cheese powder and alternate the layers, making sure the last one is cheese
4. Cover with the rest of the milk and add two ounces of melted butter
5. Place in the oven or over two fires
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarrones al Horno (Baked Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- salt
- butter
- cheese powder

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni with salt
2. Butter a pan and alternately layer powdered cheese and macaroni until finished
3. Top with another layer of butter
4. Bake for ten minutes or cook over two fires
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarrones al Horno (Baked Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- macarrones (macaroni)
- sal (salt)
- mantequilla (butter)
- polvo de queso (grated cheese)

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni with salt
2. Butter a pan
3. Place alternately a layer of grated cheese and a layer of macaroni, continuing until finished
4. Top with another layer of butter
5. Place in the oven or over two fires for ten minutes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarrones con Queso (Macaroni with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Hard cheese powder
- Truffles or Extremadura chorizo (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni in water with salt
2. When cooked, remove and butter a pan with one tablespoon of butter
3. Place a layer of macaroni and a layer of hard cheese powder
4. If desired add a layer of truffles or Extremadura chorizo
5. Alternate the layers so the last one is cheese and place in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarrones en Leche (Macaroni in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Lightly boil the macaroni; when boiled, add to a glass of milk with two tablespoons of butter and let boil until the macaroni is cooked through
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarrones en Leche (Macaroni in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- macarrones (macaroni)
- leche (milk)
- mantequilla (butter)

## Directions

1. Lightly parboil the macaroni
2. When parboiled, place in a glass of milk with two tablespoons of butter
3. Let boil until the macaroni finishes cooking in the milk
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macarrones en su Jugo (Macaroni in its Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound macaroni
- 3 pounds good quality meat
- butter
- onion
- salt
- powdered pepper
- Parmesan or Gruyère cheese

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni in boiling salted water; if possible leave them whole; when cooked, remove them carefully so they do not break
2. Prepare the sauce: for one pound of macaroni, pound three pounds of very good meat in a meat grinder to extract all the juice
3. Fry the meat in hot butter where onion, very finely chopped, has already been fried
4. If there is no meat grinder, slice the meat and pound it well, then fry it
5. When it has taken on a golden color, fry the onion
6. Cover with water, add a little salt and a little powdered pepper
7. Let it boil for two hours
8. Remove the meat and cover the macaroni with this juice, sprinkling with Parmesan or Gruyère cheese
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Macrones (Macarons)
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of sugar
- three egg whites
- one pound of peeled and ground almonds

## Directions

1. Place three cups of sugar and three egg whites in a container
2. Beat until it becomes hard
3. When well hardened, add one pound of peeled and ground almonds
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Magdalena (Madeleine)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces of sugar
- three ounces of flour
- two ounces of lard
- six eggs
- honey
- filling of choice

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs with the sugar and let warm slightly
2. When it has risen sufficiently, add melted lard and sifted flour
3. Stir with a skimmer without beating so it does not become firm
4. Put in a greased mold and place in the oven
5. Cut in half, brush with honey and fill with whatever is desired
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Magdalenas (Madeleines)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg whites
- six egg yolks
- six ounces of sifted flour
- four ounces of sifted sugar
- one ounce of melted lard

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites until stiff like nougat
2. Mix in the yolks with the sifted flour, sifted sugar, and melted lard
3. Place in buttered molds
4. Bake in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Magdalenas (Madeleines)
body:
## Ingredients

- Six egg whites
- Six egg yolks
- Five ounces of sugar
- Zest of one lemon
- Six ounces of sifted flour
- One ounce of melted butter

## Directions

1. Beat the six egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Mix the egg yolks with five ounces of sugar and the lemon zest
3. Add the six ounces of sifted flour and lastly one ounce of melted butter
4. Place in greased paper boxes in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Manjar Real (Royal Delicacy)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of sugar
- one ounce of rice or rice flour, well-sifted
- two liters of milk
- one ounce of bread flour
- four ounces of ground and peeled almonds
- cinnamon powder
- chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Clarify two pounds of sugar
2. When it reaches the proper stage, lower heat and grind one ounce of rice or use well-sifted rice flour
3. Place in two liters of milk with one ounce of bread flour
4. Mix well and place over fire, stirring so it does not stick
5. When it begins to thicken, add the sugar mixture with four ounces of ground and peeled almonds
6. Leave until it reaches the soft-ball stage (punto de ante)
7. Place in a platter and decorate with cinnamon powder and chopped almonds
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Mantecadas (Spanish Butter Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of butter
- 1 pound of sugar
- 1 ounce of cinnamon powder
- 6 eggs
- flour as needed
- zest of 1 lemon
- sugar for topping (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat one pound of butter with one of sugar until white
2. Add one ounce of cinnamon powder
3. Add six eggs and beat again for a long time
4. Add flour, as much as needed to achieve a consistency like buñuelo dough
5. Add the zest of a lemon and beat well again to achieve good results
6. Make small white paper boxes coated with butter
7. Place one small spoonful of the dough in each and bake in the oven
8. If desired, sprinkle sugar on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Mantecadas de Astorga (Astorga Butter Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound of sugar
- Half pound of butter
- Half pound of peeled, toasted, and ground almonds
- A bit of ammonium bicarbonate or Royal baking powder
- Twelve ounces of toasted flour
- One egg white, well beaten

## Directions

1. Mix everything very well
2. Place in paper boxes or chat shapes on baking sheets
3. Top with well-beaten egg white
4. Place in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Mantecadas de Morella (Morella Butter Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sugar
- half a pound of lard
- half a pound of peeled and toasted almonds
- a little ammonium bicarbonate or one teaspoon of Royal baking powder
- twelve ounces of toasted bread flour

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar, lard, toasted almonds, baking powder, and toasted flour
2. If too soft, add two more ounces of flour
3. Place in buttered molds or paper boxes
4. Bake in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Mantecados de Vino (Wine Butter Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Nine ounces of butter
- Half glass of sweet sherry
- Nine ounces of bread flour
- Powdered sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until it whitens
2. Add the sherry little by little
3. Add the bread flour and beat again
4. If too wet, add a bit more flour
5. Place on greased baking sheets
6. Place in the oven
7. When very hot, sprinkle sifted powdered sugar on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Manzanas a la Italiana (Italian-Style Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten large ripe apples
- one pound of clarified sugar at medium-firm stage
- ten peaches
- four ounces of rice cooked in milk
- two egg yolks
