- Chiapas peppers
- Cloves
- Thyme branch
- Chicken or plump fowl
- Good bone (six pounds if for over twenty people)
- Sheep head
- Garbanzo beans
- Leeks
- Celery branch (if desired)
- Flour
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Mince very finely: green beans, turnips, carrots and cabbage, adding peas
2. Lightly fry everything in butter with two bay leaves, two Chiapas peppers, crushed cloves and a thyme branch
3. Put all this to cook with a broth prepared as follows: cook a plump hen or chicken
4. If the soup is for more than twenty people, add six pounds of very good bone and a very clean sheep head
5. Add a handful of garbanzo beans, a bundle of leeks and if desired a celery branch
6. This broth should boil for six hours and into it drop everything above
7. Thicken the soup with a little flour fried in butter with chopped parsley
8. Serve at the third boil
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Sopa Rellena (Stuffed Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- 1 pound pork meat
- lard
- fried seasoning
- pepper
- cinnamon
- clove
- salt
- vinegar
- sherry
- raisins
- almonds
- olives
- capers
- chicken or squab
- eggs

## Directions

1. Make the day before some rice as for daily use, parboil one pound of pork meat, mince it very well and fry it with a little lard, sufficient fried seasoning, pepper powder, cinnamon, clove and salt; a little vinegar and a little sherry (if there is none it is not necessary)
2. Add raisins, well-peeled almonds, olives, capers and small pieces of chicken or whole squabs that will have been previously made in a stew
3. Line a pan with lard or butter, put one layer of rice and on top of this place all the filling that was explained, cover this filling with another layer of rice and cover this with three beaten eggs
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Soufflé Omelette (Tortilla Suflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Sugar
- Cognac
- Butter

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs, the yolks with one ounce of sugar and a small glass of cognac.
2. Beat both things well and then combine them.
3. Form the omelette and fry in hot butter.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Supremos de Pavo (Turkey Supremes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey, deboned
- ground meat for filling
- salt
- Castilian pepper powder
- nutmeg
- parsley, chopped
- caul fat (membrane from sheep's belly)
- 2 carrots, peeled and sliced
- lard
- water and salt

## Directions

1. Debone the turkey and cut into 2-inch square pieces.
2. Prepare a filling of ground meat seasoned with salt, Castilian pepper powder, nutmeg, and chopped parsley.
3. Fill the turkey pieces with this mixture.
4. Prepare caul fat in pieces and wrap the turkey pieces in it, securing with a stitch.
5. Place in a pan with lard and the sliced carrots.
6. Add a little water with salt.
7. Let simmer until cooked through.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Supremos de Pollo (Chicken Supremes)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat chicken
- pork meat
- salt
- pepper powder
- nutmeg
- parsley
- thin membrane from lamb belly
- lard
- carrot

## Directions

1. Debone a fat chicken and cut into two-inch square pieces
2. Grind a little pork meat and season with salt, pepper powder, nutmeg and chopped parsley
3. Use this to fill the small squares which are wrapped in thin membrane from the lamb belly
4. Sew and place in a dish with lard and a halved carrot
5. Let boil for half an hour
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Suspiros (Sighs)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sugar
- very little water
- 2 egg whites
- half a pound of very dry peeled almonds, cut in strips or ground
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Place half a pound of sugar on the fire with very little water
2. When at thread stage, pour hot into two egg whites that will be well beaten as for meringue
3. Continue beating and when very stiff
4. Add half a pound of very dry peeled almonds, cut in strips or ground
5. Add the juice of a lemon
6. Place on butters-coated tins in desired sizes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tallarines o Macarrones a la Italiana (Italian-Style Noodles or Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 tablespoon salt
- 2 teaspoons milk or water
- 1 fat hen
- 2 pounds soft and fatty meat
- lard and butter
- onion
- green chile
- ripe tomato
- 2 cups water
- powdered pepper
- salt
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. For macaroni, boil as previously described; for noodles, mix one pound of flour with four eggs (white and yolk), half a tablespoon of salt and two teaspoons of milk or water
2. Knead until the dough is very fine, then roll it out with a rolling pin until very thin
3. Cut with a special cutter if available, otherwise with a knife, into long forms or as desired
4. Dust the work surface with flour and place the strips on a cloth also dusted with flour
5. Prepare a large pot or dish with boiling water and drop the noodles in, making sure they do not stick together
6. Add a tablespoon of salt to the water, let boil for a quarter hour then remove so they do not retain a flour taste
7. Drop them in cold water
8. Prepare the sauce: clean a fat hen and cut it into very small pieces
9. Fry in lard and butter with two pounds of soft and fatty meat
10. When it has taken a golden color (without burning), add chopped onion and, if desired, finely chopped green chile
11. Have ready peeled and chopped ripe tomato; to improve the taste of the sauce, strain it to remove all seeds
12. Fry this together with the meat for a quarter hour
13. Add two cups of water with powdered pepper very well seasoned with salt
14. Let boil for two or three hours and if it becomes too thick add a little water so it has a consistency that is neither thin nor thick
15. Remove all the meat and in a platter layer sauce, noodles, Parmesan cheese powder and so on until finished, making sure the last layer is cheese
16. Serve with the sauce as hot as possible; if desired, bake for a quarter hour in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tallarines Suecos (Swedish Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork
- salt
- thyme
- bay leaf
- garlic
- onion slices
- pepper powder
- sweet chile
- chorizo
- longaniza (Spanish sausage)
- ham
- roasted tomato
- dried guaque or pasilla chile
- butter
- noodles or spaghetti
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Place a piece of pork with salt, thyme, bay leaf, crushed garlic, onion slices, pepper powder, sweet chile, chorizo, longaniza, and ham to cure overnight.
2. The next day, chop the meat and add roasted tomato and well-ground dried guaque or pasilla chile.
3. Fry with the previous meats.
4. Coat a pot with sufficient butter.
5. Layer noodles or spaghetti, then Parmesan cheese powder, then sauce; make the top layer cheese.
6. Place in the oven or between two fires to cook.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamal-Ayote (Squash Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- coastal squash
- one and a half pounds of sugar
- a small teaspoon of lime lye
- water

## Directions

1. Peel the coastal squash very well
2. Place it in water for half an hour with a small teaspoon of lime lye
3. Clarify one and a half pounds of sugar
4. Add the squash cut as desired
5. Allow the syrup to reach a very high point and let the fruit dry
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamales Bolcheviques (Bolshevik Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- chiles pimientos, about eighteen
- roasted tomatoes, twelve
- onion head, one
- lard, two ounces
- salt
- rice, one pound
- milk, two liters
- lard, fourteen ounces
- corn dough
- chicken pieces
- cheese pieces
- butter pieces
- corn husks
- twine

## Directions

1. Grind the red chiles pimientos, roasted tomatoes, and ground onion head
2. Fry in two ounces of lard and season with salt
3. Grind one pound of rice that has been soaked the day before
4. When well ground, cook in two liters of milk and fourteen ounces of lard with salt to taste
5. When the dough is well cooked, place a little white corn dough on the husk
6. Add in the middle a piece of chicken and the prepared mixture with cheese and butter pieces
7. Wrap well, tie with twine, and cook like regular tamales
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamales Colorados (Red Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of rice
- 1 liter of water
- 1 ounce of salt
- half an eighth of saffron
- hot water
- 1 pound of lard
- 2 ounces of pepitoria
- 2 ounces of sesame
- 2 cooked guaque chiles
- a small piece of fine achiote
- half an eighth of Castilian pepper
- half an eighth of clove
- 6 French beans soaked
- pieces of turkey or pork
- a small teaspoon of salt
- tamale leaves

## Directions

1. Soak 2 pounds of rice overnight
2. The next day let it half dry and grind it very fine
3. Put it in a pan or dish with 1 liter of water and 1 ounce of salt
4. The day before, extinguish half an eighth of saffron with hot water and add it to the well-broken dough
5. Put on the fire with 1 pound of lard, stirring continuously so it does not become lumpy
6. After half an hour of boiling on medium heat, lower and let cool
7. Toast 2 ounces of pepitoria and 2 ounces of sesame until golden but not burnt
8. Grind with 2 cooked guaque chiles, a small piece of fine achiote, half an eighth of Castilian pepper and half an eighth of clove
9. Grind 6 cold soaked French beans
10. Dissolve this with a little water and fry in 2 ounces of lard
11. Add the pieces of turkey or pork with a small teaspoon of salt
12. Let the sauce and meat boil for one hour
13. Remove from fire and let cool
14. Prepare the leaves well cleaned, remove a piece from the bottom, put the leaf upside down with the small piece right side up
15. Put a spoonful of white dough, make a hole in the middle with the same spoon or skimmer
16. Put the meat with half a spoonful of dough, trying not to stain the tamal
17. Place to cook in a clay or pewter pot with cleaned leaf sticks or folders on the bottom
18. Arrange the tamales on top, leaving a hollow in the middle where a small jug containing 3 liters of water is placed
19. The tamales should boil for 3 hours on medium heat
20. If the water runs out during this time, add another liter and a half
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamales con Carne Picada (Tamales with Minced Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice or two pounds corn kernels (salpor) cooked with lime
- one liter water
- one ounce salt
- half liter saffron water infused the night before
- one and a half pounds lard
- ten cooked tomatoes
- two guaque chiles
- a small piece of achiote
- Castilian peppercorns
- a small piece of cinnamon
- two ounces lard
- three pounds raw pork meat, minced
- olives
- capers
- hard-boiled egg (optional)

## Directions

1. Soak the rice; the next day wash it and while still half-damp, grind it very fine.
2. If using corn kernels, cook two pounds with lime, wash, grind while dry, and sieve as before.
3. Dissolve the masa with one liter of water, one ounce salt, and half liter of saffron water that was infused the night before.
4. Add one and a half pounds lard.
5. After boiling for half an hour over moderate heat, remove and let cool.
6. Grind ten cooked tomatoes with two guaque chiles, a small piece of achiote, some Castilian peppercorns, and a small piece of cinnamon.
7. Fry this in two ounces of lard.
8. Add three pounds of minced raw pork meat to the seasoned sauce and let it boil for half an hour.
9. When everything is cool, place one tablespoon of white masa in the center of a corn husk, then one tablespoon of meat with sauce, an olive, about four capers, and if desired, a slice of hard-boiled egg.
10. Wrap and cook as before.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamales de Verdura con Cecina (Vegetable Tamales with Dried Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrot, chopped
- güisquil, chopped
- potatoes, chopped
- buxmayes, chopped
- pacayas, chopped
- green beans (ejotes), chopped
- roasted tomatoes
- two dried chile peppers (chiles de pasa)
- salt
- a small piece of achiote
- pieces of dried beef (cecina), more than parboiled
- cooked masa

## Directions

1. Chop carrot, güisquil, potatoes, buxmayes, pacayas, and green beans.
2. Grind roasted tomatoes with two dried chile peppers.
3. Season this with salt and grind in a small piece of achiote.
4. Mix with the vegetables and add pieces of well-parboiled dried beef.
5. Have some masa cooked as for red tamales ready.
6. Place one tablespoon of this masa on a corn husk, in the middle place a piece of dried beef with vegetables and sauce.
7. Wrap and cook like the others.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamales Dulces (Sweet Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds corn dough
- half pound lard
- four ounces melted butter
- six egg yolks
- salt and sugar to taste
- milk (if needed)
- cinnamon powder
- raisins
- blanched almonds
- dobladores (corn husk wrappers)

## Directions

1. Cook two pounds of corn dough
2. Grind and sift it
3. Grind again without moistening
4. Add half pound of lard and four ounces of melted butter
5. Add six egg yolks, salt and sugar to taste
6. If too thick, add a little milk
7. Place the dough in the wrapper
8. In the middle, add a little cinnamon powder, one raisin, and half a blanched almond
9. Wrap and tie
10. Cook by steaming
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamales Mexicanos (Mexican Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds corn kernels (salpor)
- salt
- one glass cold water
- one pound melted lard
- half pound additional lard
- six dried chile peppers (chiles de pasa), seeded and soaked
- four roasted tomatoes
- two ounces lard
- raw chicken pieces
- corn husks (dobladores)
- olives
- capers
- almonds
- raisins

## Directions

1. Cook two pounds of corn kernels as before and grind the masa well.
2. Season with salt and add one glass of cold water and one pound of melted lard.
3. Knead for half an hour, beating it very hard.
4. Add another half pound of lard and knead for another quarter hour.
5. If too thick, add a little more water until the masa is not hard but can be wrapped.
6. Grind six seeded and soaked dried chile peppers with four roasted tomatoes.
7. Fry this in two ounces of lard and season with salt.
8. Add raw chicken pieces to this sauce and let them boil for half an hour, then remove from heat and let cool.
9. Wash large corn husks with hot water, wring them out, and shake until all water is removed.
10. Place a little white masa on the corn husk, in the middle put a piece of chicken with its sauce, an olive, two capers, half an almond, and two raisins.
11. Cover with another small amount of white masa, wrap, and tie on both sides like small cambray tamales, cutting the tip of the corn husk with scissors.
12. Cook in steam; they will take about one hour over moderate heat, adding a little water, and when it is consumed add more, ensuring the pot has a good base so they do not burn.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamales Negros de Primera (Black Tamales, First Version)
body:
## Ingredients

- salpor or rice
- water with lime
- 1 liter of boiled milk
- 1 ounce of salt
- half an eighth of saffron
- half liter of boiling water
- 1 pound of lard
- 6 ounces of peeled almonds
- 1 pound of white sugar
- 4 ounces of pepitoria
- 4 ounces of sesame
- 15 cents of egg bread
- clove
- Castilian pepper
- quarter canela
- banana peel
- 2 roasted dried chiles or 2 guaque chiles
- 2 ounces of raisins
- 2 ounces of lard for frying
- 2 glasses of water
- pieces of turkey and pork
- 1 small teaspoon of salt
- half a pound of sugar
- prune
- raisin
- almond
- soaked and split fibers for tying

## Directions

1. If using salpor, cook with lime water, wash, grind, and sieve, everything very clean
2. If using rice, soak the night before
3. The next day wash very well and dissolve with 1 liter of boiled milk and 1 ounce of salt
4. The night before, extinguish half an eighth of saffron with half a liter of boiling water
5. Add to the dough 1 pound of lard
6. Put on fire without stopping stirring so it does not stick
7. Add 6 ounces of peeled almonds ground dry, not very fine, and 1 pound of white sugar
8. If too thick, add more milk
9. For the sauce, toast 4 ounces of pepitoria, 4 ounces of sesame, 15 cents of the best egg bread
10. When the bread is well toasted, grind dry as fine as possible
11. Then grind the pepitoria with one eighth clove, one eighth Castilian pepper, a quarter canela, the well-burnt peel of a plantain, 2 dried chiles roasted in lard; if unavailable, grind 2 guaque chiles and 2 ounces of raisins
12. Dissolve this with a little water
13. Put a pan on the fire with 2 ounces of lard and fry the bread powder
14. Then add the sauce and 2 more glasses of water, the pieces of turkey and pork (both kinds are used), 1 small teaspoon of salt and half a pound of sugar
15. Let boil until the water is consumed
16. Remove and let cool
17. Put a large spoonful on the leaves, in the middle the pepián without staining the tamal
18. Put a prune, a raisin and half an almond
19. Wrap and cook as the previous ones, tying them with soaked fibers split in four
20. The way the tamal is wrapped is what gives it its appeal
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamales Negros de Segunda (Black Tamales, Second Version)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice or salpor
- water
- saffron
- sugar
- salt
- 2 ounces of pepitoria
- 2 ounces of sesame
- 1 cup of miltomate
- 2 dried chiles
- lard
- 4 French beans
- Castilian pepper
- clove

## Directions

1. Cook and grind rice or salpor as in the previous version
2. Simmer the dough with water, saffron, sugar and salt in the same quantities as before
3. For the sauce, toast 2 ounces of pepitoria, 2 ounces of sesame, 1 cup of miltomate, 2 dried chiles browned in lard, 4 well-fried French beans
4. Grind with half an eighth of Castilian pepper and half an eighth of clove
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamales Negros de Segunda (Second Black Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice or corn kernels (salpor)
- two ounces pepitoria
- two ounces sesame seeds (ajonjolí)
- one cup miltomate
- two dried chile peppers (chiles de pasa), fried in lard
- four French peppers, well fried
- half ochavo of Castilian pepper
- half ochavo of clove
- half ochavo of cinnamon
- a piece of burned plantain peel
- three ounces lard
- a small piece of achiote
- one teaspoon salt
- six ounces brown sugar
- raisins and almonds

## Directions

1. Cook the rice or corn kernels as in previous recipes and grind them.
2. Parboil the masa with water, saffron, sugar, and salt in the same quantities as before.
3. For the recado (sauce), toast two ounces pepitoria and two ounces sesame seeds, one cup miltomate, and two dried chile peppers fried in lard, and four well-fried French peppers.
4. Grind these with half ochavo of Castilian pepper, half ochavo of clove, and half ochavo of cinnamon, and a piece of burned plantain peel.
5. Fry this mixture in three ounces of lard in which a small piece of achiote has been dissolved.
6. Add one teaspoon salt and six ounces of brown sugar.
7. When cool, wrap as before, adding only raisins and almonds.
8. Cook the same way as the other tamales.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamalitos de Cambray con Azafrán (Saffron Cambray Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds corn dough
- one cup saffron water
- salt and sugar to taste
- one pound melted lard
- one pound cooked pork meat (chopped)
- one spoonful lard (for frying the meat)
- sugar and salt to taste
- pepper powder
- cloves
- cinnamon
- wide, fine dobladores (wrappers)
- gragea (small candies)
- leaves of maxán (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook three pounds of corn dough as for the others
2. Sift and grind dry
3. Moisten with one cup of saffron water, salt, sugar to taste, and one pound of melted lard
4. Beat for half an hour
5. Have ready one pound of cooked pork meat, chopped
6. Fry it in one spoonful of lard
7. Add sugar and salt to taste, pepper powder, cloves, and cinnamon
8. When well fried, remove from heat and let cool
9. Have clean, wide, fine wrappers ready
10. Place one spoonful of dough in each wrapper, in the middle put a teaspoonful of the chopped meat
11. Tie with a thin strip of wrapper, very tightly so the tamales come out very round
12. Cook the same as the others; they can also be wrapped in maxán leaves, putting a little gragea before adding the dough
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamalitos de Cambray de Leche (Milk Cambray Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds corn dough
- one glass clean cooked milk
- one pound melted lard
- salt and sugar to taste
- small candies (gragea)
- raisins
- blanched almonds
- dobladores (wrappers, clean and white)

## Directions

1. Cook three pounds of corn dough as for tamales
2. Sift and grind twice more dry
3. Add one glass of clean cooked milk and one pound of melted lard
4. Add salt and sugar to taste
5. Knead for half an hour until it sounds well-beaten
6. Have ready clean, white, well-washed wrappers
7. Put a little gragea on each wrapper, then two separate spoonfuls of dough on top; put one raisin in one spoonful and half a blanched almond in the other
8. Wrap and tie very tightly so the tamale comes out very round
9. Cut the ends of the wrapper
10. Cook in a very clean pot with a good base of clean wrappers so they do not burn
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamalitos de Elote (Corn Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn ears (6 to 8, not very ripe)
- cheese (1)
- salt (to taste)
- sugar (to taste)
- lard or butter (1 large spoon)

## Directions

1. Shell 6 to 8 ears of corn that are not very ripe, grinding until the masa is very fine.
2. Grind cheese with salt and sugar to taste.
3. Add 1 large spoon of lard or butter.
4. Wrap the mixture in the corn husks.
5. Cook in a steamer.
6. The next day, reheat well and serve.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamalitos de Elote (Corn Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty corn cobs, shelled
- salt
- one pound lard
- two pounds sugar
- one cup milk (if corn is very ripe)

## Directions

1. Shell and grind the corn, then strain it
2. For twenty corn cobs, add one teaspoon salt, one pound lard, and two pounds sugar
3. Mix everything very well
4. If the corn is very ripe, stir in one cup of milk
5. Ensure the corn is tender
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamalitos de Elote (Fresh Corn Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh corn (not too ripe, ground)
- sugar or salt to taste
- ground cheese
- one ounce butter
- one ounce lard
- corn husks

## Directions

1. Grind fresh corn that is not too ripe
2. Add sugar or salt to taste, ground cheese, one ounce of butter, and one ounce of lard
3. Mix everything well
4. Wrap the tamales in the same corn husks
5. Cook by steaming
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tamalitos de Hongos (Little Mushroom Tamales)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn (maíz)
- salt
- half pound melted lard
- miltomate, ground
- dried chile, cooked
- a small piece of achiote
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cook and grind the corn.
2. Add salt, half pound of melted lard, a little ground miltomate with cooked dried chile to taste, and a small piece of achiote.
3. Mix everything very well.
4. Prepare the mushrooms by cutting them and soaking them in water overnight, changing the water many times to ensure no insects remain.
5. Wrap in corn husks and cook like the previous tamales.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tiste (Tiste Drink)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of salpor
- One ounce of cinnamon
- Three ounces of cacao
- Two small cloths of very fine achiote
- Three pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Toast two pounds of salpor
2. Grind it, sift in a strainer and once sifted, grind again with one ounce of cinnamon, three ounces of cacao, two small cloths of very fine achiote and three pounds of sugar
3. To serve, put one large spoon in a pitcher of water and beat well so that when poured into a glass it makes foam
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tomates al Graten (Tomatoes au Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- parsley
- garlic
- bread powder
- salt
- pepper powder
- oil

## Directions

1. Cut the tomatoes in half and extract the juice and seeds, being careful not to bruise them
2. Prepare parsley and garlic finely chopped, mix with bread powder and use this to fill the tomato halves
3. Add salt to taste and a little pepper powder
4. Place the tomatoes in a dish or pan, put sufficient oil on top and place in a not very hot oven to prevent them from browning too much
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tomates Rellenos (Stuffed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large tomatoes
- Half ground beef
- Half ground pork
- Pepper powder
- Salt
- Chopped parsley
- One tablespoon of butter
- One cup of broth

## Directions

1. Peel the large tomatoes with boiling water
2. Carefully remove the seeds
3. Grind half beef and half pork and mix with a little pepper powder, salt, chopped parsley, and one tablespoon of butter
4. Fill the tomatoes with this mixture
5. Place them in a casserole with one cup of broth
6. Bake in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torrijas (Bread Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread sliced into rounds
- two eggs
- lard for frying
- one pound of clarified sugar
- cinnamon sticks
- ground or chopped almonds

## Directions

1. In two small bowls beat two eggs, first the white and then the yolk
2. Wrap the bread slices in this
3. Fry in hot lard
4. Wash and dry with a clean and damp cloth
5. Have ready one pound of clarified sugar
6. Put the torrijas in this with cinnamon sticks and when they heat, remove them
7. Place on a platter and decorate them with almonds
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta a la Española (Spanish Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- onion
- two eggs
- parsley
- oil or lard

## Directions

1. Fry potatoes sliced so they do not brown.
2. Fry chopped onion.
3. Mix everything with two eggs and chopped parsley.
4. Fry as an omelet.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Almendra (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half pounds of sugar
- one-half pound of peeled and ground almonds
- four egg whites
- biscotela dough
- two egg whites
- sugar powder
- four drops of lemon
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Clarify one and one-half pounds of sugar and bring to syrup stage (punto de almíbar)
2. Add one-half pound of peeled and ground almonds
3. Place over fire until it reaches the dry stage (punto seco)
4. Lower heat and when cold, add four beaten egg whites
5. Place a mass of biscotela dough on a platter and bake in the oven
6. Pour the almond preserve over the baked dough and return to the oven
7. When baked, coat with two beaten egg whites mixed with sugar powder, four drops of lemon, and a little cinnamon powder
8. Return to the oven until the glaze is golden
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Almendra (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- four egg whites
- eight egg yolks
- one-half pound of peeled and ground almonds
- butter
- clarified sugar at medium stage
- one glass of sherry

## Directions

1. Beat four egg whites and eight egg yolks separately
2. When both are well beaten, combine them
3. Add one-half pound of peeled and ground almonds
4. Place in a buttered mold and pour the mixture into it
5. Bake in the oven or in a water bath (bain-marie)
6. When baked, remove and place in another clean mold
7. Have the sugar clarified and at medium stage with one glass of sherry, coat the cake with this mixture and return to the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Almendras (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of peeled almonds
- seven egg whites
- egg yolks
- marquesote powder or egg bread
- butter
- sweet bread powder
- one pound of clarified sugar
- a small glass of dry sherry

## Directions

1. Grind half a pound of peeled almonds dry
2. Beat seven egg whites until very stiff
3. Mix in the egg yolks, almonds, and marquesote or egg bread powder
4. Coat a mold with butter and sweet bread powder so it does not stick
5. Pour the batter into the mold and place in the oven
6. When cooked, add one pound of clarified sugar at the right point with a small glass of sherry
7. Pour over the cake and leave
8. Serve cold the next day
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Almendras (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pounds peeled almonds
- 7 egg whites
- egg yolks
- marquesote powder or egg bread
- butter
- sweet bread powder

## Directions

1. Grind 0.5 pounds of peeled almonds
2. Beat 7 egg whites until very stiff
3. Blend in the yolks, the almonds and marquesote powder or egg bread
4. Have a mold greased with butter and dusted with sweet bread powder so it does not stick
5. Pour the batter into it and put in the oven
6. When cooked, add
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Arroz (Rice Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 glasses milk
- 0.5 pounds very well washed rice
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 4 ounces peeled and ground almonds
- 1 pound sugar
- 2 whole eggs and 6 egg yolks
- a little marquesote powder or egg bread

## Directions

1. Put 0.5 pounds of very well washed rice in 5 glasses of milk with 1 cinnamon stick
2. When well cooked, add 4 ounces of peeled and ground almonds and 1 pound of sugar
3. When it reaches the point, lower the heat and when cool, add 2 whole eggs and 6 egg yolks
4. Add a little marquesote powder or egg bread
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Berros (Watercress Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of pork
- watercress
- tomato
- onion
- garlic
- pepper powder
- two eggs
- bread flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Partially cook a piece of pork, chop it very finely
2. Chop a small amount of watercress
3. Mix the pork and watercress together
4. Add tomato, onion, garlic, and pepper powder
5. Add two eggs and a little bread flour
6. Fry in hot lard
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Bojón (Bojón Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- bojón
- salt
- eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove all the skin and thin center stalk from the bojón
2. Chop it
3. Cook it with salt
4. When cooked, drain all the water
5. Mix with beaten egg
6. Form the cake
7. Fry like the previous ones
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Espinaca (Spinach Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- eggs
- tomato
- onion

## Directions

1. Clean the spinach well, remove the stems and chop very fine.
2. Beat eggs according to the quantity desired.
3. Add the spinach to the beaten eggs and fry the omelet in a hot pan.
4. Make a fried seasoning of tomato and onion.
5. Give the omelet a quick boil in this seasoning.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Flor de Izote (Izote Flower Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- izote flower
- salt
- eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the seed from the flower
2. Cook it with salt
3. When done, remove and squeeze to release all the water
4. Mix with beaten egg, seasoning with salt
5. Fry in hot butter
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Garbanzo (Chickpea Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of chickpeas
- One eighth ounce of cinnamon powder
- Sugar to taste
- Three eggs
- Butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Soak one pound of chickpeas; the following day peel them
2. Add one eighth ounce of cinnamon powder, sugar to taste, and three eggs
3. Mix everything well
4. Place in a pan greased with butter
5. Bake in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Güicoyes (Güicoy Squash Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender güicoyes
- butter
- salt
- green chile pimiento
- fresh cheese or butter

## Directions

1. Slice the tender güicoyes.
2. Butter a pan and put a layer of güicoy slices with a little salt and small pieces of chile pimiento chopped lengthwise.
3. Alternate layers, some of güicoy and others of small pieces of fresh cheese or butter.
4. Put in the oven or on very low heat; when it has boiled for a quarter hour, cover with a beaten egg; if there is no oven, place a tin with hot coals on top to brown it.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Ichintal (Ichintal Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- ichintales
- salt
- eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel and clean the ichintales well
2. Cook them with salt
3. When done, remove and let all the water drain
4. Grind them, mixing in eggs like the previous ones
5. Form the cake
6. Fry it in butter over very low heat
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Leche (Milk Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- three glasses of milk sweetened to taste
- three egg yolks
- egg bread
- raisins
- chopped almonds
- lard or butter

## Directions

1. In three glasses of sweetened milk place three egg yolks and enough crumbled egg bread to form the cake
2. Add raisins and chopped almonds
3. Mix everything well and place in a well-buttered cooking pot
4. Put in the oven until it is cooked
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Madre de Cacao (Cacao Flower Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- cacao flower
- eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the cacao flower
2. Squeeze it to remove all the water
3. Mix in egg
4. Fry like the others
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Requesón (Cheese Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of milk
- a small piece of rennet tablet or half a teaspoon of rennet
- one ounce of almonds
- one ounce of sugar
- two ounces of requesón (curd cheese)
- three eggs
- two ounces of melted butter
- one eighth of an ounce of cinnamon powder
- half a pound of clarified sugar
- half a teaspoon of vanilla

## Directions

1. Heat one liter of milk over the fire and add a small piece of rennet tablet or half a teaspoon of rennet
2. When the requesón forms, remove and squeeze very well in a clean cloth
3. Grind one ounce of almonds and one ounce of sugar
4. Beat three eggs, first the whites and then the yolks
5. Mix all the ground ingredients with the eggs, two ounces of melted butter, and one eighth of an ounce of cinnamon powder
6. Coat a mold with butter
7. Pour in the mixture and place in the oven for twenty minutes
8. Insert a knife; if it comes out clean it is done
9. Remove and place in another mold
10. Have ready half a pound of clarified sugar with half a teaspoon of vanilla
11. Cover the cake and put back in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta de Salmón (Salmon Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- salt
- pepper powder
- 1 tin salmon, without bones
- 1/2 cup milk
- lard
- puree or peas for garnish

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs with salt and pepper powder to taste
2. When well beaten, add one tin of salmon (without bones) and one-half cup of milk
3. Mix everything well and fry in hot lard and when golden, fold and decorate with puree or peas around the edge
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta del Cielo (Cake of Heaven)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ounces peeled almonds
- 1 glass milk
- 4 ounces sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 ounces butter
- Leche Nevada (snowy milk)

## Directions

1. Grind 3 ounces of peeled almonds
2. Dissolve them in 1 glass of milk
3. Add 4 ounces of sugar
4. Put on the fire to reach the point
5. Lower the heat and when cool, mix in 4 egg yolks and 2 ounces of butter
6. Grease a dish with butter
7. Put a layer of this mixture and a layer of Leche Nevada (snowy milk) and cover with another layer
8. Put in the oven to brown
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta Mexicana (Mexican Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- half a pound of powdered sugar
- nine egg whites
- half a pound of sifted flour
- half a teaspoon of bitter almond water
- one ounce of currants
- one ounce of raisins
- one teaspoon of Royal baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat half a pound of butter for half an hour
2. Then add half a pound of powdered sugar and beat for half an hour more
3. While this is being beaten, have nine egg whites beating
4. When the whites are very stiff, add them to the butter mixture
5. Add half a pound of sifted flour, half a teaspoon of bitter almond water, one ounce of currants, one ounce of raisins, and one teaspoon of Royal baking powder
6. Pour into a mold coated with butter and bread powder so it does not stick
7. Put in the oven for fifteen minutes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Torta Real (Royal Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- 6 egg yolks
- half a pound of peeled and ground almonds (dry)
- half a pound of sifted sugar
- pieces of candied fruit: lemon, chilacayote, orange, sweet potato, figs

## Directions

1. Beat six egg whites to stiff peaks and the six yolks separately
2. When well beaten, combine them
3. Add half a pound of peeled and ground almonds (dry) and half a pound of sifted sugar
4. Mix everything well
5. Add pieces of candied fruit such as lemon, chilacayote, orange, sweet potato, and figs
6. Mix well, pour into a mold coated with butter
7. Place in a not-very-hot oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortas de Carne con Lorocos (Meat Cakes with Lorocos)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw meat
- fried seasoning
- breadcrumb powder
- lorocos
- salt
- eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop the raw meat, using half beef and half pork if desired
2. Add fried seasoning, one teaspoon of breadcrumb powder and the lorocos which should have been cooked with salt beforehand
3. Mix in some raw eggs according to the quantity of meat
4. Season everything with salt
5. Form the small cakes
6. Fry them in hot butter
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortas de Espárragos y Crema (Asparagus and Cream Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1/3 cup milk
- 4 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper powder
- 1 cup asparagus tips
- egg whites
- lard or butter

## Directions

1. Make a sauce with one tablespoon of butter and one-third of a cup of milk
2. Beat four egg yolks (keeping the whites separate); once the yolks are beaten, mix in the cold sauce with salt, a little pepper powder and one cup of asparagus tips
3. When everything is well mixed, fold in the beaten egg whites and pour into a pan that is already on the fire with hot lard or butter
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortas de Papa (Potato Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- eggs
- cheese
- shrimp (optional)
- salt
- butter
- tomato
- onion
- breadcrumb powder
- annatto
- black pepper

## Directions

1. After the potatoes are cooked, peel and grind them
2. For half a pound of potatoes, mix in three eggs, a piece of cheese, and if available one ounce of shrimp, peeled and blanched
3. Season with salt
4. Form the small cakes
5. Fry them in hot butter over low heat
6. Serve alone or with a sauce made from peeled and chopped tomato with onion
7. When fried, add a little water, salt, and a little breadcrumb powder with a small piece of annatto and some black pepper
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortilla de Cebolla (Onion Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender onion stalks
- eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the tender onion stalks into pieces
2. Blanch them lightly
3. Squeeze them very well so they have no water
4. Mix them in beaten egg
5. Fry them in hot butter like the previous recipe
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortilla de Harina (Flour Tortilla)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 4 ounces sugar
- 4 ounces lard
- 2 eggs
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour, four ounces of sugar, four ounces of lard and two eggs very well with a little water and a pinch of salt
2. Stretch with a roller, prick with a knife
3. Cook on a comal, on the stove plate or in the oven
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortilla de Huevo Rellena (Filled Egg Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- goose liver or any poultry liver

## Directions

1. Make a regular omelet and fill with goose liver or the liver of any poultry.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortilla de Pascua (Easter Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper powder
- 4 teaspoons hot water
- egg whites
- butter
- optional toppings: chopped ham, cooked chicken mixed with cream, grated cheese, chopped onions, pieces of stewed meat, peas with fish or cream, or tomato slices

## Directions

1. Beat four egg yolks with salt to taste, a little pepper powder and four teaspoons of hot water
2. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks and when well beaten, mix with the yolks
3. Grease a pan with butter; when very hot, pour in the mixture, put on low heat and when already golden on the bottom, put in the oven to brown on top
4. If desired, top with chopped ham, cooked chicken mixed with cream, grated cheese, chopped onions, pieces of stewed meat, peas with fish or cream, or tomato slices, all to taste
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortilla de Pescado (Fish Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup of milk
- 1 cup of flour
- salt
- parsley, chopped
- garlic
- shredded salt cod
- oil or lard

## Directions

1. Beat well 2 eggs with 1 cup of milk
2. Mix in 1 cup of flour
3. Add salt, plenty of chopped parsley, garlic, and shredded salt cod
4. Drop by spoonfuls into hot oil or lard so they cook evenly
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortilla para Almuerzo (Breakfast Pancake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- four tablespoons cornmeal or corn flour
- five tablespoons wheat flour
- one small glass milk
- sugar
- half teaspoon baking soda
- lard or butter

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs well.
2. Mix in four tablespoons of cornmeal or corn flour, five tablespoons of wheat flour, one glass of milk and sugar to taste.
3. Beat everything very well together.
4. Heat lard or butter in a pan.
5. When the mixture is beaten, add half a teaspoon of baking soda.
6. Fry by spoonfuls.
7. As they cook, place them on a butter-greased plate and continue with the rest.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortilla Rellena (Filled Tortilla)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat (loin or ribs)
- tomato
- miltomate
- garlic head
- onion head
- a little burned sugar
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces
2. Fry on low heat without browning
3. Add tomato, miltomate, a garlic head, and an onion head
4. Grind well with a little burned sugar
5. Cover with water and salt until cooked
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortillas de Acelgas (Swiss Chard Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Swiss chard
- salt
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the Swiss chard with salt
2. When done, remove, squeeze, and mix in beaten egg like the others
3. If desired, make small cakes
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortitas de Aceite (Oil Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- half a pound of sugar
- 1 small glass of white wine
- 1 small glass of very fine oil
- a little anise
- 1 small teaspoon of salt
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs with half a pound of sugar until thick
2. Add one small glass of white wine
3. Add one small glass of very fine oil
4. Add a little anise
5. Add one small teaspoon of salt
6. Add one tablespoon of butter
7. Add flour, ensuring the dough is not too stiff
8. Roll out thin with a rolling pin
9. Cut out figures as desired
10. Place on a tin and bake in the oven
11. When cooked, coat with half-thread stage syrup
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortitas de Carne (Meat Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked meat
- tomato
- onions
- breadcrumb powder
- eggs
- salt
- sherry (optional)

## Directions

1. After the meat is cooked, chop it very well
2. Add a fried mixture of tomato and onions, a little breadcrumb powder
3. For half a pound of meat, mix in three eggs
4. Season with salt
5. Add a little sherry if desired
6. Form the small cakes
7. Fry them in hot butter
8. Can also be made with raw ground meat, or half beef and half pork meat
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortitas de Carne (Meat Patties)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked, chopped meat
- three raw eggs per half pound of meat
- fried recado
- bread powder
- Castilian pepper
- a little sherry optional
- butter
- onion
- tomato
- garlic
- achiote coloring optional

## Directions

1. Chop the cooked meat and mix with three raw eggs, fried recado and bread powder with Castilian peppers; if desired add a little sherry
2. Form the patties and fry in hot butter
3. Once fried, prepare a fried recado with onion, tomato and garlic
4. Add water, bread powder or achiote coloring to make a thin sauce
5. Serve the patties with this sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tortitas de Yuca (Cassava Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- yuca
- salt
- eggs

## Directions

1. Peel and clean the yuca well
2. Cook it with salt
3. When done, grind it
4. Mix in eggs like the potato cakes
5. Season with salt
6. Form the small cakes
7. Fry them in butter to serve dry
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Trapillo Poblano (Poblano-Style Squash Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- calabaza or güicoy squash, not too tender or too ripe
- butter
- peeled tomato
- chopped onion
- garlic clove
- tender elote (corn)
- green chile pimiento, finely chopped
- cream from milk

## Directions

1. Chop the calabaza or güicoy very finely.
2. Make a fried mixture in butter with peeled tomato, chopped onion, and a garlic clove.
3. Add the chopped squash with a tender elote well scraped and a finely chopped green chile pimiento.
4. Add a little cream from milk and let it boil on very low heat and serve hot.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Tripas (Tripe)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe (panza)
- salt
- pepitoria (ground seeds)
- sesame
- clove
- miltomate
- cinnamon
- cumin
- Castilian peppers
- beef liver, well cooked
- cold tortillas
- french bread or white bread
- achiote
- manteca
- onion

## Directions

1. After cleaning the tripe very well, put it to cook
2. After half an hour of boiling, remove the water and replace with an equal amount of fresh, clean water
3. Put it back on the fire and add salt
4. When well cooked, cut into large or small pieces
5. Grind together a little pepitoria, sesame, a toasted clove (if desired) or one cooked with miltomate, a little cinnamon, cumin, Castilian peppers, and a piece of well-cooked beef liver
6. Thicken with cold tortillas and a piece of french bread or white bread and a small piece of achiote
7. Dissolve this mixture in the water in which the tripe was cooked
8. Put a pan with sufficient manteca and fry some chopped onion
9. When it takes on a golden color, add this mixture and the pieces of tripe, letting it boil until a thick sauce results
10. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Turrón (Nougat)
body:
## Ingredients

- nine egg whites
- two pounds of sugar
- one and a half glasses of water
- juice of half a lemon
- lemon zest
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Beat nine egg whites for half an hour
2. Meanwhile, put two pounds of sugar with one and a half glasses of water over the fire and let it reach a very high point
3. Pour this in a stream to the whites while the syrup is very hot, beating continuously
4. Add the juice of half a lemon and the lemon zest
5. When it is cold and very thick, pour onto a platter with cinnamon powder on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Turrón (Nougat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of sugar
- 1 cup of water
- 1 cup of white honey
- 4 egg whites
- lemon zest (optional)
- ground cinnamon (optional)

## Directions

1. Put sugar, water, and white honey together
2. Cook until it reaches the ribbon stage
3. Remove and let cool
4. While the honey cools, beat 4 egg whites for the same amount of time it takes to cool the honey
5. When the honey is cool, pour it onto the beaten egg whites while continuing to beat with a wooden spatula
6. Optional: add lemon zest and ground cinnamon on top
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Venado Chojineado (Shredded Venison)
body:
## Ingredients

- venado (venison)
- salt
- cooked tomato
- chile guaque
- cumin
- garlic clove
- oregano
- achiote (annatto)
- french bread, soaked

## Directions

1. Once the venison is well shredded, place it to cook with salt.
2. Grind some cooked tomato with a chile guaque, a little cumin, a garlic clove, a little oregano, a small piece of achiote, and a soaked french bread.
3. Dissolve this mixture and put it to fry.
4. Add the well-cooked venison to this preparation.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Venado Chojineado con Patitas (Shredded Venison with Little Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- venado (venison)
- salt
- little feet, chopped
- masa (corn dough), very fine
- roasted tomatoes
- Castilian peppers
- chile
- achiote (annatto)
- epazote branch
- lard

## Directions

1. Cook the shredded venison with salt and chopped little feet.
2. Grind a little very fine masa with roasted tomatoes, Castilian peppers, chile, and a small piece of achiote.
3. Dissolve this with the same cooking broth.
4. Fry an epazote branch in lard.
5. Place the venison and feet with their well-fried seasoning into this.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Venado en Adobo (Venison in Adobe Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison
- salt
- cooked tomato
- guaque chile
- cumin
- garlic clove
- oregano
- achiote
- dried chile

## Directions

1. Clean the venison well and cook with salt
2. Grind some cooked tomato with a guaque chile, a little cumin, one garlic clove, a little oregano, a small piece of achiote, and a dried chile
3. Dissolve and fry this mixture
4. Add the well-cooked venison to cook in this sauce
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Venado en Salsa de Harina (Venison in Flour Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison pieces
- lard
- salt
- two garlic cloves, crushed
- two tablespoons flour
- pepper powder
- chopped parsley
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the venison pieces and clean them well
2. Fry in lard with salt
3. Add two crushed garlic cloves and two tablespoons of flour according to the amount of meat
4. When the flour is well browned, add pepper powder and chopped parsley
5. Cover with water and let cook until tender, serving it juicy
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Vinagre Acético (Acetic Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- acetic acid (0.5 ounce)
- water (1 liter)

## Directions

1. Buy 0.5 ounce of acetic acid.
2. Add 1 liter of water.
3. Use carefully as it is very strong.
4. This is the purest vinegar and should be used.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Vinagre de Perote o Manzana (Apple Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples (12 to 15 pounds)
- water (20 liters)
- panela (sugar cakes) (3 pieces, added after 5 days)

## Directions

1. Crush 12 to 15 pounds of apples very well.
2. Place in a barrel or large covered jug with 20 liters of water.
3. After 5 days, add 3 pieces of panela and cover again.
4. After 10 days, stir gently with a clean paddle without touching the apples, as they will be ruined if touched.
5. After 30 days, strain, bottle, and it is ready to use.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Volovan à la Francesa (French-style Pastry Case)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- Water
- Salt to taste
- One pound of butter, melted beforehand
- One tablespoon of butter
- One garlic clove
- Small pieces of pork loin
- Tender pieces of chicken meat

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour with water and a little salt, so the dough is not very hard
2. Let rest half an hour
3. Roll out with a roller and spread melted butter on it
4. Roll out again and spread more butter
5. Repeat this operation five or six times
6. Let rest ten minutes
7. Roll out the sheets with the roller to the size of the mold in which they will be made
8. Cover the mold with the dough very evenly
9. Place in the oven to bake
10. When cooked, remove very carefully so it does not break, whether large mold or small boxes
11. Make lids the size of the mold and bake the same way
12. Prepare a sauce by putting a saucepan with one tablespoon of butter, one garlic clove, and very small pieces of pork loin and tender chicken meat on heat
13. When it has taken on golden color, continue with the filling preparation
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Volovanes (Savory Pastry Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of flour
- Water
- Butter equal in weight to the dough
- One egg white
- Cooked brain
- Butter for frying
- Pepper powder
- Parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of flour with a little very cold water until the dough is soft
2. Let rest one hour
3. Weigh the dough; add an equal amount of butter
4. Roll out with a roller and spread butter on it
5. Close the dough very well, roll out two or three times and rest ten minutes
6. Roll out again and cut layers with the rim of a jar
7. Place first layers on a baking sheet, brush with egg white
8. Cover with another layer that has been cut in the middle with a smaller mold
9. Place in a very hot oven
10. Prepare filling with cooked brain fried in butter with pepper powder and chopped parsley
11. Fill the shells and cover with the baked lids
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Watercress Omelette (Tortilla de Berros)
body:
## Ingredients

- Watercress
- Eggs
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the watercress by removing everything that is stem.
2. Beat the egg (white first and then yolk).
3. Mix in the raw watercress leaves, seasoned with salt.
4. Fry in hot butter.
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Zanahorias con Queso (Carrots with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- salt
- one tablespoon butter
- cheese powder
- two roasted tomatoes
- parsley
- half cup milk

## Directions

1. Cook the carrots with salt, peel them, and cut into pieces or slices
2. Place one tablespoon of butter in a pan
3. Layer carrots and cheese powder alternately, ensuring the last layer is cheese
4. Cover with two roasted tomatoes and chopped parsley
5. Pour half a cup of milk over everything
6. Let it simmer slowly over low heat
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Zanahorias en Mantequilla (Carrots in Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- salt
- flour
- one tablespoon of butter
- onion slices
- hot water

## Directions

1. Cook the carrots in salted water and let them drain
2. Cut and coat in flour, heat one tablespoon of butter in a pan, add some onion slices
3. When they are golden (not burned) add the carrots
4. When fried, add one glass of hot water, salt to taste and let boil for five minutes
5. Serve hot
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país

title: Zanahorias Rellenas (Stuffed Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- thick carrots
- salt
- tomato
- onion
- parsley
- oil
- sardines
- egg
- hot lard
- white bread
- achiote
- vinegar
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. Cook thick carrots and once cooked, peel them and scoop out enough to fill them
2. Add salt while cooking
3. Prepare a fried mixture of tomato, onion, and chopped parsley fried in a little oil
4. Add sardines to this mixture and use it to stuff the carrots, covering with the scooped out part
5. If desired, wrap in beaten egg and fry in hot lard
6. Serve with a sauce made from chopped tomato and onion, well fried, with a teaspoon of white bread and a little achiote, leaving a thin sauce
7. Alternatively, place the carrots in a pan with one tablespoon of vinegar, a little salt, and a bay leaf, cover and heat in the oven to keep warm
8. Serve with chopped parsley
source_title: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina, pastelería y repostería al estilo del país
